Aligning Secure and Safe Large Language  Models via Meticulous Reasoning","authors":["Zhengyue Zhao","Yingzi Ma","Somesh Jha","Marco Pavone","Patrick McDaniel","Chaowei Xiao"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$1e5","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009011"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009011","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：现有安全对齐方法即使加了推理式防护，仍然会被强分布外 jailbreak 绕过，因为它们没有稳定识别出提示词里真正的恶意意图。过去的 SFT、RLHF 和不少 system-2 安全方法，更多是在表面风险模式上做拒答或审查；一旦攻击把恶意目标伪装进看似无害的上下文里，模型就容易失守。","importance":"这项工作的意义在于把安全对齐的重点，从“看到危险表述就拦截”转向“先抽取核心恶意意图，再基于意图做推理和拒绝”。这对 post-train 很直接，但对 pretrain 也有启发：安全失败并不只是偏好学习不够，而是表征层面是否形成了稳健的意图抽象。若这个判断成立，那么未来安全数据构造、合成攻击、甚至中期训练中的 reasoning 信号设计都要围绕“意图可分离性”来做。","technical_approach":"ARMOR 的核心是 meticulous reasoning，即让模型在回答前显式抽取并审查用户请求中的核心意图，再据此决定是否响应，而不是只对原始 prompt 做表层安全分类。作者的机制假设是：不同 jailbreak 形式虽然包装各异，但最终都要把同一个 malicious intent 植入上下文，因此防御关键不是穷举攻击模板，而是提高对意图的恢复能力。相较已有 inference-time reasoning 方法，ARMOR 关注的是对隐藏恶意目标的逆向提取；从摘要看，它应包含一个先解析意图、再做安全决策的链式流程，可能配合专门构造的训练或蒸馏信号，但摘要没有给出更细算法细节。","experiments":"摘要中的关键实验结论是：现有 system-2 安全方法在 AutoDAN-Turbo、Adversarial Reasoning 这类强 OOD jailbreak 上仍会失败，而 ARMOR 在这类攻击下更稳健。可惜摘要没有给出具体模型、评测基准、攻击成功率下降幅度或代价指标，因此目前只能明确比较对象是“传统后训练对齐”和“带推理的安全防护”，而论文的卖点在强 jailbreak 的泛化鲁棒性。若看实验是否扎实，最值得关注的是：一，安全性提升是否以过度拒绝 harmless prompts 为代价；二，推理链是否会被攻击者反向利用；三，不同 base model 上是否一致。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a specific failure of aligned LLMs: even reasoning-based safety defenses still break under strong out-of-distribution jailbreaks because they fail to recover the hidden malicious intent. Existing SFT, RLHF, and many system-2 defenses mostly react to surface-form risk cues, so attacks that camouflage the harmful goal inside benign-looking context remain effective.","importance":"The main contribution is a shift in alignment emphasis from pattern-based blocking to intent-centric reasoning. That matters beyond safety evaluation: it suggests that robust alignment depends on whether the model can form stable abstractions of user intent, not just on preference tuning over observed harmful prompts. For pretrain and mid-train readers, this points to a broader question about how intent separability emerges in representations.","technical_approach":"ARMOR uses meticulous reasoning to explicitly extract and inspect the core intent before producing an answer, instead of performing a shallow safety judgment on the raw prompt. The key design hypothesis is that jailbreaks may differ in wrapper but must preserve a recoverable malicious objective. So the defense target is not exhaustive jailbreak coverage, but stronger intent recovery under adversarial obfuscation. Relative to prior inference-time safety reasoning, ARMOR appears to add an intermediate intent reconstruction step and then conditions refusal or compliance on that extracted intent.","experiments":"The abstract makes one clear empirical claim: prior system-2 defenses still fail against attacks such as AutoDAN-Turbo and Adversarial Reasoning, while ARMOR is more robust on those hard OOD jailbreaks. But the abstract gives no concrete model sizes, datasets, attack success rates, or utility-retention numbers. So the evidence is promising in direction, but the effect size and the safety-helpfulness trade-off cannot be judged from the abstract alone.","authors_note":""},"tags":["alignment","safety","reasoning","jailbreak"],"notable_authors":["Somesh Jha","Marco Pavone","Patrick McDaniel"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009020","title":"NeuCLIP: Efficient Large-Scale CLIP Training with Neural Normalizer Optimization","authors":["Xiyuan Wei","Chih-Jen Lin","Tianbao Yang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$1e6","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009020"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009020","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文解决的是大规模 CLIP 训练里对比损失归一化项估计不准的问题，尤其是在小 batch、大数据场景下，传统近似会带来明显优化误差。现有做法要么依赖超大 batch 近似 partition function，算力成本高；要么用逐样本 normalizer 估计器做块坐标更新，但这种做法和编码器更新不同步，误差会随数据量/批大小比值变差。","importance":"这项工作的贡献更偏方法级和系统级之间的交叉：它不改 CLIP 目标本身，而是重新组织这个目标的优化方式，从而让大规模对比学习不再过度依赖巨 batch。对预训练的启示很直接——很多所谓“需要大 batch”的现象，部分是优化器或估计器设计造成的，而不全是目标函数本身的硬约束；如果 normalizer 能更稳定地估计，CLIP 类训练的硬件门槛可以下降。","technical_approach":"NeuCLIP 的核心是把对比学习中的 normalizer 估计问题改写成一个可学习的神经归一化器优化问题，而不是继续用每个样本一个显式估计值的陈旧更新方式。摘要提到两点关键设计：第一，对 contrastive loss 做重参数化或重写，把归一化项的优化变成更适合函数逼近的问题；第二，用 neural normalizer 来追踪随编码器变化而变化的 partition function。这个设计比 blockwise per-sample estimator 更像“共享参数的函数估计器”，优点是误差不再线性受数据规模拖累，而且更能适应 encoder 持续更新。代价通常会是引入额外模型和训练耦合，但摘要强调其框架是 elegant，说明实现复杂度可能可控。","experiments":"摘要明确声称 NeuCLIP 在大规模 CLIP 训练中比现有 normalizer 估计方案更有效，尤其适合大数据集或小 batch 场景；但没有给出具体 backbone、样本规模、batch 大小、吞吐、zero-shot 指标或提升数字。可以确定的 baseline 至少包括“大 batch 近似”和“逐样本 normalizer estimator”。最值得看的实验应该有三类：一，固定算力下与大 batch CLIP 的性能比较；二，数据规模/batch 规模变化下误差是否按理论预期放大或收敛；三，神经 normalizer 的额外开销是否值得。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a central optimization bottleneck in large-scale CLIP training: inaccurate estimation of the contrastive normalizer, especially when batches are small and datasets are large. Standard CLIP training either relies on very large batches to approximate the partition function, which is expensive, or uses per-sample normalizer estimators updated in a stale blockwise manner, which introduces optimization error that worsens with dataset-size-to-batch-size ratio.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution with clear systems implications. It does not change the CLIP objective; it changes how the hard part of that objective is optimized. For pretraining practice, the useful message is that some of the perceived need for massive contrastive batches comes from the estimator and optimization scheme, not only from the loss itself.","technical_approach":"NeuCLIP reformulates the normalizer estimation problem and replaces explicit per-sample estimators with a neural normalizer optimized as a shared function. That is the main design difference from prior block-coordinate approaches. Instead of maintaining one stale estimate per example, the method learns a parametric estimator that can track the partition term as encoders evolve. This should reduce the error amplification seen in dataset-scale regimes and make training less sensitive to batch size. The likely trade-off is extra model coupling and some additional optimization complexity, but the intended gain is much better scalability.","experiments":"The abstract claims stronger large-scale CLIP training performance than prior normalizer-estimation methods, with particular benefit on large datasets or under small-batch constraints. However, it does not report model sizes, data scale, throughput, zero-shot metrics, or exact gains. The key baselines are clearly large-batch CLIP and prior per-sample normalizer estimators, but the abstract alone is not enough to quantify the practical advantage.","authors_note":""},"tags":["clip","contrastive-learning","optimization","training-efficiency"],"notable_authors":["Chih-Jen Lin","Tianbao 个文化领域的 reward modeling benchmark。它的重要性不只是补一个新数据集，而是把 reward model 这个 alignment 中间件拉到更细粒度的审视下。对 pretrain/post-train 接口的启示是，RM 的偏差会直接放大到偏好优化和拒答行为里，因此文化意识不是单靠 SFT 文本多语覆盖就能解决的，需要专门建模和评估。","technical_approach":"技术上核心是两部分：先构建文化感知 RM 评测基准，再分析现有 RM 的失效模式，并据此改进 reward modeling。摘要提到他们发现 CARB 表现与下游多语言文化对齐任务正相关，这说明 benchmark 不是孤立数据集，而是有一定外部效度。更关键的是，作者指出 culture-aware RM 中存在 spurious correlations，也就是模型可能抓住表面语言或区域线索，而不是真正理解文化规范。相比单纯报分数，这种错误来源分析更有价值，因为它直接决定后续 RM 训练该如何重采样或去偏。","experiments":"摘要给出了几个关键事实：一，作者构建了覆盖 10 种文化、4 个领域的 CARB；二，对多种 SOTA reward model 的评测显示它们文化感知不足；三，CARB 分数与下游 multilingual cultural alignment 呈正相关。摘要没有给出数据规模、文化领域定义、被测 RM 名单、相关系数大小或改进方法带来的数值提升，因此目前能确认的是评测结论和相关性结论。最关键的实验应包括：跨文化迁移是否对称、语言与文化因素是否可分离、以及 debias 策略能否在不损害通用对齐的情况下提升 CARB。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a missing piece in LLM alignment: we do not have a strong benchmark for evaluating the cultural awareness of reward models. Existing RM evaluations focus mostly on general helpfulness, harmlessness, or preference quality, with limited culturally grounded coverage, so a reward model can score well overall while still encoding systematic cross-cultural bias.","importance":"This is primarily a benchmark and analysis contribution, but it matters because reward models are the control surface of many alignment pipelines. If a reward model is culturally shallow or biased, downstream preference optimization will inherit that failure. For the pretrain-to-posttrain interface, the paper is a reminder that multilingual data alone does not guarantee culturally aware alignment.","technical_approach":"The technical core is the CARB benchmark, which covers 10 cultures across 4 cultural domains, plus an analysis pipeline for diagnosing culture-aware reward modeling failures. The paper also identifies spurious correlations in culture-aware reward models, meaning models may rely on superficial linguistic or regional cues instead of modeling cultural norms more faithfully.","experiments":"The abstract provides three concrete empirical claims: CARB spans 10 cultures and 4 domains, state-of-the-art reward models show clear deficiencies on it, and CARB scores correlate positively with downstream multilingual cultural alignment performance. magnitude of current RM failure.","authors_note":""},"tags":["reward-model","alignment","evaluation","culture"],"notable_authors":["Min Zhang"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009040","title":"Understanding In-Context Learning on Structured Manifolds: Bridging Attention to Kernel Methods","authors":["Zhaiming Shen","Alexander Hsu","Rongjie Lai","Wenjing Liao"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$1e8","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009040"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009040","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是结构化流形数据上的 in-context learning 理论解释缺失，尤其是 attention 到底在这类回归问题里学到了什么。ICL 理论过去多围绕线性回归、离散 token 或简单生成模型展开，对带几何结构的数据和 Hölder 函数回归几乎没有可用解释，因此 transformer 在这类场景中的泛化来源并不清楚。","importance":"这项工作的贡献是理论机制层面的：它把 attention 与经典 kernel method 连接起来，给 ICL 在结构化几何数据上的表现提供了可分析框架。对做预训练的人来说，这类结果不会立刻改训练 recipe，但它能帮助判断某些 attention 变体什么时候更像核回归器、prompt 长度为什么有用、以及 ICL 的收益在哪些数据几何条件下才会出现。","technical_approach":"作者的核心方法不是提出新模型，而是建立一个理论桥梁：对于流形上的 Hölder 回归，transformer 通过 query 和 prompt 的交互，等效地执行一种 kernel-based prediction。这个结论的重要点在于，它把 learned attention score 解释成与高斯核近似对应的相似度权重。基于这个视角，作者进一步推导泛化误差界，把 prompt length、流形几何和函数平滑性都纳入分析。与泛泛而谈“attention 像 nearest neighbor”不同，这里给的是更细的核方法对应关系和误差界条件。","experiments":"摘要里的实验主要用于验证理论映射：作者观察到在 Hölder 函数任务上，学习到的 query-prompt 分数与 Gaussian kernel 高度相关。除此之外，论文还给出了依赖 prompt length 等因素的泛化误差界。摘要没有给出实验数据规模、模型配置、相关系数数值或与其他理论预测的对比，因此实验部分更像理论 sanity check，而不是性能 SOTA。最值得关注的是：相关性是否跨架构稳定、在偏离理论假设的数据上是否仍成立，以及误差界与实际 scaling 是否匹配。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a gap in ICL theory: we have little understanding of what attention is doing on structured geometric data, especially for regression on manifolds. Existing analyses are mostly built around simpler linear or tokenized settings, leaving transformer generalization on manifold-valued structure largely unexplained.","importance":"This is a theory contribution with useful interpretive value. It connects attention-based in-context prediction to classical kernel methods, which gives a more concrete lens on when ICL should work, why prompt length matters, and how data geometry enters the picture. It is not a recipe paper, but it can inform how practitioners think about attention behavior beyond standard text settings.","technical_approach":"The main contribution is a theoretical bridge: for regression of Hölder functions on manifolds, a transformer can be interpreted as performing kernel-based prediction through query-prompt interactions. The notable part is that the learned attention scores are shown, and numerically validated, to align closely with a Gaussian-kernel-like similarity structure. On top of that, the paper derives generalization error bounds that depend on prompt length, manifold geometry, and function regularity. This goes beyond loose analogies between attention and nearest neighbors by giving a more formal kernel connection.","experiments":"The empirical component appears to be mainly theory validation. The abstract states that learned query-prompt scores are highly correlated with the Gaussian kernel for Hölder-function tasks, supporting the proposed interpretation. No model details, dataset sizes, or quantitative correlation values are given, so the experiments should be read as evidence for the theoretical mechanism rather than as a benchmark-driven result.","authors_note":""},"tags":["icl","attention","kernel-methods","theory"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009043","title":"Watch the Weights: Unsupervised monitoring and control of fine-tuned LLMs","authors":["Ziqian Zhong","Aditi Raghunathan"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$1e9","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009043"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009043","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：当开源微调模型没有完整训练数据可用时，如何在无监督、分布外条件下监控和控制其潜在风险，例如 backdoor。现有很多可解释性和安全分析方法依赖 activation，并默认你手上有和训练分布相近的数据；但真正危险的威胁恰恰常常是 OOD 的，因此这条路线在实战里很受限。","importance":"这项工作的价值在于它把分析对象从 activation 转向 weight difference，提出了一种不依赖相似数据分布的监控思路。对 pretrain/post-train 接口很有启发：微调并不只是行为层面变化，很多能力偏移和潜在后门会在参数更新的低秩结构里留下痕迹。如果这种痕迹稳定存在，那么权重级解释可以成为继续预训练、SFT、LoRA 合并乃至安全审计的通用工具。","technical_approach":"方法核心是分析 fine-tuned model 与 base model 的权重差分矩阵，并利用其顶部奇异向量来恢复微调引入的主导行为方向。摘要中的关键发现是，这些 top singular vectors 对应可解释的行为变化，且可用于监控与控制模型。相比 activation-based 方法，这种做法不需要准备探测数据来激活风险模式，而是直接从参数变化中读出主成分。设计上的强点是无监督、OOD 友好；边界则在于它更适合分析相对低秩、集中式的微调变化，对分散在大量层和参数中的复杂行为改写可能不够敏感。","experiments":"摘要明确给出的实验结论是：微调前后权重差的 top singular vectors 与可解释行为变化对齐，并可用于理解、监测和控制 fine-tuned LLM。摘要没有提供模型种类、微调任务、backdoor 类型、检测准确率、控制成功率或与 activation baseline 的比较数字，因此目前只能确认这是一个以机制演示为主的工作。最关键的实验应该包括：标准 SFT、恶意 backdoor、对齐微调等不同更新类型下，奇异方向是否稳定；以及基于这些方向的干预是否会伤害正常能力。","authors_note":"作者来自开源模型安全/可解释性方向常见社区。Aditi Raghunathan 在鲁棒性与可解释性方面有较高知名度。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a practical security and interpretability problem: how to monitor and control fine-tuned LLMs when the original training data is unavailable and potential threats are out of distribution. Many activation-based methods assume access to distributionally similar data, which is exactly what fails in the presence of unknown backdoors or novel malicious fine-tunes.","importance":"The main contribution is a weight-space view of model monitoring. That is important because it suggests that fine-tuning leaves structured signatures in parameter updates that can be analyzed without relying on matched probe data. For pretrain, SFT, and model-merging workflows, this opens a useful direction for auditing behavior shifts directly from checkpoints.","technical_approach":"The method studies the weight difference between a fine-tuned model and its base model, and uses the top singular vectors of that difference to identify dominant behavior-changing directions. The key claim is that these singular directions are interpretable and actionable. Compared with activation-based interpretability, this avoids dependence on curated in-distribution inputs and instead extracts signal from the update itself. The approach is especially attractive when fine-tuning changes are low-rank or concentrated. Its limitation is that highly distributed behavioral changes may be harder to recover cleanly this way.","experiments":"The abstract claims that top singular vectors of the weight difference align with interpretable fine-tuning behaviors and can be used for understanding, monitoring, and control. It does not report model families, task setups, backdoor detection metrics, or intervention success rates. So the evidence sounds mechanism-driven rather than benchmark-heavy from the abstract alone. The most important tests would be whether the method works across benign SFT, adversarial fine-tunes, and alignment updates, and how much capability is lost when intervening along those directions.","authors_note":"Aditi Raghunathan is well known for work on robustness and interpretability. Prompting is explicit and cheap but limited in expressive power, while fine-tuning is powerful but more expensive and less interpretable. Prior work rarely optimizes both channels together.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution with relevance to the pretrain-posttrain interface. If prompt and parameter adaptation can be co-optimized effectively, then task information does not need to be forced entirely into weights. Some of it may be better represented in explicit language, while the rest is handled by parameter updates. That division of labor is an interesting practical and conceptual result.","technical_approach":"MetaTuner uses two neural generators, one for prompts and one for parameter updates, with a shared bottom encoder that lets both branches learn from a common task representation. This is more than simply stacking prompt tuning and LoRA: the two adaptation channels are trained jointly under the same supervised signal. The intended benefit is coordinated specialization, where each branch learns what kind of task information it should carry. The main trade-off is added training complexity and the risk that one branch dominates while the other becomes redundant.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide tasks, model scales, baselines, or quantitative results. LLA 的实际优势。摘要没有提供模型规模、序列长度、速度/显存指标或精度提升数字，因此目前无法判断这是偏理论验证还是已有较强实证。最关键的实验应该是：在相同显存预算下，LLA 相对 softmax 和 linear attention 的精度-效率曲线，以及局部性超参数如何影响偏差与方差。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a gap in attention design: most efficient-attention work focuses on reducing cost, while comparatively less work asks whether we can design a more statistically appropriate attention rule even if it is somewhat more expensive. LN also adds nonlinear coupling that complicates mechanistic interpretability.","importance":"This delivers a strong empirical takeaway about transformer inference structure: across GPT-2 variants, all LayerNorms can be removed with minimal loss after light finetuning, which can simplify deployment and make mechanistic analysis cleaner by reducing nonlinear entanglement.","technical_approach":"The approach is straightforward: delete all LN layers from a pretrained GPT-2, then finetune so surrounding parameters absorb LN’s functional role (effectively folding normalization behavior into nearby weights/biases). The key distinction is separating LN’s training-stability role from its inference-time representational necessity; the results support replaceability at inference, without claiming LN is unnecessary during training.","experiments":"They report LN removal for every GPT-2 model, with only a small validation loss increase (e.g., +0.03 cross-entropy for GPT-2 XL). Prior work often applies them separately because they prefer conflicting weight distributions: quantization likes compact ranges, while pruning benefits from high variance and clear saliency. A naive pipeline usually lets one method undermine the other.","importance":"The contribution is mainly methodological with clear systems relevance. It does not invent a new compression primitive; it provides a practical way to jointly use two mature ones in a post-training, data-light setting. That matters for deployment, where retraining is often unavailable. For pretraining researchers, the useful lesson is that compression is increasingly a coupled optimization problem across distribution shaping, sensitivity, and hardware constraints, not a single-technique game.","technical_approach":"The proposed method, OBR (Optimal Brain Restoration), is a training-free framework for joint pruning and quantization. The key idea is to compensate pruning error and quantization error against each other instead of minimizing them in isolation. It is in the spirit of Optimal Brain style sensitivity-aware compression, but the emphasis here is joint restoration after both constraints are imposed. Operationally, the method makes sparsity and quantization decisions, then uses the remaining degrees of freedom to restore the model’s function by correcting the induced output error. This is more principled than a sequential pipeline, where the first compression step often creates a poor landscape for the second.","experiments":"From the abstract alone, the reliable takeaway is that the method aims to reduce accuracy loss under joint post-training compression without additional training. The provided text does not include concrete model sizes, bit-widths, sparsity levels, baselines, or gains, so a quantitative summary would be speculative. The critical experiments to inspect in the full paper are likely: whether joint compression beats either quantization-only or pruning-only under equal budgets, and whether OBR beats sequential post-training baselines across the bit-width × sparsity grid. The most informative ablation would be whether error compensation still helps in the aggressive compression regime, where these methods usually break first.","authors_note":""},"tags":["quantization","pruning","llm-compression","joint-compression"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009226","title":"Jailbreak Transferability Emerges from Shared Representations","authors":["Rico Angell","Jannik Brinkmann","He He"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$1f2","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009226"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009226","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的核心结论是：jailbreak 的可迁移性主要来自模型之间共享的表示，而不是某个安全训练漏洞或某个模型家族的偶然缺陷。过去大家知道攻击能跨模型迁移，但原因解释很分裂，有人归因于对齐不足，有人归因于提示模板相似，缺少能跨多模型统一解释的证据。","importance":"这项工作对安全研究很重要，但它对 pretrain 也有直接启示，因为共享表征本身就是预训练产物。它告诉我们：模型越在良性输入上学到相近的内部表示，就越可能共享同一类攻击脆弱面。这个发现的量级接近机制级，不是提出一个更强 jailbreak，而是给出了 transferability 的可操作预测因子，并把安全脆弱性和表征几何联系起来。","technical_approach":"作者在 20 个开源模型和 33 种 jailbreak 攻击上做系统分析，识别出两个稳定影响迁移的因素：良性 prompt 下的表征相似度，以及源模型上攻击成功的强度。关键不只是做相关性统计，他们还做了因果方向更强的验证：通过仅用 benign 数据蒸馏，刻意提高两个模型的表征相似度，再观察 jailbreak transfer 是否随之上升。如果这个实验成立，说明迁移不是安全对齐细节的副作用，而更像是共享语义处理电路带来的副产品。和过去只比较 attack success rate 的工作相比，这篇文章往前走了一步，开始解释“为什么会转移”。","experiments":"摘要里有明确数字：覆盖 20 个 open-weight 模型、33 种 jailbreak 攻击。核心实验结论是，跨模型迁移可由 benign-prompt 下的表征相似度和源攻击强度系统性解释；进一步地，仅通过 benign-only distillation 增加相似度，就会提高迁移性。摘要没有给出相关系数、转移提升幅度或具体模型名单，因此无法补充定量细节。最值得看的 ablation 是控制模型家族、参数量和 safety tuning 强度后，这两个因素是否仍稳健；如果仍成立，说明 shared representations 的解释具有跨架构普适性，而不是简单的同门模型效应。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The central claim is that jailbreak transferability mostly arises from shared representations across models, not from accidental quirks of one safety pipeline or one model family. Transfer has been widely observed, but the field has lacked a unifying explanation for why attacks move across models so reliably.","importance":"This is important for safety, but it also matters for pretraining because shared representations are a direct product of pretraining. The paper suggests a concrete trade-off: models that process benign inputs more similarly may also inherit similar attack surfaces. That makes the contribution closer to a mechanistic explanation than a new attack recipe. It gives transferability predictors that are actionable and representation-level.","technical_approach":"The authors run a broad analysis over 20 open-weight models and 33 jailbreak attacks, and identify two stable factors: representational similarity under benign prompts, and the attack strength on the source model. The stronger part is that they go beyond correlation. By deliberately increasing similarity through benign-only distillation, they test whether transferability rises accordingly. If that intervention holds up, it supports a causal account: transfer is tied to shared internal processing rather than only to alignment-specific flaws. Compared with prior work that mostly reports attack success rates, this paper tries to explain the mechanism behind transfer.","experiments":"The abstract gives two concrete coverage numbers: 20 open-weight models and 33 jailbreak attacks. The main empirical findings are that transfer can be systematically predicted by benign representational similarity and source attack strength, and that increasing similarity via benign-only distillation increases transfer. The abstract does not report effect sizes, correlations, or model-specific results, so a finer quantitative summary would be speculative. The most important ablations to inspect are whether the finding survives controls for model family, parameter scale, and safety-tuning strength. If it does, the representation-sharing account is much stronger than a same-family explanation.","authors_note":""},"tags":["jailbreak","representation","safety","transferability"],"notable_authors":["He He"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009239","title":"CAD-Tokenizer: Towards Text-Based CAD Prototyping via Modality-Specific Tokenization","authors":["Ruiyu Wang","Shizhao Sun","Weijian Ma","Jiang Bian"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$1f3","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009239"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009239","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：如果希望用语言模型统一做 Text-to-CAD 生成和 CAD 编辑，现有通用 tokenizer 对 CAD 序列的切分方式是不合适的。CAD 不是自然语言，也不是原始坐标点云，而是由 sketch、extrusion 等构造原语组成的程序式序列；把它按词片切碎会破坏 primitive-level 语义，也让注意力模块难以捕捉几何结构和编辑依赖。","importance":"这项工作的意义主要在 tokenizer / 表示层面，属于典型的多模态预训练基础问题。对 pretrain 研究来说，这类工作很重要，因为统一建模往往先卡在 tokenization：如果输入单元错了，后面的模型容量和训练目标都很难补回来。CAD 是一个很好的压力测试场景，它比图文更结构化，也更接近程序生成；若模态特定 tokenizer 有效，说明统一 LM 进入工业设计类序列时不能简单复用 NLP 分词器。","technical_approach":"作者提出 CAD-Tokenizer，核心是按 CAD 模态自身的构造语义做 tokenization，而不是沿用自然语言 tokenizer。虽然摘要没展开细节，但从描述看，它应当采用多模态或层次化切分，使 token 与 CAD primitive、操作参数或局部几何语义对齐。设计上的关键取舍是：不追求完全统一的单一 token 粒度，而是让 token 边界匹配 CAD 的编辑和生成原语。这样做的原因很直接——CAD 序列的有效依赖是‘操作到操作、约束到约束’，不是 wordpiece 邻接关系。和以往把 CAD 当文本串喂给 LLM 的方法相比，这篇文章把性能瓶颈前移到表示层处理。","experiments":"摘要没有给出定量结果，但能确认论文面向的是 text-guided CAD prototyping，覆盖 CAD 生成和编辑两个场景，并主张 modality-specific tokenization 能更好建模 CAD 结构。缺少模型规模、数据集、任务定义和指标，因此不能编写可靠数字。最值得看的实验应包括：与 BPE/WordPiece 等通用 tokenizer 的直接对比；在生成初始化和后续编辑两类任务上是否都提升；以及 token 粒度变化对上下文长度、训练稳定性和几何有效率的影响。如果仅提升编辑但不提升从零生成，说明 tokenizer 主要帮了局部结构对齐；如果两者都提升，价值会更高。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a representation bottleneck in text-guided CAD modeling: standard LLM tokenizers are a poor fit for CAD sequences. CAD is not natural language and not raw geometry either; it is a procedural sequence of construction primitives such as sketches and extrusions. Breaking these sequences into word pieces destroys primitive-level semantics and makes it harder for attention to track geometric and editing structure.","importance":"This is primarily a tokenizer and representation contribution, which makes it directly relevant to multimodal pretraining. In unified modeling, tokenization is often the first hard constraint: if the basic units are wrong, model scale and objective tweaks rarely recover the lost structure. CAD is an especially useful testbed because it is more structured than image-text and closer to program-like generation. A positive result here would argue against blindly reusing NLP tokenizers for industrial design modalities.","technical_approach":"The proposed CAD-Tokenizer is modality-specific: it tokenizes according to CAD construction semantics rather than standard text segmentation. The abstract does not spell out the exact format, but the intended design is clear: tokens should align with CAD primitives, operation parameters, or other geometry-aware units. The main design choice is to prioritize semantic boundaries that matter for generation and editing, instead of enforcing a single text-style granularity. That should help because the useful dependencies in CAD are between operations and constraints, not between arbitrary subword fragments.","experiments":"The abstract does not report quantitative results, but it makes clear that the target setting is text-guided CAD prototyping, covering both generation and editing. Earlier work on short binding lists suggested that models retrieve entities mainly by position, but that account degrades once many bindings are present, especially for middle positions.","importance":"This is a mechanistic contribution with direct relevance to in-context learning and reasoning. Its main value is replacing a single-mechanism story with a mixed-mechanism one: when positional retrieval becomes noisy, models supplement it with a lexical retrieval mechanism. For pretraining, the implication is that LMs do not implement one fixed retrieval algorithm; they combine cues depending on context complexity and ambiguity. That helps explain why entity-binding behavior often degrades in long contexts.","technical_approach":"The authors study retrieval under more demanding binding setups and show that positional retrieval becomes unreliable as the number of bound entities grows, especially in middle positions. To compensate, models use a lexical mechanism that retrieves the target via content associated with its bound partner rather than position alone. This is the core idea behind the title’s 'mixing mechanisms'. The paper’s key shift from earlier work is that retrieval is conditional: positional cues are cheap but noisy, while lexical cues are more specific but depend on content discrimination, so models mix them as needed.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide model sizes, task scales, or quantitative metrics, but it gives a clear empirical claim: positional retrieval degrades with more bindings, and lexical retrieval emerges as a compensatory mechanism. The most important experiments to inspect are whether this pattern holds across model scales and architectures, whether causal tools such as activation patching can separately localize positional and lexical pathways, and how the balance between the two mechanisms changes with context length and binding density. Strong causal evidence would make this much more than a probing in LLM Problem Solving: The Case of the Shortest Path","authors":["Yao Tong","Jiayuan Ye","Anastasia Borovykh","Reza Shokri"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$1fc","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009467"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009467","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要回答的是：LLM 在问题求解里的泛化到底卡在哪，尤其是“会不会做更长的组合式推理”。作者选 shortest path 这个可控环境，是因为真实 benchmark 里训练数据、任务表述和推理策略缠在一起，失败很难归因；而最短路同时有清晰算法结构、可控长度和可控地图分布，适合分离不同泛化维度。","importance":"这项工作的主要贡献是把“系统泛化”拆成了两个正交方向，并发现二者表现完全不同：模型对 unseen maps 的空间迁移可以很强，但对更长路径长度的 scaling 会稳定失效。这个结论对预训练和推理研究都很有启发，因为它说明很多看似会做规划的模型，学到的可能是局部转移规律或短程启发式，而不是可递归扩展的算法。对长链推理、程序合成、搜索式任务来说，这比单纯报告 benchmark 分数更有解释力。","technical_approach":"核心方法是构建一个 synthetic shortest-path problem-solving environment，并显式区分两类泛化：spatial transfer 到未见地图，以及 length scaling 到更长规划地平线。作者不仅测模型最终对错，还分析训练范式和推理时策略如何影响泛化表现。关键洞见是“recursive instability”：模型在局部步骤上也许看起来合理，但误差会随着推理链递归放大，最终导致长度扩展失败。和常见做法相比，这里不是追求更复杂任务，而是故意把环境做得足够干净，从而能把泛化失败定位到学习动态、监督形式或 inference policy，而不是数据集噪声。","experiments":"摘要给出的核心实验事实是：模型在 spatial transfer 上表现强，但在 length scaling 上持续失败，且作者把失败归因为 recursive instability。论文还进一步分析了 learning pipeline 不同阶段对这种系统泛化的影响，但摘要未提供模型规模、训练样本量、是否比较 CoT / search / tool use、以及性能随路径长度增长的具体衰减曲线。即便没有数字，这个设置本身已经很有价值，因为它允许读者看清楚：当问题长度超出训练支持时，错误是突然崩掉，还是逐步累计；这种误差形态比平均准确率更能指导后续训练设计。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a clean version of a broad question: where does LLM problem-solving generalization actually fail, especially when the required reasoning chain gets longer? The authors use shortest-path planning because real benchmarks confound training data, task format, and inference strategy, making failure analysis hard. Shortest path offers algorithmic structure with controllable horizon and distribution shift.","importance":"The main contribution is the separation of two orthogonal generalization axes and the finding that they behave very differently. Models can generalize well to unseen maps, yet consistently fail when asked to scale to longer planning horizons. That is important for pretraining and reasoning research because it suggests many models learn local transition heuristics rather than an algorithm that composes recursively. For long-chain reasoning, planning, and program-like tasks, this is more informative than a single benchmark score.","technical_approach":"The method is a controlled synthetic environment for shortest-path problem solving, with explicit tests for spatial transfer and length scaling. The paper does not only report end accuracy; it studies how training paradigms and inference-time strategies affect these two kinds of generalization. The key explanation is recursive instability: local decisions may look plausible, but small errors compound along the reasoning chain and break long-horizon performance. Compared with broader reasoning benchmarks, the setup is intentionally simplified so that failures can be traced to learning dynamics or inference behavior rather than benchmark noise.","experiments":"The main empirical result from the abstract is that models show strong spatial transfer but persistent failure under length scaling, and the authors attribute the latter to recursive instability. They also analyze how different stages of the learning pipeline shape systematic generalization, although the abstract does not specify model sizes, training scale, baselines such as CoT or search, or the exact degradation curve with horizon length. Even without numbers, the setup is valuable because it reveals whether extrapolation fails abruptly or through gradual error accumulation. That error profile is often more actionable than average task accuracy.","authors_note":""},"tags":["generalization","reasoning","synthetic-task","test-time-scaling"],"notable_authors":["Reza Shokri"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009480","title":"Group Verification-based Policy Optimization for Interactive Coding Agents","authors":["Silong Dai","Changzhi Sun","Haolun Wu","Huanran Zheng","Tao Ji","Junchi Yan","Yuanbin Wu","Dell Zhang","Xiaoling Wang","Xuelong Li"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$1fd","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009480"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009480","category_id":"coding_agent","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：interactive coding agent 的 RLVR 训练只看最终结果奖励不够，因为中间执行反馈本身就包含大量可验证过程信息。像 GRPO 这类方法主要按最终是否通过测试来估优势，但编码代理是强环境交互任务，编译错误、运行报错、单步执行失败都在告诉你当前推理哪一步偏了；忽略这些信号会让 advantage estimation 很粗。","importance":"这项工作的意义在于把 process-verifiable feedback 正式纳入 RLVR。对 reasoning/coding 后训练来说，这比单纯调 reward shaping 更关键，因为它说明一类常被浪费的环境监督可以稳定改善 credit assignment。更广一点看，这给 agentic post-training 一个清晰方向：只要环境可验证，就不必把所有学习都压缩成终局奖励；中间状态同样可以成为高价值训练信号。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 GVPO，Group Verification-based Policy Optimization。它在 group-based policy optimization 框架里加入 advantage shaping，把 outcome-verifiable rewards 和 process-verifiable feedback 结合起来：前者保证长期目标对齐，后者利用中间执行反馈修正每一步的优势估计。和 GRPO 最接近的差别是，GVPO 不再假设一整条轨迹只能靠最终成败来打分，而是把环境返回的可验证过程信号显式变成 step-aware 学习信号。这样设计有效的原因很直接：编码任务中的错误往往可局部定位，中间信号比最终 pass/fail 稠密得多。边界在于它依赖环境反馈质量；如果反馈噪声大、与最终目标错位，shaping 也可能误导策略。","experiments":"从摘要可知，GVPO 针对 interactive coding agents，且主要对比对象是 RLVR/GRPO 类方法。作者声称整合 process-verifiable 与 outcome-verifiable 信号后，能改善 advantage estimation 与学习效果，但摘要没有给出模型规模、代码任务基准、pass@k 或 success rate 数字，也没有说明过程反馈是来自编译器、单测、执行 trace 还是更细粒度工具。读者应重点关注 full paper 里的 ablation：仅用终局奖励、仅用过程信号、两者结合三种设置差多少；以及这种方法能否推广到数学工具使用或 web agent，而不仅是 coding。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper argues that RLVR for interactive coding agents is underusing supervision when it relies mostly on final outcome rewards. In coding environments, intermediate signals such as compile errors, execution failures, and step-level verification already reveal where the reasoning trajectory went wrong. Ignoring them makes advantage estimation too coarse for effective learning.","importance":"This is important because it formalizes process-verifiable feedback as a first-class training signal in RLVR. For reasoning and coding post-training, that is more than simple reward shaping. It shows that environment feedback can improve credit assignment in a principled way when the environment is verifiable. More broadly, it points toward a useful direction for agent training: do not compress all supervision into terminal rewards if the environment already exposes reliable intermediate checks.","technical_approach":"The proposed method is GVPO, Group Verification-based Policy Optimization. It extends group-based policy optimization with an advantage-shaping mechanism that combines outcome-verifiable rewards with process-verifiable feedback. The final reward maintains alignment with the long-horizon objective, while intermediate feedback refines step-level advantages during reasoning. Compared with GRPO-style approaches, the key difference is that a trajectory is no longer judged only by whether it eventually passes. The environment’s local verification signals become explicit learning targets. This should help because coding errors are often locally attributable, so process feedback is much denser than binary success or failure. The limitation is equally clear: if intermediate feedback is noisy or poorly aligned with the final goal, shaping may introduce bias.","experiments":"From the abstract, the paper focuses on interactive coding agents and compares against RLVR baselines such as GRPO. The claimed benefit is better advantage estimation and improved learning by integrating process-verifiable and outcome-verifiable signals. However, the abstract gives no model sizes, coding benchmarks, pass-rate metrics, or exact gains, and it does not specify whether process feedback comes from compilers, tests, execution traces, or finer-grained environment tools. The most important things to inspect in the full paper are ablations among terminal-only, process-only, and combined rewards, and whether the method transfers beyond coding to tool-using math or web agents.","authors_note":""},"tags":["coding-agent","rlvr","process-reward","verification"],"notable_authors":["Silong Dai","Tao Ji"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009483","title":"LookaheadKV: Fast and Accurate KV cache 线性增长，必须做 eviction，但现有更准的“看一眼未来再决定丢谁”方法往往需要先额外生成 draft response，代价太高。也就是说，问题不是大家不知道未来信息有用，而是拿到未来代理信号本身太贵，抵消了 cache 压缩带来的收益。","importance":"这项工作的意义是把 KV eviction 里的“future-aware importance estimation”做成了低开销近似。对 serving 和长上下文推理系统来说，这属于系统级贡献，但对 pretrain 研究也有启发：它进一步说明注意力缓存的重要性不是静态的，而是与未来生成需求强相关；如果未来依赖可以被更便宜地估计，就能在不明显伤害质量的前提下压缩上下文记忆。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 LookaheadKV，一种无需显式生成草稿答案、就能近似窥视未来的 KV eviction 方法。现有路线通常先用 draft generator 产出 surrogate future response，再据此评估哪些 prompt KV 未来还会被注意到；LookaheadKV 的创新点就在于去掉这一步生成开销，直接从某种更廉价的未来代理信号中估计缓存重要性。虽然摘要没展开机制细节，但设计目标很明确：保留 future-aware eviction 的精度优势，同时避免额外 decoding。和 purely retrospective importance score 方法相比，它赌的是“近未来相关性可以在不生成 token 的情况下被近似预测”；边界则在于代理信号若不够准，eviction 错误会在长生成过程中累积放大。","experiments":"摘要能确认的实验主线是：在长上下文 KV cache eviction 场景下，LookaheadKV 追求同时兼顾速度和准确率，主要对比对象应包括传统 importance-based eviction 和需要 draft generation 的 future-glimpsing 方法。作者把“without generation”放进标题，说明延迟或算力开销下降应是核心卖点之一。遗憾的是摘要没有给出目标模型规模、上下文长度、cache 压缩比、吞吐提升、准确率损失或相对 baseline 的具体数字。对实践者而言，最该看的不是单一 benchmark，而是质量-延迟 Pareto：在相同 memory budget 下它比 H2O、SnapKV 或草稿式 future-aware 方法省了多少算，同时掉点多少。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a practical bottleneck in long-context inference: KV cache grows linearly with prompt length, so eviction is necessary, but the more accurate future-aware eviction methods are expensive because they first generate a draft future response. The issue is not that future information is unhelpful. It is that obtaining a surrogate future through extra decoding can erase much of the efficiency gain.","importance":"This is primarily a systems contribution, but it is relevant beyond serving details. It shows that KV importance is inherently tied to future generation needs, not just past attention patterns. If that future dependence can be estimated cheaply, cache compression can be improved without a large quality penalty. That matters for long-context deployment and also informs how we should think about memory usage in autoregressive models.","technical_approach":"The method, LookaheadKV, aims to estimate future-aware KV importance without explicitly generating draft tokens. Existing approaches produce a surrogate future response and use it to decide which prompt KV entries will matter later. LookaheadKV removes that generation step and replaces it with a cheaper proxy for future relevance. The abstract does not expose the exact mechanism, but the design goal is clear: keep the quality advantage of lookahead-based eviction while avoiding the decode overhead. Compared with purely retrospective importance scores, this approach assumes near-future relevance can be predicted well enough without full token generation. Its failure mode is also clear: if the proxy is inaccurate, eviction mistakes may accumulate over long responses.","experiments":"From the abstract, the evaluation is on long-context KV eviction, with the intended comparison likely against standard importance-based eviction and prior lookahead methods that rely on draft generation. However, the abstract gives no model sizes, context lengths, compression ratios, throughput gains, or quality drops. For practitioners, the main thing to inspect is the quality-latency Pareto: at the same memory budget, how much compute does LookaheadKV save relative to methods like draft-based future-aware eviction, and what accuracy loss does it incur relative to stronger baselines such as H2O- or SnapKV-style approaches?","authors_note":""},"tags":["kv-cache","long-context","inference","serving"],"notable_authors":["Jinwoo Ahn","Akhil Kedia"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009493","title":"GEPA: Reflective Prompt From Gradient Analysis to Efficient Curriculum Learning for Reasoning LLMs","authors":["Yongcheng Zeng","Zexu Sun","Bokai Ji","Erxue Min","Hengyi Cai","Shuaiqiang Wang","Dawei Yin","Haifeng Zhang","Xu Chen","Jun Wang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$20a","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009577"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009577","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：面向推理任务的 LLM 强化学习里，如何把训练算力花在最值得的 prompt 和 rollout 上。现有 curriculum learning 往往只做粗糙的样本过滤，默认每个样本分配近似相同的采样预算，没有把“样本难度差异”和“梯度贡献差异”显式建模，因此容易把大量 rollout 浪费在低收益或高噪声样本上。","importance":"这项工作的价值主要在方法级和训练机制级。它把 curriculum 问题从经验性启发式，往“基于 RL 梯度效率的资源分配”推进了一步，对 reasoning post-train 尤其有启发，因为当前很多推理 RL 的瓶颈不是模型不会学，而是 rollout 太贵、有效梯度太稀。若论文里的分析和策略在不同基座与任务上都稳健，它的意义不只是提一点分数，而是给出一种更系统的训练 compute allocation 原则。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 CurES：从强化学习梯度优化角度，同时决定训练 prompt 的选择和每个 prompt 的 rollout 数量分配。它不是简单按难度排序做课程，而是先分析哪些样本能带来更高效、更稳定的梯度更新，再据此做采样分布和 rollout budget 的联合设计。和常见的难度过滤、reward 过滤不同，这类方法的关键优点是把“是否训练这个样本”和“训练它训练到什么程度”拆开处理；前者控制偏差，后者控制方差。论文摘要显示作者给出了理论分析，指出最优采样分布由若干可估计量决定；如果这个估计足够稳定，那么它比静态 curriculum 更适合 reasoning RL 这种高方差场景。边界也很明确：这类方法依赖在线统计或 proxy 估计，若估计噪声过大，可能反而引入新的训练不稳定。","experiments":"摘要没有给出具体模型规模、数据规模、基线方法和数值结果，因此目前只能确认实验重点应是训练效率而非单点能力上限。合理预期的核心实验会比较：固定训练预算下的最终性能、达到同等性能所需 rollout 数、不同 prompt 选择策略、以及不同 rollout 分配策略。真正值得关注的不是平均提升多少，而是两个细节：第一，是否在不同难度分布的数据集上都稳定；第二，收益是否主要来自减少无效 rollout，而不是额外引入复杂调度开销。若论文包含 ablation，最关键的应是 prompt selection 与 rollout allocation 分别贡献多少，以及梯度统计 proxy 的误差对性能有多敏感。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"This paper targets a concrete bottleneck in RL for reasoning LLMs: how to spend rollout compute on the right prompts, and in the right amounts. Existing curriculum methods mostly rely on coarse filtering or narrow difficulty bands, which ignores variation in gradient utility across prompts and wastes substantial compute on low-yield or high-variance trajectories.","importance":"The contribution is methodological and training-dynamics oriented. It reframes curriculum learning as a compute allocation problem grounded in RL gradient efficiency, which matters for reasoning post-training where rollout cost, not model capacity, is often the main constraint. If the proposed allocation rule transfers across tasks and base models, the impact is larger than a small benchmark gain: it suggests a more principled way to schedule expensive RL training.","technical_approach":"The proposed method, CurES, jointly optimizes two decisions: which prompts to train on and how many rollouts to allocate to each prompt. This is not just difficulty-based ordering. The design starts from gradient analysis, aiming to prioritize samples that produce more useful and stable policy updates. Relative to common filtering heuristics, the main design choice is to separate prompt inclusion from rollout budgeting: the former controls bias in what the model sees, while the latter controls variance in how noisily each sample is learned. The abstract suggests a theoretical derivation of an efficiency-optimal sampling distribution. That is well matched to reasoning RL, where gradient variance is a first-order issue. The main risk is estimation noise: if the proxy for gradient utility is unstable, the scheduler may become brittle.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide model size, dataset scale, baselines, or exact gains, so the evidence cannot yet be summarized numerically. The key experiments are likely to focus on training efficiency: final performance under fixed rollout budgets, rollout savings to reach a target performance level, and comparisons against standard prompt filtering or uniform rollout allocation. The most informative ablations would be whether prompt selection and rollout allocation each matter independently, and how sensitive the method is to errors in the gradient-based proxy. For this paper, the critical question is not headline SOTA but whether it consistently reduces wasted rollouts without introducing large scheduling overhead.","authors_note":""},"tags":["curriculum-learning","rl-for-reasoning","training-efficiency","gradient-analysis"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009588","title":"Generalizable End-to-End Tool-Use RL with LLM agent 的 RL 训练为什么很难泛化到新工具和新工作流，以及如何构造一个既可扩展又可验证的训练环境。现有做法要么依赖静态 SFT 轨迹，学到的是表面模仿；要么在很窄的任务上做 RL，策略容易过拟合开发集，换工具或换交互结构就脆弱。","importance":"这项工作的贡献首先是数据/环境级，其次是方法级。它提出的不是一个单独的 agent policy assignment。对后续研究，这类环境基础设施往往比单个算法更有复用价值。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 CodeGym：用 coding problem 作为结构化载体，自动合成多轮、多配置、可验证的工具使用环境，并在其上做端到端 RL。这个设计的关键判断是，代码执行天然具备状态转移清晰、反馈可验证、任务可程序化扩展的优势，能模拟很多真实世界 workflow 的组合结构，比如调用工具、读写中间结果、错误恢复和多步规划。与只收集人工工具轨迹不同，CodeGym 更强调环境生成能力；与只做 code agent 不同，它把代码看成训练 tool-use policy 的“代理世界”。代价也很明显：代码环境虽然结构丰富，但和真实网页、API、桌面操作仍有 domain gap，因此泛化主张最终要看跨环境迁移证据，而不能只看在合成环境内的 reward。","experiments":"摘要没有给出模型规模、环境数量、训练步数或具体数值，但从表述看，关键实验应该围绕‘泛化’而不是单任务性能。最重要的结果应包括：在未见工具、未见 workflow 配置或不同任务组合上的成功率提升；和 SFT、窄域 RL、或已有 tool-use agent 训练框架的比较；以及合成环境多样性对泛化的影响。最值得关注的 ablation 是：环境合成的哪些维度最有用，比如工具组合数、回合长度、可验证反馈粒度；以及 RL 是否真的学到了跨任务策略，而不是只在代码域内记住模板。如果论文只证明对 coding benchmarks 有效，那它对通用 agent RL 的外推会偏弱。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a central weakness in tool-use LLM agents: RL training often fails to generalize beyond the specific tools and workflows seen during development. Static SFT trajectories mostly teach imitation, while narrow-task RL tends to overfit environment details and breaks when tools, interfaces, or interaction structure change.","importance":"The main contribution is at the environment and data-generation level, with a secondary methodological angle. Instead of proposing another agent policy tweak, the paper builds a scalable, controllable, and verifiable substrate for tool-use RL. That matters for the pretrain-to-posttrain pipeline because agent capability may depend more on the structure and diversity of training environments than on simply adding more preference data or more elaborate RL objectives. Infrastructure of this kind is often more reusable than a single optimization trick.","technical_approach":"The core proposal is CodeGym, a framework that uses coding problems as a structured substrate for synthesizing multi-turn, tool-using environments with verifiable outcomes. The design choice is deliberate: code execution provides explicit state transitions, cheap verification, and programmable variation, which lets the authors simulate many structural motifs of real workflows such as tool invocation, intermediate artifact handling, error recovery, and multi-step planning. Unlike human-collected tool traces, CodeGym emphasizes environment generation. Unlike code-only agents, it treats coding as a proxy world for training general tool-use policies. The trade-off is also clear: coding environments are rich but still narrower than real web, API, or desktop ecosystems, so the generalization claim must ultimately be supported by cross-environment transfer rather than in-domain reward alone.","experiments":"The abstract does not report model sizes, number of environments, training budget, or exact improvements. The critical experiments are likely to test generalization: success on unseen tools, unseen workflow configurations, and new task compositions, compared against SFT baselines, narrow-task RL, or prior tool-use training pipelines. The most informative ablations would isolate which environment synthesis dimensions matter most, such as tool diversity, trajectory length, and verification granularity, and whether RL learns reusable interaction strategies rather than code-specific templates. If the gains hold only on coding benchmarks, the broader claim about general tool-use RL would remain limited.","authors_note":""},"tags":["tool-use","rl","generalization","synthetic-data","llm-agent"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009692","title":"SafeDPO: A Simple Approach to Direct Preference Optimization with Enhanced Safety","authors":["Geon-Hyeong Kim","Yu Jin Kim","Byoungjip Kim","Honglak Lee","Kyunghoon Bae","Youngsoo Jang","Moontae Lee"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$20c","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009692"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009692","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：在 DPO 框架里，如何更简单地把安全约束纳入优化，而且不依赖额外 reward model、辅助网络或多阶段训练。现有安全对齐方法常常有效但工程链路很重，导致训练复杂、目标不透明，也更难确认最终优化的到底是不是原始安全目标。","importance":"这项工作是方法级但带有一定理论支撑。它的重要性在于，如果安全约束下的最优策略真能写成闭式形式，并导出一个与原问题等价且可直接优化的目标，那么安全对齐可以从‘复杂 RLHF pipeline’收敛到‘轻量 DPO 变体’。这对工业训练尤其有意义，因为它减少了组件数量，也降低了 reward hacking 和目标漂移的空间。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 SafeDPO：从原始安全对齐目标出发，在温和假设下推导闭式最优策略，再进一步得到一个可 tractable 优化且与原问题等价的目标。它不是在 DPO 外面再套一个安全 classifier，也不是把 helpfulness 和 safety 做多阶段折中，而是把安全约束直接写进 preference optimization 的目标里。设计上最关键的优点是‘保留原最优解’：很多安全方法通过 surrogate loss 换取可训练性，但 surrogate 与真实目标不完全一致，容易出现训练有效、行为不稳。SafeDPO 如果理论上真是等价变换，那么它的核心卖点就是简单且不偏题。边界在于假设条件是否现实，以及安全数据分布是否足够支持这个目标学习。","experiments":"摘要没有给出所用基座模型、偏好数据规模、安全 benchmark 或提升数值。最关键的实验应比较 SafeDPO 与 vanilla DPO、带安全 reward model 的 RLHF/DPO 变体、以及多阶段安全对齐流程，在 helpfulness-safety trade-off 上的差异。值得重点看两个方面：第一，在不牺牲太多帮助性的前提下，安全指标提升多少；第二，是否在不同风险类型上都稳健，而不是只对 refusal 类 benchmark 有效。若论文包含消融，最有价值的是验证理论推导中的每个简化假设去掉后会发生什么，以及 SafeDPO 对数据噪声和 preference 质量的敏感性。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a practical gap in preference optimization: how to incorporate safety constraints into DPO without adding extra reward models, auxiliary networks, or multi-stage training. Existing safety alignment pipelines can work, but they are often operationally heavy and make it harder to tell whether the training objective still matches the original safety goal.","importance":"This is a method contribution with a meaningful theoretical backbone. If the safety-constrained optimum really admits a closed-form policy and a tractable objective that is provably equivalent to the original problem, then part of safety alignment can move from complex RLHF stacks to a lightweight DPO-style recipe. That is particularly relevant in production settings because fewer components usually mean less objective drift and fewer opportunities for reward hacking.","technical_approach":"SafeDPO starts from the original safety alignment objective, derives the optimal policy in closed form under mild assumptions, and then turns that result into a directly optimizable objective. The main design choice is to bake safety into preference optimization itself rather than bolt on a separate safety classifier or use a staged helpfulness-then-safety pipeline. The attractive property is solution preservation: many practical methods rely on surrogate objectives that are easier to optimize but no longer exactly target the desired constrained optimum. If SafeDPO truly retains the same optimum, its key strength is simplicity without changing the target. The main caveats are whether the assumptions are realistic and whether available safety preference data are rich enough to support the objective well.","experiments":"The abstract does not specify the base models, dataset sizes, safety benchmarks, or gains. The decisive experiments should compare SafeDPO against vanilla DPO, safety-aware RLHF or DPO variants with auxiliary reward models, and multi-stage alignment pipelines, focusing on the helpfulness-safety trade-off rather than a single metric. The most useful evidence would show how much safety improves without a large drop in utility, and whether those gains hold across different types of unsafe behavior rather than only refusal-style evaluations. A strong ablation would test how robust the method is when the assumptions behind the derivation are weakened or when preference labels become noisy.","authors_note":""},"tags":["dpo","safety","alignment","preference-optimization","rlhf"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009699","title":"Influence-Preserving Proxies for Gradient-Based Data Selection in LLM FineTuning","authors":["Sirui Chen","Yunzhe Qi","Mengting Ai","Yifan Sun","Ruizhong Qiu","Jiaru Zou","Jingrui He"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$20d","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009699"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009699","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：梯度影响力驱动的数据选择方法在大模型 SFT 中太贵，怎样找到既便宜又真正保留 influence 结构的 proxy model。现有做法通常直接拿一个现成小模型代替目标模型，但这种 proxy 与目标模型的学习动力学往往不对齐，因此选出来的数据并不一定真对目标模型最有用。","importance":"这项工作是方法级，也带有明显系统价值。数据选择是高杠杆问题，因为它影响的不只是最终精度，还影响训练成本和数据治理方式。对 pretrain/post-train 来说，这篇工作的启示是：proxy 不是越小越好，关键是它是否保留了目标模型梯度影响关系。如果 IProX 真能在低成本下逼近 target influence，它会让基于 influence 的数据筛选从研究工具更接近工程可用。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 IProX，一个两阶段框架，用目标模型本身导出 influence-preserving proxy，而不是直接选用现成小模型。摘要说明作者的目标是让 proxy 在梯度影响估计上与 target 对齐，并且 proxy 的大小可灵活调节。这个设计比‘直接蒸馏一个小模型’更具体，因为它关注的不是一般任务性能，而是保留样本间相对影响排序。两阶段流程大概率分别解决：先构造能继承目标模型表示/梯度结构的 proxy，再进一步校准其 influence estimation。和 TracIn、Influence Functions 相比，它牺牲了一部分精确性来换取可扩展性；和 off-the-shelf small LM 相比，它牺牲了即插即用，换来更强的目标相关性。关键边界是 proxy 若只在局部训练状态有效，那么跨训练阶段复用能力可能有限。","experiments":"摘要没有给出基座模型规模、proxy 压缩比、SFT 数据集、下游任务或具体提升数字。值得重点关注的实验有三类：第一，proxy influence 排序与 target influence 排序的一致性；第二，用所选数据做真实 SFT 时，相比随机选、启发式选、现成小模型代理选，最终性能提升多少；第三，成本节省是否足够显著，能否覆盖构造 proxy 的额外开销。最有价值的 ablation 应该是 proxy 尺寸、对齐方式、以及是否需要持续重估 proxy。如果只有静态一次性 proxy 就能跨多个 checkpoint 有效，这会很强；若必须频繁重建，工程吸引力会下降。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a practical bottleneck in gradient-based data selection for LLM fine-tuning: exact influence methods are too expensive, but naive small-model proxies often fail because they do not preserve the target model’s learning dynamics. As a result, the selected data may be cheap to rank but not actually useful for the model we care about.","importance":"This is a method contribution with clear systems relevance. Data selection is a high-leverage problem because it affects quality, cost, and dataset curation strategy at the same time. The key message here is that a proxy should not just be smaller; it should preserve the target model’s influence structure. If IProX can do that at low cost, it could make influence-based selection much more practical for real SFT pipelines.","technical_approach":"IProX is a two-stage framework that derives an influence-preserving proxy from the target model itself rather than relying on an off-the-shelf smaller model. The design goal is not generic task transfer but alignment in gradient-based influence estimation, with flexible control over proxy size. That is a meaningful shift. A generic distilled small model may match outputs while still distorting which training examples matter most. IProX instead tries to preserve relative influence relationships among samples. Relative to TracIn or influence functions, it trades some exactness for scalability. Relative to standard small-model proxies, it gives up pure convenience in exchange for much better target relevance. The main practical question is whether the proxy remains valid across training stages or only around a narrow optimization state.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide target model sizes, proxy compression ratios, datasets, tasks, or numerical gains. The key experiments should therefore establish three things: how well proxy influence rankings match target influence rankings, how much downstream SFT performance improves when data are selected using IProX instead of random or heuristic baselines, and whether the compute savings are large enough to justify the extra proxy-construction step. Microscaling Formats for Large Language Models","authors":["Wenyuan Liu","Haoqian Meng","Yilun Luo","Peng Zhang","Xindian Ma"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$20e","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10009711"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10009711","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：如何让 LLM 量化真正吃到 Blackwell 上 FP4 Tensor Core 的硬件红利，而不是算法上做了 INT4、系统上却跑不满。现有 weight-activation INT4 方法在精度上已经很激进，但 kernel 和数据格式与新硬件的 FP4/MX 路线不匹配，导致理论速度优势难以兑现。","importance":"这项工作属于系统与算法协同设计。对 LLM 部署研究很重要，因为它不是单纯把量化 bit 再压低，而是把量化格式、混合精度策略和 GEMM kernel 一起重做，直接面向新一代硬件。对训练研究的间接启示是，未来 low-bit 路线越来越依赖软硬件共设计；只报 perplexity 而不报真实 kernel 吞吐，意义会越来越有限。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 MicroMix：基于 Microscaling 数据格式的 mixed-precision quantization 算法和配套 GEMM kernel 联合设计。它支持 MXFP4、MXFP6、MXFP8 任意通道组合，并输出 BF16 结果，目标是在 Blackwell 架构上兼顾精度与吞吐。设计思想很明确：不同通道或张量子结构对量化敏感度不同，统一压到最激进的 4-bit 往往不是最优；用 microscaling 格式做细粒度混合精度，可以把高敏感部分留更高精度，把低敏感部分下压到 FP4，从而更好匹配硬件执行路径。与传统 INT4 kernel 相比，它不是只换了数据类型，而是重做了适配 FP4 Tensor Core 的算子链路。","experiments":"摘要给出了一个明确数字：Blackwell 的 FP4 Tensor Core 相比 FP16 最高可提供 4× 加速，但没有给出 MicroMix 的实际端到端速度、精度损失和模型规模。最关键的实验应包括：在主流 LLM 上，与 INT4 weight-activation quantization 和其他 mixed-precision 方法相比，真实 token/s 或 GEMM throughput 的提升；在相近精度下，MXFP4/6/8 的组合如何优于纯 FP4 或纯 INT4；以及 kernel 层和模型层结果是否一致。值得重点看的是 sensitivity study：哪些层或通道必须保留更高精度，是否存在跨模型可迁移的模式。如果只有 kernel microbenchmark 漂亮，而整模型推理收益有限，这类工作落地价值会打折。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a deployment bottleneck: how to actually realize the speed advantages of Blackwell FP4 Tensor Cores for LLM quantization, rather than having an INT4 algorithm that looks efficient on paper but maps poorly to the new hardware. Recent weight-activation INT4 methods push compression aggressively, but their kernels and data formats do not align well with Blackwell’s native low-precision path.","importance":"This is a systems-plus-algorithm co-design contribution. It matters because the paper is not just lowering bit-widths further; it redesigns the quantization format, mixed-precision policy, and GEMM kernel together for a new hardware target. The broader lesson is increasingly relevant for LLM research: low-bit efficiency is becoming hardware-specific, and reporting perplexity without reporting real kernel throughput is no longer enough.","technical_approach":"MicroMix jointly proposes a mixed-precision quantization algorithm and a GEMM kernel built around Microscaling (MX) formats. The kernel supports arbitrary combinations of MXFP4, MXFP6, and MXFP8 channels, producing BF16 outputs, with the goal of balancing accuracy and throughput on Blackwell. The underlying design logic is simple and practical: different channels have different sensitivity to quantization, so forcing everything into the most aggressive 4-bit format is often suboptimal. Fine-grained mixed precision with microscaling allows sensitive channels to stay at higher precision while pushing more robust ones down to FP4, all while staying on the hardware-native execution path. Relative to standard INT4 kernels, this is not just a datatype swap but a full re-alignment to the FP4 Tensor Core stack.","experiments":"The abstract gives one hardware reference point: Blackwell FP4 Tensor Cores can offer up to 4x speedup over FP16, but it does not report MicroMix’s actual end-to-end throughput, model sizes, or accuracy deltas. The crucial experiments should compare real GEMM speed and model-level inference throughput against INT4 weight-activation baselines and other mixed-precision schemes, while showing how MXFP4/6/8 combinations improve the accuracy-speed trade-off relative to pure FP4 or pure INT4. The most informative analysis would identify which layers or channels require higher precision and whether those patterns transfer across models. If the gains are strong only in kernel microbenchmarks but modest in full-model inference, the practical impact would be narrower than the hardware pitch suggests.","authors_note":""},"tags":["quantization","mixed-precision","llm","fp4","serving"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10009720","title":"INSTANT: Compressing Gradients and Activations for Resource-Efficient Training","authors":["Tuan-Kiet Doan","Trung-Hieu Tran","Enzo Tartaglione","Nikola Simidjievski","Van-Tam Nguyen"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Deep learning has advanced at an unprecedented pace. This progress has led to a significant increase in its complexity. However, despite extensive research on accelerating inference, training deep models directly within a resource-constrained budget remains a considerable challenge due to its high computational and memory requirements. In this paper, we introduce INSTANT (compressIng gradieNtS and acTivAtions for resource-efficieNt Training), a method designed to address both the computational and the memory bottlenecks when training. INSTANT reduces resource demands during backpropagation by projecting gradients and activations into a low-rank subspace and performing computation within that compressed representation. Experimental results demonstrate that INSTANT achieves a $15\\times$ reduction in computational cost and $32\\times$ reduction in activation memory with negligible impact on model performance. The code will be made publicly available upon the paper's acceptance. In this work, we first analyze thousands of reasoning paths to identify Reasoning Drift, a key failure mode where models get locked into flawed reasoning patterns. We reveal that the manifestation of drift is a complex interplay between universal functional tendencies and unique, model-specific signatures. Based on the diagnosis, we propose Reasoning-Aware Activation Steering, a novel inference-time intervention method to gently nudge the model's activations away from pathological patterns. We pre-compute a library of vectors from contrastive functional transitions and apply them dynamically. Experiments show that our method effectively mitigates the drift problem and boosts accuracy. Additionally, it generalizes to out-of-distribution tasks, demonstrating a deeper capture of valid reasoning principles. RLHF 或 Constitution 风格方法通常把原则转成偏好比较再学一个 reward model，但训练目标和最终社会选择性质之间常常脱节，所以即使局部 loss 最优，也不保证整体决策满足想要的规范性约束。","importance":"这篇工作的贡献主要是理论层面，不直接给预训练算法，但对 pretrain↔posttrain 接口仍有价值。它的重要性在于提醒我们：如果对齐信号来自少量抽象原则，那么损失函数设计不是中性的，某些 loss 可能天然违背希望满足的公理；这会影响 reward modeling、AI feedback 和原则驱动数据合成的可靠性边界。","technical_approach":"核心方法是在线性 social choice 模型下分析 loss-based rules 的公理性质。作者把 helpfulness、toxicity 这类原则建模为响应嵌入空间中的线性方向，这个假设来自 linear representation hypothesis；在此基础上，研究最小化二元偏好损失的规则是否满足若干对齐公理，并给出可 enforced 的条件或不可能性结果。这个框架的价值不在拟合真实世界全部复杂性，而在于把“原则-偏好-loss-决策”链条拆开，找出哪一步带来系统性偏差；边界也很明确，线性方向假设只适合作为一阶近似。","experiments":"从摘要看，这篇论文的重点是理论分析，不是大规模经验 benchmark，因此没有可报告的模型规模和 SOTA 数字。若正文带有实验，作用更可能是验证理论构造或展示反例，而不是追求任务分数；读者应重点看定理假设是否合理、结论覆盖哪些常见 loss，以及这些结论能否映射到现代 reward model 的实际训练设置。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a theoretical but important question: when alignment is trained through loss minimization on binary preferences, do the resulting rules satisfy basic axioms one would want from principle-guided AI alignment? In current RLHF- or constitution-style pipelines, principles are often turned into pairwise preferences and then optimized with standard losses, but the loss optimum need not preserve the intended normative structure.","importance":"This is mainly a theory contribution, not a new pretraining recipe, yet it matters for the pretrain-to-posttrain interface. The key message is that loss design is not innocuous when alignment signals come from abstract principles: some losses may systematically violate desirable axioms, which has implications for reward modeling, AI feedback, and principle-driven data generation.","technical_approach":"The authors study a linear social choice model in which principles such as helpfulness or toxicity are represented as linear directions in a response embedding space. Within this setup, they analyze whether loss-based rules trained on binary comparisons satisfy specified alignment axioms, and likely derive conditions, guarantees, or impossibility results. The framework is useful because it isolates the full chain from principles to preferences to loss to decision rule. Its limitation is equally clear: the linear-direction assumption is a first-order approximation, not a full model of real alignment behavior.","experiments":"The abstract suggests a primarily theoretical paper, so there are no reliable model scales or benchmark gains to report here. 预训练研究，它的重要性在于表明长上下文视频不是单靠更大上下文窗能解决的，必须利用视频冗余结构做层次化 token budgeting，这类思路对原生多模态预训练很有借鉴价值。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 HiCo，Hierarchical video token Compression。它利用长视频里的视觉冗余，从 clip-level 进一步压到 Kanade"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$222","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10010060"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10010060","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文讨论的是一个偏理论但和语言模型支持集学习直接相关的问题：如果我们不仅看到正样本，还能得到 next-symbol prediction 形式的标签，是否就能高效识别一个语言或近似恢复 LM 的支持集。过去很多关于神经序列模型的经验分析用了 NSP 设定，但它到底带来了多大可学习性提升、边界在哪，并不清楚。","importance":"这项工作的贡献是理论边界澄清，而不是直接改进模型性能。它的重要性在于把“带 NSP 标签的支持集学习”从模糊直觉变成了可证的可学/不可学结论：某些识别任务确实可行，但 PAC 学习 DFA 仍然计算困难。对 pretrain 研究的启示是，哪怕给模型额外的 prefix-level 结构信号，支持集恢复也未必在计算上变容易；这提醒我们不要把 richer supervision 和 tractable learning 混为一谈。","technical_approach":"方法上是形式语言与学习理论分析。作者研究的监督形式比纯正样本更强：对每个前缀，不仅知道该前缀是否本身属于语言，还知道哪些下一个符号还能走向某个接受串。基于这个设定，他们首先证明 n 状态以内的 DFA 在这种增强监督下是可识别的；但接着又证明，即便有这种更强信号，PAC-learning DFA 仍然是计算上困难的，而且仅靠正样本做精确识别也有限制。这个结果的价值在于把“信息是否足够”和“算法是否高效”清楚地区分开。它和经验性 LM 分析工作的不同，是不去看某个模型学得好不好，而是给出这个任务本身的可学性边界。","experiments":"摘要主打理论结果，没有给出常规意义上的模型实验、参数量或 benchmark。能确认的关键事实有三点：第一，作者证明了在 NSP 标签增强下，有限状态 DFA 类可识别；第二，他们证明了 PAC-learning 依旧计算困难；第三，他们指出这种设定可用于学习语言模型的截断支持集。若论文包含实验，更多也应是算法示例或合成 automata 上的验证，而不是大模型 benchmark。对工程读者来说，真正有用的不是数字，而是这个结论：prefix 级可行后继标签确实增加了信息量，但没有神奇地消除组合复杂度。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"This paper studies a theoretical question with direct relevance to language-model support recovery: if we augment positive examples with next-symbol-prediction style labels, can we efficiently identify a language or recover the support of a language model. The NSP setting has appeared in empirical analyses of sequence models, but its actual learnability benefits were not well characterized.","importance":"The contribution is about learning-theoretic boundaries, not a new practical model. That still matters because it separates what richer supervision makes identifiable from what it makes computationally tractable. For pretraining researchers, the message is useful: adding prefix-level structural supervision may increase information, but it does not automatically make support learning easy.","technical_approach":"The paper analyzes formal language learning under an enriched supervision regime. For each prefix, the learner is told whether the prefix itself is in the language and which next symbols can still lead to an accepting string. Under this setup, the authors show that the class of DFAs with at most n states is identifiable from positive examples plus NSP labels. They then show a harder result: despite this extra supervision, PAC-learning DFAs remains computationally hard, and exact identification from only positive examples still has strict limits. The key value is the distinction between identifiability and efficient learnability, which is often blurred in empirical sequence-model discussions.","experiments":"The abstract emphasizes theory, not benchmark-driven experiments. The main factual outcomes are theorem-level: DFAs are identifiable under NSP-augmented supervision, PAC learning remains computationally hard, and the setting can be used to learn truncated LM support. If empirical sections exist, they are likely illustrative algorithmic validations on synthetic automata rather than large-model evaluations. For a systems or pretraining reader, the practical takeaway is conceptual rather than numeric: NSP labels provide meaningful extra signal, but they do not remove the fundamental combinatorial hardness.","authors_note":""},"tags":["automata-learning","formal-language","next-token-prediction"],"notable_authors":["Michael Hahn","Varun
