Encoding Shapes Unlearning in LLMs","authors":["Ruihan Wu","Konstantin Garov","Kamalika Chaudhuri"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in the real world, the ability to ``unlearn'', or remove specific pieces of knowledge post hoc, has become essential for a variety of reasons ranging from privacy regulations to correcting outdated or harmful content. Prior work has proposed unlearning benchmarks and algorithms, and has typically assumed that the training process and the target model are fixed. In this work, we empirically investigate how learning-time encoding in knowledge encoding impact the effectiveness of unlearning factual knowledge.  We conduct two studies: (i) examining how paraphrased descriptions influence unlearning performance, and (ii) analyzing unlearning when multiple facts are embedded within the same training text chunk.  Our empirical study reveals two important implications: a new perspective for interpreting unlearning performance and practical strategies for improving LLM unlearning. Show more","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10010933"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10010933","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["unlearning","llm","training-dynamics","memorization"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10010944","title":"Post-training Large Language Models for Diverse High-Quality Responses","authors":["Yilei Chen","Souradip Chakraborty","Lorenz Wolf","Ioannis Paschalidis","Aldo Pacchiano"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Reinforcement learning has emerged as a popular method for post-training large language models (LLMs). While improving the model's performance on downstream tasks, it often reduces the model's output diversity, leading to narrow, canonical responses. Existing methods to enhance diversity are limited, either by operating at inference time or by focusing on lexical differences. We propose a novel training method named DQO (Diversity Quality Optimization) based on determinantal point processes (DPPs) to jointly optimize LLMs for quality and semantic diversity. Our approach samples and embeds a group of responses for each prompt, then uses the determinant of a kernel-based similarity matrix to measure diversity as the volume spanned by the embeddings of these responses. Experiments across instruction-following, summarization, story generation, and reasoning tasks demonstrate that our method substantially improves semantic diversity without sacrificing model quality. Show more","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10010944"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10010944","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文解决的是：RL 后训练虽然能提高任务表现，但经常把回复压成少数“标准答案”，导致高质量却不多样。现有提升多样性的办法大多停留在推理时采样控制，或者只鼓励词面差异，难以在训练阶段直接优化“语义上不同但都好”的响应集合。这个问题重要，因为很多真实应用并不需要唯一最优答案，而需要一组高质量、风格或内容互补的候选。","importance":"结论是：这是一篇典型的 post-training 目标函数改造工作，贡献点在于把‘质量-多样性’联合优化从推理启发式推进到训练目标层面。对 pretrain/posttrain 接口的启示是，RL 奖励如果只盯单样本质量，模型会自然收缩到低熵策略；要保留有用的响应分布，训练信号必须显式奖励跨样本的语义分散性。这个思路对通用助手、检索问答、多候选生成都 relevant，但它更像目标设计进展，而不是范式级突破。","technical_approach":"方法上，作者提出 DQO，用 determinantal point process 来同时优化质量和语义多样性。关键机制不是单条回答打分，而是对同一 prompt 一次采样一组回答，先把它们嵌入到语义空间，再用相似度核矩阵的行列式衡量这组回答张成的体积；体积越大，表示答案之间越不冗余。这个设计有效的原因是 DPP 天然惩罚语义上彼此接近的样本，比 lexical diversity 更接近用户真正关心的“不同思路”。代价也很明确：训练时要做组采样和嵌入，相比标准 RL 更贵，而且多样性奖励依赖嵌入空间质量；如果嵌入不能反映任务相关语义，优化目标就会偏。","experiments":"摘要只明确说在多个设置上做了实验，并展示 DQO 能提升高质量多样响应，但没有给出模型大小、任务、baseline 和数值幅度，因此无法可靠写出具体提升。按方法性质，合理的核心比较对象应包括标准 RL post-training、只做推理时多样性控制的方法、以及只优化 lexical diversity 的训练目标。最值得看的 ablation 是：不加 DPP、换不同嵌入器、组大小变化、以及质量与多样性权重的 trade-off 曲线；如果论文把这几项讲清楚，才能判断它是在“保质量的前提下扩展支持集”，还是只是把回复拉散但牺牲任务完成度。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a common failure mode of RL post-training: better task performance often comes with narrower, more canonical responses. Existing diversity methods are usually inference-time heuristics or focus on surface-level variation, which does not directly optimize for semantically distinct yet high-quality answers during training. This matters because many real applications need a set of good alternatives, not a single polished response mode.","importance":"The contribution is best viewed as an objective-design advance in post-training. Its main value is moving quality-diversity optimization from decoding heuristics into the training objective itself. The broader lesson for the pretrain/posttrain interface is straightforward: if RL rewards only per-sample quality, the model will collapse toward low-entropy policies; preserving a useful response distribution requires explicit reward for semantic spread across samples. That is practically relevant, though it is not a paradigm shift.","technical_approach":"The method, DQO, uses determinantal point processes to jointly optimize response quality and semantic diversity. The key move is to score a set of responses for the same prompt rather than scoring each response independently. Responses are embedded into a semantic space, and the determinant of a kernel similarity matrix measures the volume spanned by the set; larger volume means less redundancy. This is a sensible choice because DPPs penalize semantically similar outputs more directly than lexical-diversity metrics do. The trade-off is cost and dependence on the embedding space: group sampling and embedding make training heavier than standard RL, and the diversity signal is only as good as the representation used to define similarity.","experiments":"The abstract claims gains across multiple settings, but it does not provide model sizes, datasets, baselines, or exact margins, so no trustworthy quantitative summary can be given. The right baselines should include standard RL post-training, inference-time diversity controls, and training objectives that reward only lexical variation. The most informative ablations would be removing the DPP term, changing the embedding model, varying group size, and plotting the quality-diversity trade-off. Those results would tell us whether DQO truly expands the support of good answers or merely spreads outputs at the expense of task quality.","authors_note":""},"tags":["rl","diversity","post-training","llm"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10010965","title":"In-Context Algorithm Emulation in Fixed-Weight Transformers","authors":["Jerry Yao-Chieh Hu","Hude Liu","Jennifer Zhang","Han Liu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$253","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10010965"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10010965","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文讨论的是一个 ICL 理论核心问题：固定权重的 Transformer 到底能通过 prompt 在上下文中模拟多大一类算法。过去很多 in-context learning 解释停留在类比层面，比如“像做梯度下降”或“像在做回归”，但缺少明确的构造性证明。作者想证明，不需要更新参数，只靠上下文和一个极小的 Transformer 结构，就能精确模拟一类相当广的算法。","importance":"结论是：这是一篇偏理论的 ICL 能力刻画工作，贡献在于把‘prompt 作为程序’这个说法推到更严格的构造层面。对 pretrain 研究的启发是，固定权重模型的能力边界不只取决于参数存储知识，还取决于注意力结构是否能把上下文组织成一个临时计算图。这个视角对理解 ICL、工具使用乃至 latent reasoning 都有价值，因为它在问：模型在推理时究竟是在检索模板，还是在上下文里执行可编程计算。","technical_approach":"方法上完全是构造性证明。作者区分了两种 in-context algorithm emulation：task-specific mode 和 prompt-programmable mode。前者证明，对于任意可微函数 f:R→R，可以构造一个单头 softmax attention 层，使其前向传播逼近形如 f(w^T x - y) 的函数，这个模板覆盖梯度下降、线性回归、ridge regression 等常见算法。后者进一步证明，一个固定权重的两层 softmax attention 模块可以仅通过 prompt 编程，通用地模拟前述任务特定类中的所有算法。关键意义在于，它把“同一组权重支持多种算法”形式化了；边界也很明确，这里讨论的是可构造函数类和软注意力近似，而不是现实大模型在有限上下文、噪声 prompt 下自然学会这些算法。","experiments":"摘要没有给出常规实验数字，这篇工作重点显然是理论可实现性而非 benchmark 性能。可确认的结果有两层：一层是单头注意力可逼近一大类任务特定算法；另一层是固定权重两层注意力在 prompt-programmable 模式下具有某种通用性。阅读时最值得关注的不是‘能不能做’，而是这些构造需要多长上下文、怎样的数值精度、softmax 温度和 token 编码方式，以及误差如何随问题规模增长。只有这些条件足够合理，结论才对现实 ICL 有解释力。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper studies a central theoretical question behind in-context learning: how large a class of algorithms can a fixed-weight transformer emulate purely through prompting? Many explanations of ICL are suggestive but informal, often saying that transformers behave like gradient descent or regression. This work aims to replace analogy with explicit constructive proofs.","importance":"This is a theory contribution on the expressive power of ICL. Its value is that it makes the idea of 'prompt as program' much more concrete. For pretraining research, the broader lesson is that the capability of a fixed-weight model depends not only on stored parameters, but also on whether attention can organize the context into a temporary computation. That perspective is useful for understanding ICL, tool use, and reasoning-time computation more generally.","technical_approach":"The paper gives constructive proofs for two modes of in-context algorithm emulation. In the task-specific mode, it shows that for any continuously differentiable function f:R→R, a single-head softmax attention layer can approximate functions of the form f(w^T x - y), which covers algorithms such as gradient descent, linear regression, and ridge regression. In the prompt-programmable mode, it proves a stronger universality result: a single fixed-weight two-layer softmax attention module can emulate all algorithms from the task-specific class using only prompt changes. The significance is that this formalizes multi-algorithm programmability under frozen weights. The limitation is also clear: this is about constructive emulation under idealized conditions, not about whether large pretrained transformers spontaneously learn these algorithms robustly in practice.","experiments":"The abstract does not report standard empirical benchmarks, and the paper appears to be primarily theoretical. The main results are the expressivity constructions themselves: single-head attention for a broad task-specific class, and prompt-programmable universality with a fixed two-layer module. The practical questions are about the overhead of those constructions—context length, precision requirements, attention sharpness, token encoding, and approximation error as problem size grows. Those details determine how much explanatory power the theorems have for real ICL behavior.","authors_note":""},"tags":["icl","transformer-theory","algorithm-emulation"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011066","title":"How Do Transformers Learn to Associate Tokens: Gradient Leading Terms Bring Mechanistic Interpretability","authors":["Shawn Im","Changdae Oh","Zhen Fang","Yixuan Li"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$254","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011066"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011066","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文研究的是 Transformer 在训练早期如何从自然语言数据里学到 token 间的语义关联，比如 'bird' 和 'flew' 为什么会互相支持。过去机制解释工作常看训练后静态权重或激活，而较少从训练动力学出发，去问这些关联最初是如何被梯度写进去的。作者希望用一个可解析的近似，把‘关联形成’从现象描述推进到可计算机制。","importance":"结论是：这是一篇有价值的 mechanistic interpretability / training dynamics 工作，重点不在新模型，而在把早期学习过程写成闭式近似。对 pretrain 研究的启示是，很多我们在后期看到的语义结构，可能在训练早期就由梯度主导项决定了方向；这有助于理解数据共现、优化路径和最终泛化之间的关系。它的重要性偏分析工具层面，适合帮助研究者建立更精细的训练期解释，而不是直接改变训练 recipe。","technical_approach":"核心方法是基于梯度 leading-term approximation 推导训练早期权重的闭式表达，并用它解释语义关联如何在 attention-based LM 中出现。关键选择是只抓住梯度的主导项，而不是试图精确刻画全训练过程；这样牺牲了后期精度，但换来了可解释解析式。这个思路有效，因为训练最初阶段参数仍接近初始化，非线性累积效应较弱，主导项往往能揭示最先被放大的统计结构。论文的价值在于把某些关联模式与数据统计、位置结构和注意力参数更新方向直接对应起来，从而解释“哪些 token 对会先连起来，为什么是这样而不是别的”。边界也很清楚：一旦进入深度表征重组阶段，leading-term 近似未必还能覆盖复杂层间耦合。","experiments":"摘要没有提供模型规模和数值 benchmark，因此无法给出常规实验总结。可以确认的关键事实是：作者不仅做理论推导，还声称这些闭式表达能够解释训练早期语义关联的形成。真正值得看的实验应该是：理论预测的关联强度是否和真实训练轨迹一致；在哪些层、哪些头、哪些数据统计条件下近似最准确；以及近似在训练进行到多晚之前仍然有效。如果这些验证做得扎实，这篇工作会成为连接训练动力学与机制解释的有用桥梁。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper studies how transformers begin to learn semantic associations between tokens during training, such as why 'bird' and 'flew' become linked. Much of mechanistic interpretability focuses on trained models and static circuits, while this work asks a more dynamic question: how are these associations first written into the model by gradient updates? The goal is to move from descriptive observations to an analyzable training-time mechanism.","importance":"This is a useful mechanistic-interpretability and training-dynamics contribution. Its significance lies in giving a closed-form account of early learning rather than proposing a new model. For pretraining research, the main insight is that many semantic structures visible later may be strongly shaped by leading-order gradient effects very early in training. That helps connect corpus statistics, optimization trajectory, and eventual generalization.","technical_approach":"The method derives closed-form expressions for early-stage weights using a leading-term approximation of the gradients in attention-based language models. The authors deliberately model only the dominant gradient terms instead of the full nonlinear trajectory. That is a reasonable trade-off: it sacrifices late-stage fidelity but gains analytical clarity. The approach is likely effective precisely because early in training the parameters remain close to initialization, so dominant terms can expose which statistical associations are amplified first. The value of the analysis is in tying token associations to data co-occurrence patterns, positional structure, and attention parameter updates in a direct way.","experiments":"The abstract provides no model scales or benchmark numbers, so there is no standard quantitative summary to report. What matters here is whether the closed-form predictions match actual training trajectories: which token associations emerge first, in which layers or heads, and over what portion of training the approximation remains accurate. If those validations are strong, the paper could become a useful bridge between optimization dynamics and mechanistic interpretability.","authors_note":""},"tags":["mechanistic-interpretability","gradients","transformer","training-dynamics","associations"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011070","title":"Superficial Safety Alignment Hypothesis","authors":["Jianwei Li","Jung-Eun Kim"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$255","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011070"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011070","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文提出并检验一个很强的假设：安全对齐可能并没有深度改造模型能力，而主要是在模型内部建立了一个‘该满足还是该拒绝’的浅层决策边界。过去很多对齐研究把 safety alignment 和 general instruction tuning 混在一起讨论，容易高估安全训练的机制深度。作者的问题是，安全护栏是否其实只依赖少量关键组件，因此既容易生效，也容易脆弱。","importance":"结论是：如果论文证据扎实，这会是一篇对安全对齐机制很有冲击力的解释性工作。它的重要性不在于提出更强防护，而在于把 safety alignment 描述成一种近似二分类任务，并声称只需少量关键属性就能建立 guardrail。对 pretrain/posttrain 闭环的启示很直接：如果安全行为主要是浅层叠加，而不是深层重塑，那么当前很多安全 SFT/RL 结果的稳健性可能被高估，迁移性和抗越狱能力也会天然受限。","technical_approach":"核心方法是提出 Superficial Safety Alignment Hypothesis（SSAH），并据此去识别模型中支撑安全拒答的少量关键组件。摘要提到作者识别出了四类 attributes，但给出的文本被截断，无法确认具体是什么。方法思路看起来是：把安全对齐理解为隐式 binary classification，即在用户请求到来时决定进入 fulfill 还是 refuse 的推理方向；然后分析哪些局部组件足以触发这一决策。这样的 framing 有解释力，因为很多安全失败案例确实像分类边界被绕过，而不是模型完全不懂危害。它的风险在于，安全问题并不总是二元且表浅，复杂情境中的价值冲突、上下文依赖和长期规划可能超出这一假设。","experiments":"摘要只确认了两个关键点：一是作者提出 SSAH；二是他们声称找到了四类建立安全护栏的关键属性/组件。没有模型、数据、评测集和数字结果，因此不能定量总结。真正值得看的实验应该包括：移除或操控这些组件后，安全拒答率和有用性如何变化；这些组件是否跨模型、跨训练阶段稳定存在；以及 jailbreak、重写提示和多轮上下文下，这种‘浅层安全边界’是否仍成立。如果这些结果强，这篇论文会帮助大家更现实地看待 safety post-training 的能力边界。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper puts forward a strong hypothesis: safety alignment may not deeply reshape a model, but instead may mostly install a shallow decision boundary that selects between compliance and refusal. Prior alignment work often treats safety tuning and general instruction tuning together, which can obscure how brittle safety mechanisms really are. The authors ask whether safety guardrails depend on only a small set of essential components.","importance":"If the evidence is solid, this is a potentially important mechanistic account of safety alignment. Its significance is not in building a better defense, but in reframing safety alignment as something close to an implicit binary classification problem. The implication for the pretrain/posttrain loop is sharp: if safety behavior is largely a superficial overlay rather than a deep capability rewrite, then current safety SFT/RL may be less robust and less transferable than many practitioners assume.","technical_approach":"The paper proposes the Superficial Safety Alignment Hypothesis and uses it to identify a small number of model attributes or components that establish safety guardrails. The abstract says four types are identified, but the provided text is truncated and does not specify them. The framing is that safety alignment teaches the model to choose a reasoning direction—fulfill or refuse—when facing a user request, effectively an implicit binary classification task. This perspective is plausible because many safety failures look like boundary-crossing errors rather than complete ignorance. The limitation is that not all safety problems are shallow or binary; richer contextual trade-offs and long-horizon risks may fall outside this hypothesis.","experiments":"The abstract confirms the hypothesis and the claimed identification of four key attribute types, but gives no model scales, datasets, evaluation suites, or numeric results. The most informative experiments would test what happens when these components are removed or manipulated, whether they transfer across models and alignment stages, and whether the superficial-boundary view still holds under jailbreak prompts, paraphrases, and multi-turn contexts. If those checks are strong, the paper would materially sharpen how we think about the limits of safety post-training.","authors_note":""},"tags":["alignment","safety","sft","robustness","generalization"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011102","title":"Token-Based Audio Inpainting via Discrete Diffusion","authors":["Tali Dror","Iftach Shoham","Moshe Buchris","Oren Gal","Haim Permuter","Gilad Katz","Eliya Nachmani"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Audio inpainting seeks to restore missing segments in degraded recordings. Previous diffusion-based methods exhibit impaired performance when the missing region is large. We introduce the first approach that applies discrete diffusion over tokenized music representations from a pre-trained audio tokenizer, enabling stable and semantically coherent restoration of long gaps. Our method further incorporates two training approaches: a derivative-based regularization loss that enforces smooth temporal dynamics, and a span-based absorbing transition that provides structured corruption during diffusion. Experiments on the MusicNet and MAESTRO datasets with gaps up to 750ms show that our approach consistently outperforms strong baselines across range of gap lengths, for gaps of 150ms and above. This work advances musical audio restoration and introduces new directions for discrete diffusion model training. Visit our project page for examples and code. token，却不一定学到“对下游最有用”的压缩表示。现有方法大多依赖 autoencoding 任务训练专门的压缩 token，但作者认为这种目标把容量花在恢复原文细节上，和真实推理、检索、问答所需的语义保真并不完全一致。","importance":"它的重要性在于给 context compression 提了一个很实际的方向修正：不要默认 autoencoding 就是正确训练信号。对 pretrain 和 serving 交界面的启示很直接——如果压缩表示最终是给生成式 LLM继续消费的，那么训练目标应该更贴近“保留对续写/回答有用的信息”，而不是“最大化输入可逆性”。这属于方法级贡献，但如果结果足够稳定，对长上下文系统会有明显实用价值。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 Semantic-Anchor Compression（SAC），一种不依赖 autoencoding 监督的上下文压缩架构。摘要表述的关键点是：作者不是训练一组专门 token 去承担压缩能力，而是通过 contextual semantic anchors 让架构先天具备抽取和承载语义锚点的能力。这个设计背后的判断是，压缩 token 如果通过重建目标学习，往往被迫编码大量局部细节；而语义锚点更接近下游任务真正需要的全局关系、实体状态和关键证据。和典型 autoencoder-style compressor 相比，它舍弃了可逆压缩的强约束，换来面向任务的信息选择性。","experiments":"摘要没有给出模型参数、压缩率、延迟收益、下游任务列表或具体分数，因此不能报告数值。按题目和方法定位，最关键的实验应该包括：在相同压缩率下与 autoencoding-based context compression 比较问答/推理性能；在相近任务精度下比较 token 节省和推理加速；以及分析 anchor 数量或位置对性能的影响。若论文做得扎实，还会检查 SAC 是否更稳健于 domain shift，因为语义锚点方法通常比重建目标更少过拟合表面形式。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper questions a common assumption in context compression: learning to reconstruct the original context is not necessarily the right way to learn a compressed representation for downstream LLM use. Autoencoding-based compression tokens may preserve recoverability, but that objective can conflict with what real tasks actually need.","importance":"This is a useful correction for the long-context literature. The practical lesson for the pretrain-serving interface is that a compressed context should be optimized for usefulness to the decoder, not for faithful inversion of the input. That makes the work methodologically relevant even if it does not introduce a new model family.","technical_approach":"The proposed method, Semantic-Anchor Compression (SAC), removes the usual autoencoding training route and instead builds compression around contextual semantic anchors. The key design choice is to make semantic extraction an architectural prior rather than something compression tokens must discover through reconstruction losses. The trade-off is explicit: SAC gives up strong reversibility constraints in exchange for representations that are more selective about entities, relations, and evidence likely to matter downstream.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide model sizes, compression ratios, latency numbers, baselines, or exact gains, so the quantitative summary must stay conservative. The decisive experiments should compare SAC against autoencoding-based compressors at matched compression rates, measure downstream QA/reasoning quality, and report the speed-accuracy trade-off. Useful ablations would include the number and placement of semantic anchors, and robustness under distribution preference data 越训越不匹配。现有做法要么依赖静态启发式重加权，要么把 online sampling 和 preference optimization 分开做，结果是采样时机和采样强度都不能跟随模型当前最需要的对齐信号。","importance":"它的重要性在于把 data generation 和 preference optimization 重新耦合起来。对 pretrain↔posttrain 接口的启示很强：对齐训练不只是换个 loss，而是训练信号分布本身必须动态闭环更新；如果 MetaAPO 的收益稳定，这对理解 offline RLHF / DPO 为什么后期失效、何时该引入在线数据，有直接方法论价值。这属于方法级贡献，但很贴近真实大模型对齐流水线。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 Meta-Weighted Adaptive Preference Optimization（MetaAPO），用一个轻量 meta-learner 估计“当前做 on-policy 采样值不值得”。这个 meta-learner 扮演 alignment gap estimator：它衡量线上生成样本相对离线偏好数据的潜在收益，并据此引导 targeted online generation，而不是固定比例地混入在线数据。这个设计的好处是把在线数据获取从一个昂贵但盲目的过程，变成按学习阶段自适应分配预算；如果不这样做，模型可能在早期过度依赖高噪声在线样本，或在后期继续被陈旧离线数据主导。和已有 decoupled online preference optimization 的区别，在于采样策略本身被元学习地调节，而不是单独拍脑袋设定。","experiments":"摘要没有给出具体模型规模、偏好数据集、比较方法和提升数字，所以不能写准确幅度。可以确定的是，关键对比应包括纯离线偏好优化、固定配比的 offline+online 混合方案，以及可能的在线 DPO/IPO 类方法；核心指标多半是 win rate、reward model score 或 benchmark 平均分。最有价值的 ablation 会是：meta-learner 去掉后是否退化为普通在线采样；以及在不同训练阶段，MetaAPO 是否会自动提高或降低在线数据占比，这能直接验证“动态耦合”的主张。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles the distribution mismatch in preference optimization: offline preference data becomes increasingly stale as the policy changes during training. Existing methods either use static heuristics or decouple online sampling from optimization, which makes data collection poorly aligned with the model’s current learning needs.","importance":"This matters because it treats alignment as a closed-loop data problem, not just a loss-design problem. For the pretrain-to-posttrain interface, the main lesson is that preference optimization quality depends heavily on whether the training data evolves with the policy. If the method holds up, it offers a concrete answer to when offline preference tuning starts to saturate and when online data should be introduced.","technical_approach":"MetaAPO introduces a lightweight meta-learner that estimates the value of on-policy sampling relative to the existing offline data. That estimate is then used to guide targeted online generation, rather than mixing online data with a fixed schedule. The design is sensible: online data is expensive and noisy, so it should be collected when it closes the alignment gap most effectively. The difference from prior online preference methods is that the sampling policy itself is adaptively learned instead of manually specified.","experiments":"The abstract does not include model sizes, datasets, baselines, or exact gains. The expected comparisons are against offline-only preference optimization, static offline-online mixtures, and likely prior online preference methods. The most informative ablations would test whether removing the meta-learner reduces the method to ordinary online sampling, and whether the learned online sampling rate changes meaningfully over Yang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$25d","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011207"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011207","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：2:4 半结构化稀疏在硬件上很有吸引力，但现有 one-shot post-training pruning 往往精度掉得太多，导致“能加速但不敢用”。问题核心不是稀疏模式本身，而是直接在原权重上做 2:4 剪枝太刚性，误差无法被有效补偿。","importance":"它的重要性主要是系统与方法结合的部署价值。对大模型研究的启示是：如果目标稀疏模式受硬件约束，提升质量的空间不一定来自更复杂的 importance score，也可以来自重参数化，让可剪的核心矩阵外面包一层低开销纠错结构。这类思路对 post-training compression 很有参考意义，尤其是需要兼顾真实吞吐的场景。","technical_approach":"核心方法 ARMOR 先把每个权重矩阵分解成一个 2:4 sparse core，再在前后各加一个 block-diagonal 的轻量矩阵作为 pre/post transformation。作者的关键判断是：直接剪原矩阵，相当于要求 2:4 模式自己承担全部表达能力；而加上两层低成本 wrapper 后，稀疏核心只需要表示主干结构，wrapper 负责吸收剪枝误差和坐标变换。block-diagonal 的选择也有明确取舍：比全矩阵更便宜、更容易部署，同时比纯标量重缩放更灵活。和传统 2:4 pruning 相比，它本质上是在允许一个受限形式的低成本重参数化。","experiments":"摘要明确它是 one-shot post-training pruning，但没有给出模型规模、稀疏比例以外的压缩设置、基线方法和具体精度/吞吐数字，因此不能写提升幅度。比较对象大概率包括 Wanda、SparseGPT 或其他 2:4 pruning 方法；核心指标应是 perplexity、zero/few-shot accuracy 与实际硬件加速。最值得看的 ablation 是：去掉前后 wrapper 后性能掉多少；block size 怎么影响质量与开销；以及这种方法在不同模型尺度上是否稳定，因为某些误差补偿技巧在小模型有效、在大模型未必稳。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a practical bottleneck in semi-structured sparsity: 2:4 pruning is hardware-friendly, but one-shot post-training methods often degrade model quality too much to be useful. The issue is not 2:4 sparsity itself, but the rigidity of pruning the original weight matrix directly.","importance":"This is mainly a deployment-oriented contribution, but it carries a broader lesson: when sparsity patterns are fixed by hardware, quality recovery may come more from reparameterization than from better pruning scores. That is useful for anyone working on post-training compression under real serving constraints.","technical_approach":"ARMOR factorizes each dense weight matrix into a 2:4 sparse core plus two lightweight block-diagonal wrappers applied before and after it. The wrappers act as error correctors and basis transforms, so the sparse core only needs to represent the main structure. This is the central design insight: instead of forcing a rigid 2:4 matrix to approximate the full operator alone, the method gives it a small amount of adaptive support. Block-diagonal transforms strike the intended trade-off between flexibility and deployment cost.","experiments":"The abstract confirms a one-shot post-training setting but gives no model sizes, baseline names, or numeric quality/speed results. The most relevant comparisons are likely against standard 2:4 pruning methods, with evaluation on perplexity, downstream accuracy, and actual hardware throughput. Existing delimiter tokens mark image boundaries, but in practice they are not strong enough to enforce separation.","importance":"The contribution is small in form but potentially broad in impact. For multimodal pretraining, it suggests that some multi-image failures are not due to weak visual representations but to weak boundary signals inside the shared sequence. If that is true, a very cheap intervention can recover a surprising amount of multi-image capability.","technical_approach":"The method is delimiter token scaling: amplify the hidden states of image boundary tokens so they more strongly shape attention flow. The mechanism is plausible and simple. In a transformer, boundary markers only matter if they are salient enough to affect token interactions; if they are numerically weak, they become symbolic placeholders rather than functional separators. The method keeps the standard delimiter design and strengthens its effect instead of adding extra routing modules or specialized losses.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide model names, datasets, or gains, so no quantitative claim should be made. The expected evidence includes analysis showing weak delimiter effectiveness in baseline models, gains on multi-image understanding tasks, and checks that single-image performance is preserved. An important boundary-case ablation would test whether overly strong delimiter scaling hurts tasks that require cross-image comparison rather than separation.","authors_note":""},"tags":["vlm","multi-image","tokenizer","attention"],"notable_authors":["Minyoung Lee","Yeji Park","Dongjun Hwang","Yejin Kim","Seong Joon Oh"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011318","title":"FlowBind: Efficient Any-to-Any Generation with Bidirectional Flows","authors":["Yeonwoo Cha","Semin Kim","Jinhyeon Kwon","Seunghoon Hong"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$25f","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011318"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011318","category_id":"multimodal_gen","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["multimodal-generation","flow-matching","cross-modal","any-to-any"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011334","title":"Seeing What’s Wrong: A Trajectory-Guided Approach to Caption Error Detection","authors":["Gabriel Afriat","Ryan Lucas","Xiang Meng","Yufang Hou","Yada Zhu","Rahul Mazumder"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$260","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011334"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011334","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["data-quality","captioning","filtering","multimodal"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011336","title":"Emotions Where Art Thou: Understanding and Characterizing the Emotional Latent Space of Large Language Models","authors":["Benjamin Reichman","Adar Avsian","Larry Heck"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"This work investigates how large language models (LLMs) internally represent emotion by analyzing the geometry of their hidden-state space. Using a synthetic dataset of emotionally rewritten sentences, we identify a low-dimensional emotional manifold via singular value decomposition and show that emotional representations are directionally encoded, distributed across layers, and aligned with interpretable dimensions. These structures are stable across depth and generalize to eight real-world emotion datasets spanning five languages. Cross-domain alignment yields low error and strong linear probe performance, indicating a universal emotional subspace. Within this space, internal emotion perception can be steered while preserving semantics using a learned intervention module, with especially strong control for basic emotions across languages. These findings reveal a consistent and manipulable affective geometry in LLMs and offer insight into how they internalize and process emotion. Bayesian: Dynamics of SGD Through Singular Learning Theory","authors":["Max Hennick","Stijn De Baerdemacker"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"The nature of the relationship between Bayesian sampling and stochastic gradient descent in neural networks has been a long-standing open question in the theory of deep learning. We shed light on this question by modeling the long runtime behaviour of SGD as diffusion on porous media. Using singular learning theory, we show that the late stage dynamics are strongly impacted by the degeneracies of the loss surface. From this we are able to show that under reasonable choices of hyperparameters for vanilla SGD, the local steady state distribution of SGD (if it exists) is effectively a tempered version of the Bayesian posterior over the weights which accounts for local accessibility constraints. Althoff"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$270","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011580"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011580","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{},"summary_en":{},"tags":["reasoning","benchmark","knowledge","evaluation"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011610","title":"A Simple \"Motivation\" Can Enhance Reinforcement Finetuning of Large Reasoning Models","authors":["Junjie Zhang","Guozheng Ma","Shunyu Liu","Haoyu Wang","Jiaxing Huang","Ting-En Lin","Fei Huang","Yongbin Li","Dacheng Tao"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$271","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011610"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011610","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：RLVR 虽然能把大模型往可验证推理任务上推得更强，但训练过程仍然过于依赖盲目试错，模型只能从稀疏、碎片化的 reward 中慢慢摸索。以往做法通常默认 reward function 只在环境里起作用，不显式告诉模型“什么算好”；作者认为既然 reward 可验证、可自然语言描述，就应该把这部分先验直接暴露给模型，减少无效探索。","importance":"这项工作的意义主要在 pretrain↔posttrain 接口上。它不是发明新的 RL 算法，而是指出一个经常被忽略的训练信号来源：奖励规则的文本化描述本身可以成为学习条件。这个视角重要，因为它把 RL 从“只靠标量反馈”改成“标量反馈 + 语言条件”的混合训练信号，若结论成立，会直接影响 reasoning model 的后训练配方、数据构造和 prompt-conditioned policy learning。","technical_approach":"核心方法很简单：在 RL finetuning 过程中给模型加入 task motivation，也就是用自然语言显式描述 reward function，让模型在采样和更新时都知道“优化目标长什么样”。它更像对现有 RLVR 的条件化增强，而不是替换 PPO/GRPO 之类优化器。关键设计点在于，作者利用的是 LLM 已经具备的 in-context 规则理解能力，把 reward pattern 从隐式统计信号改成显式任务说明；如果不这么做，模型只能靠大量 rollouts 去近似猜测哪些行为会得高分。它的边界也很明确：只有在 reward 可清晰语言化、且文本描述不会引入歧义或被策略投机利用时，这种 motivation 才稳定有效。","experiments":"摘要没有提供参数量、任务数和具体分数，因此无法复述定量结果。能确认的是，作者比较了带 motivation 与标准 RLVR 的强化微调效果，结论是前者更高效、更能帮助大 reasoning model 学习。实验上最值得看的应是两类证据：一类是样本效率是否明显提升，比如达到同等准确率所需 rollout 或 update 更少；另一类是 motivation 到底是在帮助探索，还是只是相当于额外 instruction tuning。若有 ablation，最关键的是 reward 描述质量、详略程度以及是否对不同任务可复用。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles an inefficiency in RL with verifiable rewards: current reasoning-model finetuning still learns largely by blind trial and error, even though the reward rule is often explicit enough to be described in natural language. Existing RLVR pipelines usually leave that information implicit, forcing the model to infer the reward pattern only through many rollouts and sparse scalar feedback.","importance":"This is most relevant at the pretrain–posttrain interface. The contribution is not a new RL optimizer, but a change in how the training signal is packaged: the reward function itself becomes language conditioning. That matters because it suggests a hybrid regime where models learn from both scalar rewards and explicit textual task criteria, which could alter how we design reasoning finetuning recipes, synthetic data, and policy conditioning.","technical_approach":"The method is straightforward: during reinforcement finetuning, the model is given a natural-language 'motivation' that describes the reward function, so it knows what good behavior looks like before discovering it only through reward. This is a conditioning layer on top of standard RLVR rather than a replacement for the underlying optimizer. The design leans on the model’s existing ability to interpret rules in context. Without such explicit motivation, the policy has to approximate the reward landscape through many noisy samples. The main limitation is also clear: this helps only when the reward can be described cleanly and when the textual description does not introduce loopholes or encourage reward hacking.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide model sizes, task counts, or exact gains, so the safe summary is qualitative. The claimed result is that adding motivation improves reinforcement finetuning efficiency and task performance for large reasoning models over standard RLVR. The most important evidence to inspect would be sample efficiency, such as fewer rollouts or updates to reach the same performance, and ablations that separate the benefit of reward description from plain instruction tuning or prompt engineering.","authors_note":""},"tags":["rlvr","reasoning","motivation-prompting","rl"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011614","title":"The Illusion of Diminishing Returns: Measuring Long Horizon Execution in LLMs","authors":["Akshit Sinha","Arvindh Arun","Shashwat Goel","Steffen Staab","Jonas Geiping"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$272","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011614"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011614","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：很多人从短任务 benchmark 上看到性能增益变小，就认为 LLM scaling 已经接近收益递减，但这个判断可能被测量方式误导了。作者要回答的不是“模型会不会推理”，而是“模型能连续无错执行多长的长程任务”；因为哪怕单步准确率只提高一点，复合到长 horizon 上也可能带来指数级差异。","importance":"这项工作更偏评测范式和能力分解。它的重要性在于重新定义了如何看待 scaling 收益：如果长程执行长度对 per-step error 非线性敏感，那么短 benchmark 上的小幅进步可能在真实 agentic 或长流程任务中被极大放大。对 pretrain 和 posttrain 都有启示，因为它提醒我们不要只盯短答案正确率，而要显式区分 reasoning failure 和 execution failure，并重新设计训练与评测信号。","technical_approach":"核心做法是把“长任务失败”拆解成 execution 能力问题，并通过显式提供知识和计划来尽量排除 reasoning 缺失的干扰。也就是说，作者不给模型考‘会不会想’，而是更聚焦‘能不能按既定方案连续做对很多步’。在这个设置下，他们分析随着 horizon 增长，模型的 per-step accuracy 如何衰减，以及这如何决定最终可成功执行的任务长度。与常规端到端 benchmark 相比，这种设计的优点是能力分解更清楚；代价是它测到的是受控执行上限，而不是开放世界完整智能，因此不能直接替代标准综合评测。","experiments":"摘要没有给出具体模型名、参数量和数字，但提供了两条很关键的经验事实。第一，更大的模型即使在单步任务上只略强，也能正确执行显著更多轮次。第二，随着任务变长，per-step accuracy 会下降，而这种下降主导了长程失败。实验上最有价值的量化应是可成功执行的最大步数、per-step error 随 horizon 的曲线，以及不同规模模型在近乎相同单步准确率下的长程差距。如果这些曲线确实呈现指数放大效应，那么“收益递减”更多是 benchmark 设计的问题，而不是 scaling 真没用。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper argues that apparent diminishing returns in LLM scaling can be a measurement artifact. Short-horizon benchmarks may hide the fact that small improvements in single-step accuracy can compound into very large gains in the length of multi-step tasks a model can execute without failure. The core question is not whether a model can reason in isolation, but how long it can keep executing correctly.","importance":"This is mainly an evaluation and capability-decomposition contribution. Its importance is that it reframes how scaling progress should be measured, especially for agentic and long-horizon settings. If long-run success is highly sensitive to per-step error, then modest gains on standard short benchmarks may correspond to much larger practical gains than they appear to. That has clear implications for both training objectives and benchmark design.","technical_approach":"The paper isolates execution from reasoning by explicitly supplying the knowledge and plan needed for a long-horizon task, so failures are less likely to come from not knowing what to do and more likely to come from not carrying it out consistently. Under this setup, it studies how per-step accuracy degrades with horizon length and how that degradation determines the maximum executable task length. Compared with standard end-to-end benchmarks, this gives a cleaner decomposition of capability. The trade-off is that it measures controlled execution capacity rather than full open-ended problem solving.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide model names, parameter counts, or exact metrics, but it makes two important empirical claims. First, larger models can execute substantially more turns correctly even when smaller models already have near-perfect single-turn accuracy. Second, per-step accuracy declines as tasks get longer, and this decline is a key driver of long-horizon failure. The most informative plots would be success length versus model scale, and per-step error as a function of horizon. If those curves show strong compounding, then the appearance of diminishing returns is at least partly due to benchmark choice rather than actual saturation.","authors_note":""},"tags":["scaling","benchmark","long-horizon","evaluation"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011622","title":"RL makes MLLMs see better than SFT","authors":["Junha Song","Sangdoo Yun","Dongyoon Han","Jaegul Choo","Byeongho Heo"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$273","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011622"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011622","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：MLLM 社区通常把性能主要归因于 LLM backbone，而较少分析 vision encoder 到底学到了什么；随着训练范式从 SFT 转向 RL，这个盲点更明显了。作者关注的核心问题是：RL 不只是让多模态模型“回答得更对”，它是否真的改变了模型“看图”的方式，尤其是 vision encoder 的表征质量。","importance":"这项工作对 VLM/MLLM 预训练和后训练接口都很重要。它的重要性在于把讨论焦点从文本侧策略学习，重新拉回到视觉表征本身，并提出一个很强的判断：RL 在强视觉相关 VQA 上优于 SFT，且这种优势可能来自 vision encoder 被重新塑形，而不只是 language head 更会猜答案。若这个结论站得住，会直接影响多模态后训练的目标设计，以及我们是否需要把 RL 看成一种跨模态表征学习手段，而不只是决策优化手段。","technical_approach":"核心做法是对比 SFT 和 RL 训练后的 MLLM，并深入分析 vision encoder 和整体模型的变化。方法上的重点不是提出一个新模型，而是做训练策略影响的归因分析：RL 相比 SFT，到底改善了哪些视觉能力、这些改善是否集中在高视觉依赖任务、以及这种变化如何体现在 encoder 表征上。和很多只看最终 benchmark 的工作不同，这篇把视角落到“perception 被如何改写”。设计上的意义在于，如果 RL 确实让视觉表征更判别、更对齐任务信号，那么 RL 就不只是后端 policy optimization。边界在于，这类结论容易受 reward 设计、数据构成和基座模型初始化影响，需要小心区分是 encoder 真变强，还是系统学会了更有效利用已有视觉特征。","experiments":"摘要给出的最关键事实是：在 strongly vision-related 的 VQA benchmark 上，RL 明显优于 SFT。除此之外，没有提供模型规模和具体提升数字。实验上最值得关注的是三类证据：第一，RL 增益是否集中在真正依赖细粒度视觉辨识的任务，而不是语言先验重的题；第二，vision encoder 的表征分析是否显示出更好的类别分离、注意区域聚焦或图文对齐；第三，把 RL 后的 vision encoder 冻结/替换后，性能变化能否支持因果判断。若这些证据充分，这篇分析会比单纯“RL 分数更高”更有价值。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses an overlooked question in multimodal LMs: performance is often attributed mainly to the LLM backbone, while the role of the vision encoder is underanalyzed. As training shifts from SFT to RL, the gap becomes more important. The core question here is whether RL actually changes how an MLLM perceives images, rather than merely improving answer selection on top of fixed visual features.","importance":"This is important for the interface between multimodal pretraining and post-training. The main contribution is to re-center the analysis on the vision encoder and to argue that RL’s gains on strongly vision-dependent VQA tasks may come from reshaping visual representations, not just from better language-side policy learning. If true, that would broaden the role of RL in MLLMs from decision optimization to cross-modal representation refinement.","technical_approach":"The paper compares MLLMs trained with SFT versus RL and analyzes how both the full model and the vision encoder change. The emphasis is not on a new architecture, but on attribution: which capabilities improve under RL, whether those gains concentrate on visually demanding tasks, and how they show up in the encoder’s representations. Unlike papers that only report final benchmark numbers, this one asks how perception itself is modified by the training objective. The key caveat is causal interpretation: observed changes may reflect better use of existing features rather than a genuinely stronger encoder, so the analysis has to disentangle those possibilities carefully.","experiments":"The main fact stated in the abstract is that RL has a clear advantage over SFT on strongly vision-related VQA benchmarks. No model sizes or exact gains are given. The most informative evidence would be whether the gains are concentrated on tasks with real visual dependence rather than language priors, whether representation analyses show improved visual discrimination or alignment, and whether freezing or swapping the post-RL vision encoder supports a causal role for encoder How verifier imperfection affects test-time scaling","authors":["Florian Eddie Dorner","Yatong Chen","André F. Cruz","Fanny Yang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Test-time scaling aims to improve language model performance by leveraging additional compute during inference.  Many works have empirically studied techniques such as Best-of-N (BoN) and Rejection Sampling (RS) that make use of a verifier to enable test-time scaling. However, to date there is little theoretical understanding of how verifier imperfection affects performance — a gap we address in this work. Specifically, we prove that the instance-level accuracy of these methods is precisely characterized by the geometry of the verifier’s ROC curve. Our theory has two important takeaways, confirmed by experiments with Qwen and LLama models on GSM8K and MATH500. First, RS outperforms BoN for fixed compute, while both methods converge to the same accuracy in the infinite-compute limit. Second, it is generally impossible to predict the high-compute performance of either method based on observations in the low-compute regime. Liu","yingya zhang","Yu Liu","Hongming Shan"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$27a","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011788"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011788","category_id":"multimodal_gen","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：MoE 在 LLM 上扩容很成功，但搬到 Diffusion Transformer 上收益一直不理想，原因不是 MoE 机制本身失效，而是视觉 token 的统计结构和语言 token 不一样。语言 token 语义稠密、离散差异大，天然利于 router 把不同 token 分给不同专家；而视觉 token 有强空间冗余，同一张图里大量局部块相似，同时不同 token 往往承担位置、纹理、语义等混合功能，导致 router 学不出清晰分工，专家很难真正 specialize。作者要解决的就是这个“视觉 MoE 路由不成型”的核心瓶颈。","importance":"这项工作的价值在于，它把 DiT-MoE 效果不佳的原因指向 routing，而不是简单归咎于训练不稳或数据不够大。对预训练研究这是方法级但带范式启发的工作：MoE 是否有效，不只取决于参数和负载均衡损失，还取决于 token 统计是否支持可分的专家功能。这个判断对视觉、视频、音频甚至多模态统一 MoE 都有启发。如果 ProMoE 的收益稳健，它说明跨模态扩 MoE 不能照搬 LLM 路由设计，需要显式诱导专家分工。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 ProMoE，一个带显式 routing guidance 的两阶段 router。关键思想不是让 router 直接端到端学专家分配，而是先用指导信号鼓励它进行更可解释的粗分组，再在组内做更细的专家选择，从而让专家 specialization 更容易形成。摘要里明确说 guidance 会先引导 router 去 partition 视觉 token，再做后续路由；这说明作者在设计上承认视觉 token 天生不够“自发可分”，因此需要额外偏置来打破空间冗余带来的塌缩。与已有 DiT-MoE 方法相比，区别不在是否采用 top-k、auxiliary loss 或 load balancing，而在是否显式把“专家分工形成”当成训练目标的一部分。代价是路由器设计更复杂，也可能对 guidance 形式和视觉结构先验敏感。","experiments":"从摘要可确定的结论是：作者认为 ProMoE 在扩展 Diffusion Transformers 时比已有 MoE 尝试更有效，核心收益来自更好的专家 specialization，而不是单纯加大参数量。摘要没有给出具体模型尺寸、专家数、训练数据、FID 或采样速度数字，因此无法确认提升幅度。值得重点看正文的实验包括：DiT dense vs naïve MoE vs ProMoE 的质量-算力曲线；路由可视化或 expert usage entropy，用来证明确实学到了更清晰的专家分工；以及不同视觉 token 粒度、不同 guidance 形式下的 ablation。如果作者还能展示在更大规模 DiT 上收益扩大，那会比单点 benchmark 更有说服力，因为这正对应 MoE 的核心价值——更好的 scaling。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a specific failure mode: MoE scales very well in LLMs, but the same recipe has delivered limited gains in Diffusion Transformers. The authors argue that the bottleneck is routing, not the MoE idea itself. Language tokens are semantically sharp and diverse, which naturally supports expert specialization. Visual tokens are far more redundant and functionally mixed across space, so the router struggles to produce clean expert assignments and the experts never specialize enough to justify the added capacity.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution with broader implications for multimodal pretraining. The useful message is that MoE scaling does not transfer across modalities by default because token statistics determine whether routing can create meaningful specialization. That matters for vision, video, audio, and unified multimodal models. If the paper’s results are robust, it strengthens the case that successful MoE scaling in non-text domains requires explicit inductive bias in the router rather than simply reusing LLM-style top-k routing plus load balancing losses.","technical_approach":"ProMoE introduces a two-step router with explicit routing guidance. The main idea is to avoid asking the router to discover expert structure entirely from the end objective. Instead, the router is first encouraged to partition visual tokens in a coarse, more structured way, and only then to perform finer expert selection. This extra guidance is meant to break the collapse caused by spatial redundancy and encourage clearer expert roles. Relative to prior DiT-MoE work, the novelty is not in standard routing mechanics like top-k or auxiliary balancing losses, but in explicitly optimizing for specialization formation. The likely trade-off is additional router complexity and some dependence on how the guidance prior is chosen.","experiments":"The abstract supports one main empirical claim: ProMoE makes MoE scaling more effective for Diffusion Transformers because it improves expert specialization rather than merely increasing parameter count. The abstract does not provide model sizes, number of experts, datasets, FID values, or throughput numbers, so the empirical magnitude is unclear from the provided text. The most informative experiments should compare dense DiT, naive MoE DiT, and ProMoE on quality-versus-compute curves, along with routing diagnostics such as expert usage distributions or visualization of specialization. Scaling results at larger DiT sizes would be especially important, because that is where MoE should matter most if the routing fix is real.","authors_note":""},"tags":["moe","diffusion","routing","dit","scaling"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011797","title":"Understanding vs. Generation: Navigating Optimization Dilemma in Multimodal Models","authors":["Sen Ye","Mengde Xu","Shuyang Gu","Di He","Liwei Wang","Winston Hu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Current research in multimodal models faces a key challenge where enhancing generative capabilities often comes at the expense of understanding, and vice versa. We analyzed this trade-off and identify the primary cause might be the potential conflict between generation and understanding, which creates a competitive dynamic within the model. To address this, we propose the Reason-Reflect-Refine (R3) framework. This innovative algorithm re-frames the single-step generation task into a multi-step process of \"generate-understand-regenerate\". By explicitly leveraging the model's understanding capability during generation, we successfully mitigate the optimization dilemma, achieved stronger generation results and improved understanding ability which are related to the generation process. This offers valuable insights for designing next-generation unified multimodal models. Show more","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011797"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011797","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：多模态模型里“理解”和“生成”常常互相拉扯，生成能力增强后，理解类指标下降；反过来强化理解，开放式生成又变弱。过去很多工作把这看成数据配比或模型容量问题，但作者认为更直接的原因是两类目标在同一参数空间里竞争，尤其生成训练通常是单步直接输出，没显式利用模型已有的理解能力。作者想解决的是，能否把生成过程重构成一个会调用理解能力的多步过程，从而缓和这种优化冲突。","importance":"这项工作的意义在于，它不是在理解和生成之间再做静态权重调参，而是重写了训练/推理接口，让生成显式依赖中间理解反馈。对多模态预训练研究，这更像方法级但带训练范式启发的工作。它提示一个重要判断：理解和生成未必天然冲突，冲突可能来自我们把生成目标写得过于短路，导致模型绕过了可泛化的理解过程。如果这一点成立，对 native multimodal pretraining、统一 loss 设计和 post-train reasoning augmentation 都有直接启发。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 R3（Reason-Reflect-Refine），把原来单步完成的生成任务改成“先生成、再理解、再重生成”的多步流程。它不是简单加一个 chain-of-thought，而是把模型自身的 understanding capability 作为生成的一部分训练信号和决策环节：第一次生成给出候选结果，中间理解/反思阶段识别语义、对齐或视觉细节上的问题，最后 refinement 再根据这些反馈修正输出。这样设计有效的原因是，它让生成路径经过更强的语义检查点，从而减少为了短期 token loss 最优而牺牲理解表征的情况。和最接近的做法相比，区别在于这里强调的是 generation-understanding coupling，而不是单独给生成器加 verifier。代价是训练和推理流程更长，收益可能主要落在那些本来就需要中间语义核对的任务上。","experiments":"摘要明确声称：R3 不仅提升生成结果，还改善与生成相关的理解能力，说明它至少部分缓解了 optimization dilemma。摘要没有给出使用的模型规模、任务类型、数据规模或具体提升数字，因此无法判断收益是普遍存在还是集中在某些 benchmark。最关键的实验应包括：理解-only、generation-only 和 unified baseline 的对照；单步生成 vs R3 多步生成的效果差异；以及在哪些任务上理解能力也随之提升，哪些任务没有收益。如果作者还报告了中间 reflection 质量与最终生成质量的相关性，那会很有说服力，因为论文的核心主张就是‘生成变好是因为真正调用了理解，而不是单纯多算一步’。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper studies a recurring trade-off in multimodal models: improving generation often hurts understanding, and strengthening understanding can weaken generation. Many prior efforts treat this as a data-mixing or scaling issue. The authors instead argue that the two objectives compete inside the shared model, especially when generation is trained as a single-step direct prediction that does not explicitly make use of the model’s understanding capability. The question is whether generation can be restructured so that understanding becomes part of the generation process rather than a separate objective.","importance":"This is a method contribution with broader training-design implications for multimodal pretraining. The useful idea is that the understanding-versus-generation conflict may be partly self-inflicted by overly short-horizon generation objectives. If generation is forced to pass through an intermediate understanding stage, the conflict can be reduced rather than merely reweighted. That has implications for native multimodal pretraining, unified objective design, and post-training schemes that try to improve generation through reasoning or self-reflection.","technical_approach":"R3, or Reason-Reflect-Refine, turns one-shot generation into a multi-step process of generate, understand, and regenerate. The design is more than adding extra decoding tokens. The model first proposes an output, then uses an explicit understanding or reflection stage to inspect semantic alignment and likely errors, and finally refines the output based on that feedback. The rationale is straightforward: forcing generation to route through an internal understanding checkpoint should reduce the tendency to optimize only for immediate token-level output quality at the expense of deeper multimodal grounding. Compared with nearby approaches, the emphasis is on coupling generation with understanding inside one loop, not merely attaching a verifier after the fact. The trade-off is extra training and inference complexity, and the benefits may be strongest on tasks where intermediate semantic checking matters.","experiments":"The abstract makes a clear empirical claim: R3 improves generation quality while also improving understanding abilities that are related to generation, suggesting that it alleviates the optimization dilemma rather than just shifting it. But the abstract does not provide model scales, tasks, datasets, or exact gains, so the breadth of the effect is unclear. The most important experiments should compare understanding-only, generation-only, and unified baselines, quantify the gains of one-shot generation versus R3, and identify which understanding tasks improve and which do not. Evidence that intermediate reflection quality correlates with final output quality would be especially convincing, because the paper’s central claim is that better generation comes from explicitly using understanding rather than simply adding more steps.","authors_note":""},"tags":["multimodal","understanding","generation","optimization","joint-training"],"notable_authors":["Di He","Liwei Wang"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011798","title":"Achieving Olympia-Level Geometry Large Language Model Agent via Complexity Boosting Reinforcement Learning","authors":["Haiteng Zhao","Junhao Shen","Yiming Olympia-level。摘要没有给出具体评测集、通过率、与 AlphaGeometry 2 的差距、训练轮数或模型参数量，因此无法确认‘Olympia-level’究竟是指接近 IMO medal 线还是某个定制基准。最值得关注的实验包括：在标准几何竞赛数据集上的 solve rate，与 AlphaGeometry 2、通用数学 LLM agent、以及无 RL / and intermediate propositions. Pure language-model heuristics are still weak there, which is why state-of-the-art geometry systems such as AlphaGeometry rely heavily on large-scale data synthesis and search. The authors ask whether reinforcement learning can teach this constructive heuristic loop more directly.","importance":"This is not a pretraining advance in the narrow sense, but it is relevant to the pretrain–posttrain interface because geometry offers a clean, highly verifiable environment for studying what RL adds beyond next-token prediction. Instead of asking the model to directly emit a final proof, the agent iteratively proposes propositions and auxiliary constructions, checks them with the symbolic engine, and uses the resulting feedback to guide subsequent reasoning. The RL component gradually increases problem complexity so the agent can learn stronger construction heuristics over time. Compared with systems like AlphaGeometry, the emphasis is on improving the policy through learning rather than relying primarily on handcrafted search and massive synthetic data. The main condition for success is dense enough symbolic feedback and a smooth enough curriculum; otherwise the method still faces sparse rewards and combinatorial search explosion.","experiments":"The abstract makes a strong headline claim: this is the first attempt at a medalist-level geometry LLM agent, and InternGeometry reaches 'Olympia-level' performance. ability.","authors_note":""},"tags":["rl","reasoning","math","agent","curriculum"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011813","title":"Specialization after Generalization: Towards Understanding Test-Time Training in training（TTT）近年在 foundation model 上不断显示收益，但大家对它为什么有效缺少统一解释。早期说法常把收益归因于 OOD 适应或使用了特权测试数据，可这对如今大多数 in-distribution 的 foundation model 场景解释力已经不够。作者提出的核心问题是：在模型已经很大、测试数据也不陌生的情况下，TTT 到底在补什么？他们的答案是，foundation model 仍然在全局上欠参数化，TTT 提供的是 generalization 之后的 specialization，把容量临时聚焦到当前任务相关概念上。","importance":"这项工作的重要性主要在解释框架，而不是新算法。对预训练研究者来说，这很有价值，因为它把 TTT 从‘测试时补丁’重新理解成模型容量分配机制：预训练学到广覆盖表征，但在具体任务局部仍不够专门，TTT 通过少量额外更新完成任务内专精。这个观点会影响我们如何看待 mid-training、continual pretraining、adapter、甚至推理时自适应。如果论文的理论和实验都站得住，它会帮助区分哪些收益来自 distribution shift，哪些来自 specialization 本身。","technical_approach":"核心方法不是提出新的 TTT 算法，而是提出一个 specialization-after-generalization 的理论视角，并在某种 linear representation setting 下分析 TTT 何时有效。摘要表明作者把 foundation model 视作全局表达很强但对单个测试任务局部容量不足，因此测试时继续训练能把参数或表示子空间重定向到当前任务相关方向。这样的解释与旧观点的差别在于：TTT 不必依赖明显 OOD，也不要求 privileged labels，它在 IID 场景里也可能有效，因为重点是任务专化而不是分布纠偏。边界同样明确：如果模型对任务已经足够专化，或测试时可用信号太弱，TTT 的收益就会消失，甚至因过拟合而变差。","experiments":"摘要能确认的事实是：论文同时给出理论分析和经验研究，并挑战了‘TTT 只在 OOD 或 privileged setting 下有用’的传统解释。摘要没有提供具体模型、任务、更新步数、学习率或提升数字，因此无法判断它覆盖的是视觉 foundation model、LLM 还是多模态模型，也无法评估结论外推范围。最值得看的实验包括：IID 与 OOD 场景下 TTT 收益对比；模型规模变化时 TTT 收益是否减弱但不消失；以及任务相关概念越集中时，specialization 假说是否更强。如果作者做了表示层分析，展示测试时更新主要集中到任务相关子空间，那会非常有说服力。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a conceptual gap around test-time training. TTT has repeatedly shown gains in foundation models, yet the standard explanations—out-of-distribution adaptation or access to privileged test-time data—look increasingly incomplete for modern large models operating mostly on in-distribution inputs. The authors ask what TTT is actually doing in that regime. Their answer is that foundation models remain globally underparameterized for specific tasks, and TTT works by specializing a broadly trained model to the local concepts that matter for the current task.","importance":"This is mainly an interpretive contribution, but it is an important one for pretraining research. It reframes TTT from a test-time patch into a capacity-allocation mechanism: pretraining learns broad reusable structure, but not enough task-specific specialization everywhere. Small amounts of test-time optimization can then focus effective capacity where it matters. That perspective matters for how we think about continual pretraining, mid-training, adapters, and adaptive inference, and it helps separate gains due to distribution shift from gains due to specialization itself.","technical_approach":"The main contribution is a specialization-after-generalization view of TTT, supported by theoretical analysis in a linear representation setting. The abstract suggests the authors model foundation models as globally expressive but locally insufficient for particular tasks, so test-time updates can rotate parameters or representations toward task-relevant directions. This differs from older explanations because it does not require strong OOD shift or privileged labels. TTT can help even in IID settings if the task still demands local specialization that the pretrained model has not fully allocated capacity to. The limitation is straightforward: if the base model is already sufficiently specialized, or if the test-time signal is too weak, TTT should offer little benefit and may overfit.","experiments":"The abstract indicates both theory and empirical evidence, with the key claim being that TTT remains useful beyond the usual OOD or privileged-data scenarios. But it gives no model families, tasks, update budgets, or quantitative gains, so the scope is impossible to judge from the provided text alone. The most useful experiments should compare IID versus OOD settings, examine whether TTT gains shrink but persist as model scale grows, and test whether gains correlate with task concentration or concept locality as the specialization hypothesis predicts. Representation-level analysis showing that test-time updates move the model primarily along task-relevant subspaces would be especially compelling.","authors_note":""},"tags":["test-time-training","adaptation","foundation-models","generalization","specialization"],"notable_authors":["Andreas Krause"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011826","title":"Don't Throw Away Your Pretrained Model","authors":["Shangbin Feng","Wenhao Yu","Yike Wang","Hongming Zhang","Yulia Tsvetkov","Dong Yu"],"institutions":["University of Washington"],"abstract":"$27d","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011826"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011826","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"对齐训练（alignment）在提升推理和指令遵循能力的同时，会损害 base model 在创造性、校准性等方面的优势。以往的做法是二选一——要么用 base model 要么用 aligned model，没有好的方式同时利用两者。","importance":"方法级贡献，对 pretrain↔posttrain 接口有直接启示。它揭示了一个被低估的事实：aligned model 并非在所有维度上都优于 base model，pretrain checkpoint 仍有不可替代的价值。Switch Generation 提供了一种不需要重新训练的协作范式。","technical_approach":"提出 Switch Generation：在生成一个 response 的过程中，让 pretrained base model 和 aligned model 交替生成不同片段。核心是训练一个轻量的 switcher LM，它根据当前 query 和上下文决定下一个片段由哪个模型生成。Switcher 的训练数据来自在多样 query 上尝试不同模型生成并评估结果。这不是 ensemble 或 MoE——它是 token 序列级别的模型切换，开销是多一个小模型的推理。","experiments":"摘要未给出具体数字。声称在推理、指令遵循、创造性、校准等多维度上优于单独使用任一模型。需看全文确认 switcher 的规模和切换粒度对效果的影响。","authors_note":"作者包括 Yulia Tsvetkov（华盛顿大学，NLP 领域知名学者）。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Alignment training improves reasoning and instruction following but degrades creativity and calibration where base models excel. Prior approaches force a binary choice between base and aligned models.","importance":"Method-level contribution with direct implications for the pretrain-posttrain interface. Highlights that aligned models are not universally superior—pretrained checkpoints retain unique strengths. Provides a collaboration paradigm without retraining.","technical_approach":"Switch Generation lets a pretrained base model and an aligned model take turns generating segments within a single response. A lightweight switcher LM is trained to decide which model generates the next segment, based on query and context. Training data for the switcher comes from evaluating outcomes of different model choices across diverse queries. The proposed angle is that many real sequences exhibit exploitable geometric structure, so full all-to-all lookup may be unnecessary.","importance":"This sits in the long-context efficiency space and is mainly a systems/algorithm topic. For pretraining, the relevance is clear: reducing attention complexity without major quality loss changes the feasible context length, batch construction, and data packing strategy. That said, the abstract reads more like a broad framing or proposal than a fully specified method with strong evidence.","technical_approach":"From the title and abstract, the central idea is to exploit geometric patterns in token interactions to avoid O(n^2) attention, likely by restricting attention to a structured subset of interactions instead of all pairs. This is related in spirit to sparse, local, hashed, or low-rank attention, but the emphasis on geometry suggests the structure may be driven by spatial patterns in token relationships rather than fixed windows or randomization. Such methods work when dependencies are compressible; they tend to struggle when tasks require dense global alignment. Using a 7B model, we evaluate multilingual SFT, RL for accuracy recovery, and model merging. Across Japanese, French, and Spanish tasks, these approaches markedly increase language-consistent reasoning while preserving strong accuracy, showing that faithful and interpretable multilingual reasoning can be achieved with low-cost alignment. We explore three main questions:  1. To what extent does budget-forcing generalize across different model families and settings? 2. Does it work with non-reasoning models? 3. Can other keywords serve the same function as \"Wait\"?  We present experimental results, including cases where budget forcing does and does not help and offer practical guidance for applying budget-forcing in test-time scaling. Suppressing Hallucinations In LVLM Encoders via Bias and Vulnerability Defense","authors":["Yiyang Huang","Liang Shi","Yitian Zhang","Yi Xu","Yun Fu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$28d","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10006527"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10006527","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文的核心判断是：LVLM 的 object hallucination 不能只归咎于 LLM 解码端，视觉编码器本身的偏置和脆弱性就是重要根因。过去很多方法把修复点放在解码、拒答或外部检索上，因为这些位置更容易改；但如果错误在视觉 token 进入语言模型之前就已经被放大，只修后端只能缓解表象，不能稳定压住幻觉。","importance":"这项工作的贡献是把 hallucination 的责任边界往 encoder 侧推了一步，属于 VLM 预训练和推理接口上的机制性启发。它的重要性不在于提出一个大训练框架，而在于说明视觉表示的统计偏置、内在偏置和对扰动的脆弱性会共同诱发幻觉，因此未来做 native multimodal pretrain 时，encoder 对齐目标和 token 质量控制不能继续被视为次要问题。另一个实际价值是它是 training-free 的，说明不少幻觉可以在不重训模型的情况下被抑制。","technical_approach":"SHIELD 是一个训练时零改动、推理时干预 visual token 的框架。它针对三类问题分别处理：先对视觉 token 重加权，降低高频统计模式对解码的牵引；再引入由噪声构造的 token，去抵消 encoder 中固有的偏置响应；最后结合对抗攻击和 contrastive decoding，暴露并压制那些一受小扰动就会触发的脆弱表征。和已有主要修 LLM head 或语言解码策略的方法相比，它的关键区别是把控制点放在 encoder output 上，假设很多 hallucination 是“看错了再说错”，不是“看对了但瞎编”。","experiments":"摘要给出的结论是 SHIELD 能有效缓解 object hallucination，同时保持较好的任务性能，但未提供具体模型、数据集和数值。可以确认的 baseline 方向应包括只改解码的 hallucination mitigation 方法，以及原始 LVLM；关键评测大概率是 hallucination rate、object precision/recall 与常规 VQA / caption 指标。由于缺少正文，无法可靠写出提升幅度，只能保守地说论文声称在多种设置下有效。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper’s main claim is that object hallucination in LVLMs is not just a decoder-side problem; visual encoders themselves introduce bias and vulnerability that materially drive hallucinated outputs. Prior work mostly intervened on the language side because it is easier to access, but that only treats symptoms if the visual representation is already skewed before it reaches the LLM.","importance":"This is a useful shift in where the field looks for failure causes. For multimodal pretraining, the paper suggests that encoder statistics, representation bias, and robustness are part of the hallucination story, so improving alignment losses or decoder heuristics alone is not sufficient. It is also practically relevant because the method is training-free, showing that some hallucination reduction can be obtained without retraining large LVLMs.","technical_approach":"SHIELD is a training-free inference-time framework that edits or reweights visual tokens. It addresses three identified failure sources separately: token reweighting to reduce statistical bias, noise-derived tokens to counter inherent encoder bias, and adversarial perturbation plus contrastive decoding to expose and suppress vulnerable representations. Compared with prior methods that mainly modify decoding on the LLM side, SHIELD acts earlier in the pipeline and assumes the model often hallucinates because it was nudged into the wrong visual evidence state.","experiments":"The abstract claims that SHIELD substantially mitigates hallucination while preserving performance, but it does not provide trustworthy details on model sizes, datasets, or exact margins. The likely evaluation includes object-hallucination metrics and standard LVLM task performance against both the raw model and decoder-side mitigation baselines. Without the full paper, the safe summary is that the gains are reported across multiple settings but cannot be quantified here.","authors_note":""},"tags":["vlm","hallucination","visual-encoder","alignment","robustness"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10006529","title":"Channel-Aware Mixed-Precision Quantization for Efficient Long-Context Inference","authors":["Chengxi Liao","Zeyi Wen"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$28e","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10006529"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10006529","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文解决的是长上下文推理时 KV cache 内存线性增长，而现有低比特量化在极低 bit 下精度掉得太快的问题。以往方法通常固定地对 key 做 channel-wise、对 value 做 token-wise 量化，这种规则简单但默认所有通道同等重要；论文指出这一假设不成立，不同 KV channel 的量化敏感度差异很大，所以统一 bit 分配会浪费预算并放大误差。","importance":"这是一个系统效率层面的改进，但对长上下文 serving 很实际。它的贡献不是发明新缓存结构，而是把 mixed-precision 做到 KV channel 这一更细粒度上，说明长期被当作工程细节的“哪几个通道更脆弱”其实有稳定结构可利用。对 pretrain 研究的启发在于，推理时的表示各向异性很强，后续如果做量化感知训练、KV 压缩或架构改造，应该直接围绕这种 channel sensitivity 设计。","technical_approach":"ChanMix 的核心是按 channel 分配不同 bit-width，而不是给整层或整个 key/value 张量统一精度。论文先分析 KV 各通道在低比特量化下的敏感度，再在 2-bit 到更高 bit 的混合精度空间里做分配，从而把预算留给最脆弱的通道。和已有 key-channel/value-token 的静态规则相比，它更细粒度，也更贴近误差来源；有效的原因是 KV cache 的信息分布高度不均匀，少数通道承担了更关键的注意力几何或值传递功能。","experiments":"摘要确认的实验结论是：ChanMix 在低比特、尤其长上下文场景下，能比现有 KV cache quantization 方法更好地保持性能。未给出具体模型、上下文长度、内存节省比例和任务指标，因此不能补数字。值得记录的观察是作者明确支持 2-bit 混合配置，这说明方法目标是进入现有方法最容易崩的 regime，而不是只在 4-bit 这种相对安全区间小修小补。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a very practical bottleneck: KV-cache memory grows linearly with context length, and existing low-bit quantization schemes degrade too much at aggressive bit widths. The standard recipe—channel-wise keys and token-wise values—implicitly treats channels as roughly equal, but the authors argue that KV channels have sharply different sensitivity to quantization noise.","importance":"This is primarily a systems contribution, but a useful one for long-context inference. The main lesson is that mixed precision becomes more effective when it follows representation anisotropy rather than fixed tensor conventions. For pretraining researchers, that is a reminder that inference-time activations are not uniformly important; some channels carry disproportionately fragile structure and deserve targeted compression strategies.","technical_approach":"ChanMix assigns precision at the channel level instead of using one bit width per tensor or a fixed rule per cache type. The method first estimates quantization sensitivity across KV channels, then allocates a mixed bit budget, including very low-bit settings such as 2-bit configurations. Compared with prior static schemes, this is more granular and more aligned with where the error actually hurts model behavior.","experiments":"The abstract supports one clear empirical takeaway: ChanMix preserves model quality better than existing KV-cache quantization methods in low-bit, long-context regimes. It does not include reliable details on model families, context lengths, memory savings, or exact accuracy deltas. One notable signal is explicit support for 2-bit mixed precision, indicating the method is aimed at the hard regime where many alternatives break down.","authors_note":""},"tags":["kv-cache","quantization","long-context","inference","mixed-precision"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10006533","title":"Efficient Quantization of Mixture-of-Experts with Theoretical Generalization Guarantees","authors":["Mohammed Nowaz Rabbani Chowdhury","Kaoutar El Maghraoui","Hsinyu Tsai","Naigang Wang","Geoffrey Burr","Liu Liu","Meng Wang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$28f","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10006533"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10006533","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：MoE 虽然算力上稀疏激活，但推理内存仍然被大量 expert 参数拖住，而现有 mixed-precision 量化往往需要昂贵的 bit allocation 搜索，也没有真正利用 expert 间敏感度差异。过去大家默认按层或按张量统一分配精度，原因是简单；但对 MoE 来说，不同 expert 被路由的频率和对最终风险的影响本来就不一样，统一处理明显不经济。","importance":"这是一篇效率方法论文，但它提供了一个对 MoE 很有用的视角：量化精度应该围绕 expert 的功能重要性来分配，而不是只看局部权重统计。它的重要性在于把 expert-wise mixed precision 和理论泛化保证结合起来，既给出可实施的压缩策略，也回应了“为什么这样的 bit allocation 合理”的问题。对大规模稀疏预训练和部署都相关，因为 MoE 的可用性常常受内存和带宽而不是 FLOPs 限制。","technical_approach":"核心方法是一个有理论支撑的 expert-wise mixed-precision 策略。根据摘要，bit-width 主要由量化后各 expert 引起的风险变化来决定，而不是靠大规模搜索或简单用 Hessian / 权重范数代理。这样做的关键选择是把量化目标从“压缩单个矩阵误差”转成“控制不同 expert 对泛化误差的贡献”，因此更贴近 MoE 的路由-专家协同机制。和已有 mixed-precision PTQ 相比，它更专门针对 MoE 的不均匀性设计。","experiments":"摘要表明论文做了 post-training quantization，并声称在减少计算量的同时保住精度，还给出了理论 generalization guarantee。具体模型规模、语言或视觉任务、bit 配置、baseline 和提升幅度均未在摘要中出现，因此不能写数字。可以确定的有效观察是：作者认为不同 expert 的量化敏感度差异显著，而且这种差异足以支撑 expert-wise 而非 uniform allocation。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a practical MoE deployment issue: sparse activation reduces compute, but inference memory is still dominated by the large set of expert parameters, and existing mixed-precision schemes either search expensively for bit allocations or ignore expert-specific sensitivity. A uniform treatment is especially wasteful in MoE because experts differ in routing frequency and downstream impact.","importance":"This is an efficiency paper with a useful modeling insight. It argues that quantization policy for MoE should track expert importance rather than rely only on generic tensor statistics. The addition of theoretical generalization guarantees makes the contribution stronger than a purely heuristic compression recipe, especially for practitioners deploying large sparse models under memory constraints.","technical_approach":"The method is an expert-wise mixed-precision post-training quantization scheme with theoretical grounding. Bit widths are assigned primarily based on the change in risk induced by quantizing each expert, instead of using expensive search or simple local proxies alone. That design better matches how MoE models actually fail: not all experts matter equally, and quantization harm should be assessed at the expert-function level rather than only at the weight-tensor level.","experiments":"From the abstract, the paper reports strong efficiency–accuracy trade-offs and provides generalization guarantees, but it does not disclose reliable details on model scale, domains, bit settings, baselines, or margins. The one clear empirical premise is that expert sensitivity to quantization is highly non-uniform, and exploiting that structure improves over more uniform allocation strategies.","authors_note":""},"tags":["moe","quantization","inference","compression","theory"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10006564","title":"Reconciling Visual Perception and Generation in Diffusion Models","authors":["Liulei Li","Yi Yang","Wenguan Wang"],"institutions":["浙江大学"],"abstract":"We present \\textsc{GenRep}, a unified image understanding and synthesis model that jointly conducts  discriminative learning and generative modeling in one training session. By leveraging  Monte Carlo approximation, \\textsc{GenRep} distills distributional knowledge embedded in diffusion models to guide the discriminative learning for visual perception tasks. Simultaneously, a semantic-driven image generation process is established, where  high-level semantics learned from perception tasks can be used to inform image synthesis, creating a positive feedback loop for mutual boosts. Moreover, to reconcile the learning process for both tasks, a gradient alignment strategy is proposed to symmetrically modify the optimization directions of perception and generation losses. These designs empower \\textsc{GenRep} to be a versatile and powerful model that achieves top-leading performance on both image understanding and generation benchmarks. Show more","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10006564"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10006564","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"如何在一个统一模型中同时做好图像理解（判别式）和图像生成（生成式），而不是让两个任务互相拖累。此前 diffusion model 主要用于生成，判别式学习和生成式建模通常分开训练，联合训练时两个 loss 的优化方向容易冲突。","importance":"对 VLM 预训练中统一理解与生成的范式有参考价值。核心贡献在于提出了一种让判别和生成互相增强的正反馈机制，以及解决两个 loss 梯度冲突的对齐策略。属于方法级贡献，对 native multimodal 预训练中如何平衡多任务 loss 有直接启示。","technical_approach":"GenRep 基于 diffusion model，通过 Monte Carlo 近似从扩散模型中蒸馏分布知识来指导判别式学习（理解任务）；同时用理解任务学到的高层语义反过来引导生成过程，形成正反馈环。关键设计是梯度对齐策略（gradient alignment）：对称地修正理解和生成 loss 的优化方向，避免两个任务的梯度互相干扰。这比简单加权两个 loss 更精细，直接在梯度空间做调和。","experiments":"Abstract 截断，具体数字未完整给出。从描述看，GenRep 在一次训练中同时完成判别和生成任务，需看全文了解具体 benchmark 和提升幅度。","authors_note":"作者 Yi Yang 和 Wenguan Wang 来自浙江大学，在视觉理解和视频分割方向有较多工作。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Jointly training a single model for both image understanding (discriminative) and image generation (generative) without the two objectives degrading each other. Previously, diffusion models focused on generation, and joint training suffered from conflicting optimization directions between discriminative and generative losses.","importance":"Relevant to unified VLM pretraining paradigms. The main contribution is a mutual-boost mechanism between perception and generation, plus a gradient alignment strategy to resolve loss conflicts. Method-level contribution with direct implications for balancing multi-task losses in native multimodal pretraining.","technical_approach":"GenRep builds on diffusion models. It uses Monte Carlo approximation to distill distributional knowledge from the diffusion process to guide discriminative learning, while high-level semantics from perception tasks inform the generation process—creating a positive feedback loop. The key design is a gradient alignment strategy that symmetrically adjusts optimization directions of both losses in gradient space, going beyond simple loss weighting to directly reconcile conflicting gradients.","experiments":"Abstract truncated; specific benchmarks and numbers not fully available. are Greedy Agents: Effects of RL Fine-tuning on Decision-Making Abilities","authors":["Thomas Schmied","Jörg Bornschein","Jordi Grau-Moya","Markus Wulfmeier","Razvan
