Pang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$39b","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011176"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011176","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：给视频-语言模型一个真正促进时序推理的自监督训练目标，而不只是做图文对齐。过去常用的视频字幕更像是在学静态语义对齐，视频问答又依赖人工或强模型标注，训练信号贵且容易蒸馏已有偏见；作者提出的 next-event prediction（NEP）试图直接利用“未来片段”作为监督，让模型必须根据过去视频去推断接下来会发生什么。","importance":"它的重要性在于把 LLM 里被验证有效的“预测未来”范式，迁移成了视频时序理解里的预训练任务。对 pretrain 研究的启示不是又多了一个视频任务，而是：如果目标是让 MLLM 获得 temporal reasoning，训练目标本身要显式要求跨时间建模，而不能指望靠 caption/VQA 这类弱时序信号自然涌现；如果实验成立，这更接近方法级到范式级之间的贡献。","technical_approach":"核心方法是原创地把视频切成 past / future 两段，并让 MLLM基于过去帧预测未来事件文本。这个设计刻意不去重建未来像素，也不只做整段描述，而是把监督放在“事件”层面：像素预测过重、容易被低层动态主导，普通描述又过于宽松；事件预测则更贴近视频问答和时序推理所需的因果与状态转移建模。与最接近的 caption-style pretraining 相比，它不是对当前可见内容做转述，而是要求模型利用上下文和动态线索做前瞻性推断，因此更像视频上的 next-token objective。","experiments":"从给出的摘要看，实验主线应当是比较 NEP 与视频字幕、视频问答监督或其他视频预训练目标在时序理解任务上的差异，但目前缺少参数量、数据规模、baseline 名称和具体提升数字，不能编造。可以合理预期作者会做两类关键分析：一是验证 NEP 对 temporal reasoning 任务收益大于对纯识别类任务；二是分析预测粒度和过去/未来切分方式的影响，因为这直接决定任务难度与监督密度。就目前信息，能确认的是这项工作强调“未来作为自监督信号”而不是额外标注。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a specific gap: current video-language training objectives do not really teach temporal reasoning. Video captioning mostly learns modality alignment, while video QA depends on expensive human annotations or stronger teacher models; NEP reframes the problem as self-supervised future-event prediction from past video segments.","importance":"The contribution matters because it tests whether the next-token intuition from LLM pretraining has a video-native analogue. For pretraining research, the key message is that temporal reasoning likely needs an objective that explicitly rewards modeling state transitions over time, rather than hoping such behavior emerges from caption-style supervision. That is more than a task tweak; it is a training-objective argument.","technical_approach":"The method is to split each video into past and future parts, feed the past frames to an MLLM, and predict textual events in the future segment. The design choice is important: it avoids pixel-space future prediction, which is expensive and often dominated by low-level motion, and also avoids plain whole-video description, which is too weak a constraint. Predicting future events forces the model to infer dynamics, causality, and likely outcomes, making it much closer to an LLM-style predictive objective for videos.","experiments":"From the abstract alone, the experimental story is clear but the hard numbers are missing. The likely evaluation compares NEP against caption-based or QA-based video training on temporal reasoning benchmarks, with ablations on how videos are segmented and how future events are represented. What can be stated safely is that the paper is positioned around self-supervised supervision from future segments, not around additional annotation pipelines.","authors_note":""},"tags":["video-reasoning","next-event-prediction","mllm","temporal-reasoning"],"notable_authors":["Haonan Wang","Hongfu Liu","Xiangyan Liu","Chao Du","Kenji world model，而不是只会从文本或图像生成短视频片段。现有 diffusion video model 擅长高保真生成，但对“给定过去观测和动作，持续预测未来”这一世界建模场景支持不足，尤其缺少对多种动作形式和流式输出的统一处理。","importance":"它的重要性主要在建模范式和系统设计两层。对 pretrain 研究的价值在于，它把 AR 的因果时序建模与 diffusion 的高质量生成结合成一个交互式世界模型，说明统一的 sequence modeling 并不一定非要纯 next-token；在多模态预训练里，这类 AR+denoising 混合范式很值得关注，因为它同时触及长历史记忆、动作条件建模和流式生成三件事。","technical_approach":"核心方法是一个 autoregressive denoising world model：历史上下文按因果方式编码，未来则以去噪形式逐步生成。作者的关键设计有三点。第一，用 temporal causal attention 聚合过去观测和动作，保证预测时不偷看未来，并支持 streaming 输出。第二，用 noise-augmented history memory，刻意在历史记忆里注入噪声，避免模型过度依赖精确历史拷贝；如果不这么做，模型很容易把世界建模退化成短视的条件重建。第三，任务目标面向“真实交互未来”而不是单次无条件视频合成，这使它更接近 decision-time modeling 而不是离线生成。和标准 diffusion transformer 相比，区别不只是加动作条件，而是把历史记忆机制和因果结构放进去了。","experiments":"摘要明确覆盖 autonomous driving、robot grasping 等场景，并支持 camera motion、robot action 等不同动作形式，这说明作者在跨场景泛化上有明确诉求。但摘要没有给出模型规模、训练数据量、比较基线、FVD/PSNR/动作一致性等指标及具体数值，因此不能写提升幅度。可以预期关键实验会包括：与纯 diffusion video model、纯 AR video/world model 的比较；长时滚动预测稳定性；是否因 noise-augmented memory 改善 exposure bias 或减少对历史帧的复制。当前能确认的结论是，这不是单纯视频生成，而是面向交互式未来预测的通用 world model。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"This paper addresses a real gap in video modeling: high-quality diffusion video generators are not yet strong general-purpose world models that can roll forward futures from past observations and actions. The missing piece is long-horizon, action-conditioned prediction that works across scenarios and supports streaming interaction.","importance":"The contribution is notable at the paradigm level. For pretraining and multimodal modeling, it is interesting because it combines autoregressive causality with denoising generation instead of committing to a pure AR or pure diffusion formulation. That matters for any unified model that needs memory, controllability, and long-horizon prediction at the same time.","technical_approach":"Astra uses an autoregressive denoising architecture: past observations and actions are aggregated causally, while future outputs are generated through denoising. The design choices are the point. Temporal causal attention enforces proper history-to-future dependency and enables streaming outputs. A noise-augmented history memory is added so the model does not overfit to exact historical observations; without that, the model can collapse into short-range conditional reconstruction rather than robust world modeling. Compared with standard diffusion transformers, the novelty is not just action conditioning but the integration of causal history modeling and denoising generation.","experiments":"The abstract indicates evaluation across autonomous driving and robot grasping, with heterogeneous actions such as camera motion and robot actions. Zhao"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$39d","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011210"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011210","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：test-time scaling 里常见的 self-consistency + majority voting 计算代价高，而且随着采样数增加收益很快递减。问题在于现有方法把所有推理轨迹一视同仁地投票，没有利用模型自己已经暴露出来的“这条思路靠不靠谱”的置信信号。","importance":"它的重要性是一个很实用的推理时方法改进。对 pretrain/posttrain 的启示在于，模型内部置信度如果足够可用，就说明训练过程中已经学到了部分可校准的过程信号，后处理不一定要依赖更复杂的 verifier 或额外训练；这对 test-time compute 的使用方式是有价值的经验，但贡献量级更偏方法和工程侧，而不是范式级。","technical_approach":"核心方法 Deep Think with Confidence（DeepConf）是在生成中或生成后，根据模型内部置信信号动态过滤低质量 reasoning traces。它的卖点是无需额外训练、无需调很多超参，说明方法大概率利用现成的 token-level 或 sequence-level confidence 统计，而不是引入新评分器。设计上它抓住一个简单事实：多数投票在样本质量分布很偏的时候效率很低，先删掉明显差的轨迹能提升单位采样预算的有效信息密度。边界也很清楚——如果模型置信度本身严重失准，过滤可能误删正确但低置信的长链推理。","experiments":"摘要说明覆盖多个任务和最新开源模型，但没有给出模型名字之外的具体列表、采样预算、准确率增幅或推理成本下降比例，因此不能写硬数字。最关键的实验应当是：在相同 compute 下比 self-consistency 更高准确率，或在相同准确率下更少 sample；另外要看 pre-filter 与 post-filter 哪种更优，以及不同置信度定义是否稳定。若论文扎实，还应报告在 harder reasoning task 上收益更明显，因为这类任务里轨迹质量分布通常更长尾。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses an inefficiency in test-time scaling: self-consistency with majority voting is expensive and quickly hits diminishing returns because it treats all sampled reasoning traces equally. The missing ingredient is to use the model’s own confidence signals to avoid spending votes on obviously weak trajectories.","importance":"This is a practical inference-time contribution. The broader takeaway is that internal confidence signals learned during pretraining and posttraining may already contain enough information to improve reasoning efficiency without training an external verifier. That is useful for serving and evaluation, though it is not a paradigm shift.","technical_approach":"DeepConf filters low-quality reasoning traces during or after generation using model-internal confidence signals. The method is intentionally lightweight: no extra training and no substantial hyperparameter tuning. The logic is straightforward and sound—majority voting wastes budget when many samples are low quality, so removing weak traces should increase the effective information per sample. The natural limitation is calibration: if confidence is poorly aligned with correctness, the filter can discard valid but uncertain reasoning paths.","experiments":"The abstract says the method is tested across multiple tasks and recent open-source models, but it does not report exact model names, budgets, or gains. The key comparisons should measure accuracy at fixed compute and compute at fixed accuracy against vanilla self-consistency. The most useful ablations would compare online filtering versus post-hoc filtering and test alternative confidence definitions.","authors_note":""},"tags":["test-time-scaling","self-consistency","reasoning","confidence"],"notable_authors":["Yichao Fu","Xuewei Wang","Hao Zhang","Yuandong Tian","Jiawei Zhao"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011307","title":"Decoupling Dynamical Richness from Representation Learning: Towards Practical Measurement","authors":["Yoonsoo Nam","Nayara Fonseca","Seok Hyeong Lee","Chris Mingard","Niclas Göring","Ouns El Harzli","Abdurrahman Erturk","Soufiane Hayou","Ard Louis"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Dynamic feature transformation (the rich regime) does not always align with predictive performance (better representation), yet accuracy is often used as a proxy for richness, limiting analysis of their relationship. We propose a computationally efficient, performance-independent metric of richness grounded in the low-rank bias of rich dynamics, which recovers neural collapse as a special case. The metric is empirically more stable than existing alternatives and captures known lazy-to-rich transitions (e.g., grokking) without relying on accuracy. We further use it to examine how training factors (e.g., learning rate) relate to richness, confirming recognized assumptions and highlighting new observations (e.g., batch normalization promote rich dynamics). An eigendecomposition-based visualization is also introduced to support interpretability, together providing a diagnostic tool for studying the relationship between training factors, dynamics, and representations. That makes it hard to study optimization dynamics cleanly, especially in phenomena like grokking or lazy-to-rich transitions.","importance":"This is a mechanistic contribution rather than a benchmark-driven one. Asymptotic Performance of Test-Time LLM Ensembling","authors":["Junpei Komiyama","Daisuke Oba","Masafumi Oyamada"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"We study best-of-$N$ for large language models (LLMs) where the selection is based on majority voting. In particular, we analyze the limit $N \\to \\infty$, which we denote as best-of-$\\infty$. While this approach achieves impressive performance in the limit, it requires an infinite test-time budget. To address this, we propose an adaptive generation scheme that selects $N$ based on answer agreement, thereby efficiently allocating inference-time computation. Beyond adaptivity, we extend the framework to weighted ensembles of multiple LLMs, showing that such mixtures can outperform any individual model. The optimal ensemble weighting is formulated and efficiently computed as a mixed-integer linear program. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. Our code is available at https://github.com/jkomiyama/BoInf-code-publish/. Prior work mostly treated N as a static knob, so the question of how far majority voting can go asymptotically, and how to approximate that behavior under finite budget, remained underexplored.","importance":"This is mainly a method and systems contribution. It turns best-of-N from a heuristic into a more principled test-time scaling framework with an asymptotic view, adaptive compute allocation, and weighted multi-model ensembling. For pretraining-facing readers, the useful takeaway is that inference performance is partly governed by sample diversity and error correlation, not only by single-pass model quality; that matters when thinking about model families, training diversity, and whether multiple checkpoints or heterogeneous models can be more valuable than one stronger model.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to analyze majority-vote best-of-N in the limit N→∞, then use that perspective to design finite-budget adaptive sampling and weighted ensembles. First, the paper characterizes the asymptotic behavior of majority voting. Second, it proposes an adaptive stopping rule based on answer agreement: stop early when samples converge, keep sampling when disagreement persists. This reallocates inference compute toward uncertain cases. Third, it extends the setup from one model to weighted mixtures of multiple LLMs, with the optimal weights solved as a mixed-integer linear program. Unlike reranking with an auxiliary verifier or reward model, this framework relies on collective agreement, which keeps deployment simple but also limits gains when model errors are highly correlated.","experiments":"The abstract does not report model sizes, dataset sizes, or exact metrics, so the experimental summary has to stay conservative. What is clear is that the paper validates two claims: adaptive generation is more compute-efficient than fixed-N sampling, and weighted ensembles of multiple LLMs can outperform every individual model. The most important empirical question is not just average accuracy, but how quickly returns saturate with N and whether adaptive budgeting consistently concentrates samples on hard prompts rather than merely shifting cost around.","authors_note":""},"tags":["test-time-compute","majority-voting","best-of-n","scaling"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011613","title":"Predicting LLM Output Length via Entropy-Guided Representations","authors":["Huanyi Xie","Yubin Chen","Liangyu Wang","Lijie Hu","Di Wang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3ad","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011613"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011613","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：LLM serving 和 RL 采样里的输出长度分布是长尾的，batch 内 padding 浪费很大，但现有长度预测通常依赖额外小模型，开销不低、迁移性差，而且对 one-to-many 的随机生成场景不稳定。问题的关键不是“能不能预测长度”，而是“能不能几乎零额外成本地、在主模型运行过程中持续预测长度”。","importance":"这是一个偏系统层的工作，但对大规模训练和采样很实用。它的重要性在于把长度预测从独立辅助任务改成对主模型表征的再利用，直接服务于 batch packing、调度和 rollout 资源分配。对 pretrain 和 posttrain 工程都有关，因为无论是离线评测、在线 serving，还是 RL 采样，长度长尾都会放大吞吐损失；如果预测足够准，就能改善 token-level utilization，而不需要修改模型权重或解码目标。","technical_approach":"核心方法是复用主模型隐状态做长度预测，包含两个部件。EGTP 是静态预测器：它对 on-the-fly activation 做 entropy-guided token pooling，用 token 熵来挑更有代表性的位置信息，从而在几乎无额外代价下预测总输出长度。PLP 是动态预测器：在解码每一步持续估计剩余长度，适配随机生成时同一输入可能产生不同长度的问题。和单独训练 auxiliary predictor 相比，这种方案减少了部署复杂度，也避免了 predictor 与主模型失配；但它依赖一个假设，即隐状态和 token entropy 足够编码“还会说多久”这一信号，如果任务强依赖外部格式约束或后验采样温度变化很大，预测稳定性可能下降。","experiments":"摘要没有给出具体模型规模、吞吐提升和误差数字，所以不能写出量化结论。能确认的实验主张有两点：一是 EGTP 能以极低成本实现高精度静态长度预测；二是 PLP 能在 stochastic one-to-many 场景中动态跟踪剩余长度，优于静态方法。真正决定这项工作价值的实验指标通常包括长度预测 MAE / calibration、batch padding 减少比例、实际吞吐提升以及在 RL 采样里的 wall-clock 节省。若有 ablation，最关键的是 entropy pooling 是否优于简单 mean/last-token pooling，以及动态 PLP 在高温采样下是否仍稳。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a serving and sampling inefficiency: output lengths in LLM inference and RL rollout generation are heavily long-tailed, so batched decoding wastes substantial compute on padding. Existing solutions often rely on separate auxiliary predictors, which add overhead, transfer poorly across settings, and struggle in stochastic one-to-many generation where the same prompt can lead to very different output lengths.","importance":"This is primarily a systems contribution, but a useful one for both pretraining-scale evaluation and post-training pipelines. Its value is in turning length prediction into a near-free byproduct of the main model’s own representations, which directly helps scheduling, batch packing, and rollout budgeting. The idea matters because sequence-length variance is a recurring throughput bottleneck, and reducing it does not require changes to the model architecture or training objective.","technical_approach":"The method reuses hidden states from the main model to predict length, with two components. EGTP performs static prediction through entropy-guided token pooling, using token entropy and live activations to extract a compact representation that is informative about final output length at negligible cost. PLP then handles dynamic prediction by estimating the remaining length during decoding, which is important when outputs are stochastic rather than deterministic. Compared with standalone predictors, this keeps deployment simple and reduces model mismatch. The main limitation is that it assumes hidden states and token entropy reliably encode length information; that may weaken under strong format constraints or large decoding-policy shifts.","experiments":"The abstract does not give model sizes, absolute errors, or throughput gains, so only the qualitative findings are safe to report. The paper claims that EGTP achieves accurate static length prediction with negligible overhead, and that PLP improves remaining-length estimation during stochastic decoding. The most important results to inspect would be prediction error and calibration, padding reduction, end-to-end throughput gains, and robustness under RL-style high-variance sampling. A strong ablation would compare entropy-guided pooling against simpler hidden-state pooling strategies.","authors_note":""},"tags":["serving","length-prediction","entropy","batched-inference"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011617","title":"A$^2$FM: An Adaptive Agent Foundation Model for Tool-Aware Jiang","Wangchunshu Zhou"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3ae","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011617"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011617","category_id":"agent","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：当前 LLM 生态在 reasoning model 和 agent model 之间分裂，前者擅长内部链式推理但不会用工具，后者会调工具和与环境交互但深推理常常弱，而且两类模型都容易在简单问题上过度思考或过度调用工具。作者想做的是一个统一模型，在同一 backbone 内根据任务难度和工具需求自适应选择模式，而不是把不同能力分散在不同系统里。","importance":"这项工作更接近范式级的 agent foundation model 探索。它的重要性在于提出“route-then-align”的统一训练视角：不是先把所有行为混在一起学，而是先让模型学会什么时候该直接回答、什么时候该深推理、什么时候该调用工具，再对齐不同模式下的轨迹。对 pretrain/posttrain 接口的启示很直接——如果未来通用模型既要 reasoning 又要 tool use，那么训练目标和数据组织方式必须显式建模模式选择，而不是只在后处理层面拼装。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 A^2FM，包含三种模式：内部 reasoning、tool-aware agentic 推理，以及处理简单查询的 instant mode。方法上的关键不是简单多任务训练，而是先 routing、后 alignment。先让模型做 task-aware routing，决定当前 query 适合哪种模式，以避免简单题走重链路、复杂题走轻链路；再把不同模式下的轨迹在共享 backbone 上对齐，减少能力割裂。与只训练 agent policy 或只强化 CoT 的方法相比，这种设计更强调 compute allocation 和行为分层。它的边界也很清楚：路由一旦错，后续模式再强也会走偏；因此性能高度依赖 mode classifier 的校准和训练数据对三种模式边界的定义。","experiments":"摘要没有给出模型规模、训练数据和 benchmark 数字，因此无法复述定量结果。能确认的实验点是，作者旨在证明统一模型相较纯 reasoning 模型和纯 agentic 模型更高效，并且通过 instant mode 缓解简单问题上的 overthinking/over-calling。真正关键的实验应该包括：不同任务类型上的模式分配是否合理；在工具需求明显和不明显的场景中，A^2FM 是否同时保住准确率与成本；以及去掉 instant mode 或 route-then-align 后会损失多少。若这些 ablation 明显，方法才不仅仅是一个多头系统工程。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a growing split between two model families: reasoning-heavy LLMs that are strong at internal chain-of-thought but lack tool use, and agentic LLMs that interact with tools and environments but often underperform on deeper reasoning. Both also waste effort on easy queries by overthinking or over-calling tools. The goal is a unified model that can choose the right mode instead of forcing one behavior everywhere.","importance":"This is close to a paradigm-level attempt at an agent foundation model. The key contribution is the route-then-align view: first learn which mode a task needs, then align trajectories from different modes under a shared backbone. That matters because future general-purpose models will likely need both deep reasoning and tool use, and this suggests that mode selection should be part of the training formulation rather than an external orchestration layer.","technical_approach":"A^2FM introduces three modes under one backbone: internal reasoning, tool-aware agentic reasoning, and an instant mode for simple queries. The main design choice is not naive multitask mixing but a two-stage route-then-align process. The model first performs task-aware routing to decide which mode to use, avoiding heavy reasoning or unnecessary tool calls on easy inputs. It then aligns mode-specific trajectories so that the shared backbone does not fragment into disconnected skills. Compared with pure agent training or pure CoT enhancement, this framework puts more emphasis on behavior allocation and compute discipline. Its main limitation is routing quality: if the model chooses the wrong mode, strong mode-specific policies cannot recover easily.","experiments":"The abstract does not provide parameter counts, training scale, or benchmark scores, so the summary remains qualitative. The intended empirical claim is that one unified model can match or exceed separate reasoning-centric and agentic baselines while being more efficient, especially by using the instant mode on simple tasks. The most revealing experiments would test whether routing decisions correlate with true task difficulty and tool need, and how much performance drops when the instant mode or the route-then-align strategy is removed.","authors_note":""},"tags":["tool-use","reasoning","agent","hybrid-training"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011615","title":"Implicit Regularisation in Diffusion Models: An Algorithm-Dependent Generalisation Analysis","authors":["Tyler Farghly","Patrick Rebeschini","George Deligiannidis","Arnaud Doucet"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3af","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011615"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011615","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：理论上如果 diffusion model 训练和采样都足够完美，它会记住训练数据，因此实际泛化一定依赖某种隐式正则化；但现有理论大多用算法无关的 uniform convergence 类工具，很难解释“为什么特定训练算法会带来泛化”。作者希望把问题从模型类的容量分析，转到训练算法本身的稳定性分析。","importance":"这是一篇偏理论的工作，但对 diffusion 预训练很相关。它的重要性在于提供了一个 algorithm-dependent 的泛化视角，试图解释扩散模型为何在高维下没有完全走向记忆化。对做 diffusion LM、图像 diffusion 或 AR+diffusion 联合建模的人来说，这类结果的价值不在于直接给配方，而在于明确哪些训练机制本身带有正则化效果，从而影响优化、步长、噪声日程和采样近似的选择。","technical_approach":"核心方法是把 algorithmic stability 的框架迁移到 diffusion setting，并引入适用于该场景的稳定性概念，分析训练算法如何影响泛化。和传统只看假设空间大小或模型 Lipschitz 性质的分析不同，这里关注的是：如果训练数据改动一个样本，学习算法输出会变化多少，以及这种变化如何传递到 diffusion model 的泛化误差。这样做的好处是更贴近实际训练过程，也更有机会解释不同优化器、噪声注入或近似策略带来的差异。边界在于，理论稳定性结果通常依赖较强假设，未必能直接覆盖大规模离散 diffusion LM 或复杂 sampler。","experiments":"摘要没有给出实验细节，重点显然是理论分析而非大规模经验结果。可以确定的是，作者将此前“完美训练+完美采样会记忆数据”的现象作为出发点，论证需要借助算法相关的正则化来解释实际泛化。若论文有实验，最值得看的是理论预测是否和训练超参数变化下的泛化趋势一致，比如步长、训练轮数、噪声水平对 memorization/generalization 的影响方向是否吻合。没有这些配套验证，理论贡献会更偏概念性。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a theoretical gap in diffusion-model generalization. In idealized settings with perfect training and sampling, denoising diffusion models have been shown to memorize the training data, which implies that real-world generalization must come from some form of regularization. Existing analyses are mostly algorithm-independent, so they say little about why specific training procedures generalize better than others.","importance":"This is a theory-oriented contribution, but one that matters for diffusion-style pretraining. Its value is in shifting the discussion from model-class capacity alone to algorithm-dependent effects. For researchers working on diffusion LMs, image diffusion, or hybrid AR-diffusion training, the practical relevance is indirect but real: if generalization depends strongly on optimization-induced regularization, then training procedure choices are part of the modeling story, not just implementation detail.","technical_approach":"The paper brings an algorithmic-stability perspective into the diffusion setting and defines a notion of stability suited to this class of models. Instead of bounding generalization only through hypothesis-space complexity or architecture-specific arguments, it studies how sensitive the learned model is to small changes in the training data and how that sensitivity translates into generalization error. The advantage is that this framework is closer to the actual learning algorithm and can, in principle, explain why certain optimization choices regularize more than others. The limitation is the usual one for stability theory: meaningful results often require assumptions that may not fully match large-scale diffusion language models or realistic samplers.","experiments":"The abstract emphasizes theory and does not provide empirical details. What can be safely said is that the work starts from the observation that perfect diffusion training and sampling lead to memorization, and then argues that algorithm-dependent regularization is needed to explain practical generalization. If the paper includes experiments, the most useful ones would test whether the theory predicts how changes in optimization settings, noise levels, or training duration affect memorization and generalization in practice.","authors_note":""},"tags":["diffusion","generalization","implicit-regularization","theory"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011663","title":"Once-More: Continuous Self-Correction for Large Language Models via Perplexity-Guided Intervention","authors":["Jiaxun Gao","Him Wai (Michael) Ng","Z. Mid-Training for Efficient Learning of Consistency, Mean Flow, and Flow-Map Models","authors":["Zheyuan Hu","Chieh-Hsin Lai","Yuki Mitsufuji","Stefano Ermon"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3b8","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011770"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011770","category_id":"multimodal_gen","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：Consistency / Mean Flow / Flow-Map 这类少步生成模型虽然推理快，但训练阶段仍然不稳定、对超参敏感，而且从预训练 diffusion 直接跳到 long-jump 映射学习时存在明显的目标错位。过去常见做法是从一个已经训好的 diffusion model 初始化，再直接做 consistency 或 flow-map 蒸馏；这样虽然比从零训练好，但模型还是要一下子把“局部微分步”改学成“跨大步的全局映射”，优化面很陡，稳定性问题并没有真正解决。作者认为这里缺了一个过渡阶段，因此提出 mid-training，把 pretrain 和最终 post-training 之间补上一个轻量但结构化的桥接阶段。","importance":"这项工作的价值首先在训练流程，而不只是某个新损失。它把 vision generation 里的 diffusion→few-step flow-map 训练，明确拆成 pretrain / mid-train / post-train 三段，这对生成模型预训练研究是有启发的：很多不稳定并不是最终目标本身不行，而是阶段切换过猛。对 pretrain 社区更重要的一点是，它提供了一个很具体的“中训接口”案例——先把表示和动力学逐步迁移，再学最终 shortcut 映射。这和大模型里的 cooldown、annealing、continual adaptation 是同一类问题，只是落在 diffusion-family 上。若结果稳健，这类设计可能比继续堆更复杂的 flow matching 目标更有工程收益。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 CMT（Consistency Mid-Training），本质上是一个插在预训练 diffusion model 和最终 consistency / mean-flow / flow-map 训练之间的轨迹映射阶段。它不是直接让学生一开始就拟合大跨度去噪终点，而是先利用预训练模型的 solver trajectory，在轨迹上的相邻或较短跨度点之间学习映射，让模型先适应“沿着老师动力学走，但步子逐渐变大”的任务。这个设计的关键选择是保留预训练模型已经学到的局部向量场结构，用中间阶段逐步把局部积分行为压缩成更粗粒度的 flow map；它舍弃了直接从 infinitesimal-step teacher 到 long-jump student 的硬切换。和最接近的做法相比，区别不在是否从 teacher 初始化，而在是否显式设计一个 curriculum，把轨迹学习难度从短跳逐步推到长跳，因此理论上更容易稳定，也更不依赖脆弱的 loss weighting 和 solver hyperparameter。","experiments":"从摘要可确认的结论是：作者报告 mid-training 能同时提升训练稳定性、超参鲁棒性和训练效率，并且适用于 CM、MF、Flow-Map 这一类 few-step 生成模型，而不是只对单一目标有效。摘要没有给出模型参数量、数据集规模、具体指标和提升数字，因此不能确认它是小规模验证还是在标准图像生成基准上的系统比较。能确定的实验重点大概率包括三类：一是直接训练 vs 从 diffusion 初始化 vs 加入 CMT 的三段式训练；二是不同求解器轨迹和步数设置下的稳定性；三是从较短跳到较长跳的课程式中训是否优于一步到位。若正文数据扎实，这类 ablation 会比单纯 FID 改善更有价值，因为论文真正的主张是优化路径变好了，而不只是终点指标偶然更高。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a practical bottleneck: few-step flow-map models such as Consistency Models and Mean Flow are fast at inference, but their training is still unstable, hyperparameter-sensitive, and expensive because the model is asked to jump too abruptly from local diffusion dynamics to long-range shortcut mappings. Prior work often initializes from a pretrained diffusion model and then directly distills into a consistency or flow-map objective. That helps, but it does not resolve the core mismatch: the model still has to convert infinitesimal-step behavior into a long-jump operator in one shot.","importance":"The main contribution is a training recipe, not just a new loss. It formalizes a three-stage pipeline—pretraining, mid-training, post-training—for vision generative models, and that is relevant beyond this specific model family. For pretraining research, the broader message is useful: many optimization failures come from sharp objective transitions rather than from the final objective itself. The work gives a concrete example of how an intermediate adaptation stage can smooth that transition, which closely parallels mid-training, annealing, and curriculum-style interface design in large-scale foundation model training.","technical_approach":"CMT, or Consistency Mid-Training, inserts a lightweight intermediate stage between a pretrained diffusion model and final consistency / mean-flow / flow-map training. Instead of asking the student to immediately learn a large denoising jump, CMT first trains it on mappings between points along the teacher solver trajectory, starting from easier, shorter-range transitions. The design choice is deliberate: preserve the local dynamics already learned during diffusion pretraining, then gradually compress those dynamics into a coarser flow map. Compared with direct teacher initialization followed by final distillation, the novelty is the explicit curriculum over trajectory mapping difficulty. That should improve stability because the optimization landscape changes more smoothly, and it should reduce dependence on brittle solver and loss-tuning choices.","experiments":"From the abstract, the key empirical claim is that mid-training improves stability, robustness to hyperparameters, and training efficiency across multiple flow-map style models rather than a single method. The abstract does not provide model sizes, datasets, metrics, or exact gains, so it is not possible to verify the scale of the study from the provided information alone. The most important experiments are likely comparisons among direct training, diffusion-initialized post-training, and the proposed three-stage pipeline, plus sensitivity studies over solver choices and jump lengths. If those ablations are strong, they matter more than a marginal FID gain because the paper’s central claim is about optimization path quality, not only endpoint quality.","authors_note":""},"tags":["mid-train","diffusion","consistency-model","flow-matching","training-stability"],"notable_authors":["Stefano Ermon","Yuki Mitsufuji"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011777","title":"KDP: Simplifying Representation Dynamics in Kernel Space","authors":["Zeyu Ma","Wanying Wang","Guchu Zou","Mingang Chen","Jianhong Wu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"This paper proposes Kernelized Dynamics Pruning (KDP), a novel layer pruning method from the perspective of simplifying representation dynamics within large language models (LLMs). Motivated by the high similarity between consecutive layer representations, we view the LLM's forward pass as a discrete-time dynamical system. We speculate that this phenomenon indicates the model's internal dynamics have entered a ``slow manifold'', which exhibits computational redundancy. Based on this insight, we project the representations into a kernel space where the complex, non-linear transformation between them is simplified to an approximately linear one. Then, a simple network learns the inverse kernel transformation, thereby enabling the pruning of the entire layer block. Both theoretical analysis and extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of KDP, demonstrating its superiority over existing pruning baselines. Show more","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011777"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011777","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：LLM 很多相邻层的表示变化已经非常小，但现有剪层方法往往从权重相似性、梯度敏感性或简单蒸馏出发，没真正解释“哪些层只是重复做了慢变化”。作者把前向传播看成离散动力系统，认为后部一些层已经进入 slow manifold，也就是表示沿着一个低速、低自由度轨道缓慢演化，因此计算上存在系统性冗余。问题的关键不是删掉哪一层参数最少影响 loss，而是能否在表示动力学层面找出可被整体替代的冗余层块。","importance":"这项工作的意义在于，它给 LLM 结构压缩提供了一个比经验式 layer pruning 更有机制感的视角。对 pretrain 研究者来说，这不只是部署优化问题：如果很多层只是在低速流形上做近线性微调，那么这会反过来影响我们对 depth scaling、层间功能分化和训练后期表示收缩的理解。贡献量级更像方法级加分析级，而不是范式级突破。它未必直接改变预训练范式，但如果 kernel-space 线性化确实稳定有效，就说明“表示动力学是否已经变慢”可以成为剪层、早退甚至动态计算的统一判据。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 KDP（Kernelized Dynamics Pruning），本质上是先把层间非线性表示演化映射到 kernel space，再在这个空间里用更简单的近线性动态替代整个层块。作者的判断是：原空间里相邻层的变换复杂且难直接拟合，但投到合适的核空间后，局部动态可以近似成线性，因此可用一个小网络学习逆核变换，把被删掉层块后的表示恢复回来。这个设计与传统做法的区别有两点。第一，它不是逐层打分再硬删，而是把若干连续层视为一个动力学段。第二，它关注的是“表示轨迹是否可压缩”，而不是单层权重是否重要。这样设计有效的前提是表示确实已经进入慢变化区；如果模型前部仍在做强非线性特征重组，这种线性化近似的收益和保真度都会下降。","experiments":"摘要给出的结论是：KDP 既有理论分析也有实验验证，并且主张在剪除整段层块时仍能较好保留性能。但摘要没有提供具体模型规模、剪枝比例、下游任务、吞吐提升或精度损失，因此目前只能确认方向，无法确认量级。值得关注的实验应包括：与常见 layer pruning baseline 比较在同等压缩率下的准确率/困惑度退化；不同层位剪枝的效果差异，用来验证 slow manifold 是否主要出现在中后层；以及 kernel 选择、块大小、逆变换网络容量的 ablation。如果这些实验显示后层可被更激进压缩而前层不行，那会直接支持它的动力学解释。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a familiar inefficiency: many consecutive LLM layers produce highly similar representations, yet most pruning methods still operate through heuristic importance scores or local sensitivity estimates rather than modeling why those layers have become redundant. The authors view the forward pass as a discrete dynamical system and argue that some layers lie on a slow manifold, where representations evolve only gradually. The key question is therefore not just which layer can be dropped with minimal loss, but whether an entire segment of representation dynamics can be replaced more compactly.","importance":"The contribution is mainly methodological with a useful mechanistic angle. For pretraining researchers, this matters beyond deployment compression because it speaks to depth scaling and layer specialization: if later layers mostly perform low-speed, near-linear refinement, then redundancy is a property of the learned dynamics, not an accident of parameterization. This is not a paradigm shift, but it could be a meaningful step toward a more principled criterion for layer pruning, early exit, or adaptive compute based on representation dynamics rather than ad hoc saliency.","technical_approach":"KDP, or Kernelized Dynamics Pruning, projects intermediate representations into a kernel space where the nonlinear transition across layers becomes approximately linear, then learns a lightweight inverse transformation so an entire layer block can be removed. The design choice is important: it does not score layers independently and prune them one by one. Instead, it treats a sequence of layers as a dynamical segment and asks whether its trajectory is compressible after kernelization. The method should work when representations have already entered a slow-evolving regime. Its limitation is also clear: if early layers still perform substantial nonlinear feature reorganization, the linearized kernel-space approximation is less likely to hold.","experiments":"The abstract claims both theoretical support and empirical validation, with the main result being that KDP can prune whole layer blocks while preserving performance better than simpler baselines. However, the provided abstract includes no model scales, pruning ratios, throughput gains, datasets, or exact accuracy/perplexity numbers, so the empirical magnitude cannot be verified here. The most informative experiments will be compression-performance curves versus standard layer pruning baselines, pruning behavior across depth, and ablations over kernel choice, block size, and inverse-model capacity. If later layers are consistently more compressible than earlier ones, that would directly support the slow-manifold hypothesis rather than just showing a new pruning heuristic.","authors_note":""},"tags":["pruning","compression","representation","inference","llm"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011789","title":"AlphaSteer: Learning Refusal Steering with Principled Null-Space Constraint","authors":["Leheng Sheng","Changshuo Shen","Weixiang Zhao","Junfeng Fang","Xiaohao Liu","Zhenkai Liang","Xiang Wang","An Zhang","Tat-Seng Chua"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3b9","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011789"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011789","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：activation steering 能明显增强 LLM 的拒答能力，但通常会把安全和可用性绑死在一起——拒答向量一加，恶意请求更难越狱，正常请求也更容易被误拒。过去的校准或条件式 steering 多半是经验修补，能缓解但缺少清晰约束，因此在复杂 jailbreak 场景里要么不够强，要么副作用大。作者要解决的核心问题是，能否在保留 refusal steering 效果的同时，系统性约束它不去破坏正常能力。","importance":"这项工作的贡献更偏 post-train / inference control，但对 pretrain-posttrain 接口也有启发。它的重要性不在于再做一个安全小技巧，而在于给 activation steering 增加了一个更有原则的几何约束：把拒答控制限制在不干扰主任务能力的子空间。对做基座模型的人，这类方法的价值是诊断性的——如果 null-space 约束真的有效，说明安全行为和有用能力在表示空间里并非完全纠缠，可以被局部分离。这个结论会影响我们如何看待表示可控性、对齐信号注入的位置，以及未来是否值得把类似约束提前并入训练。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 AlphaSteer，用一个 principled null-space constraint 来学习 refusal steering。直观上，它不是简单找一个能提升拒答率的方向，而是要求 steering 方向尽量落在“对正常任务无影响”或影响最小的零空间里，于是模型在 benign prompts 上的主导表征被保留下来。这个设计与已有向量校准或门控式 steering 的区别是，它把 utility preservation 写成显式几何约束，而不是仅靠数据上调权重来碰运气。这样做有效的前提是：正常任务表征与拒答行为确实存在一定可分子空间；如果两者高度重叠，那么 null-space 可用维度会很小，安全增益和可用性保持之间仍然会有硬边界。","experiments":"摘要能确认的实验结论是：作者声称 AlphaSteer 能比现有 activation steering 方法更好地缓解 safety-utility trade-off，尤其是在 jailbreak 防御下减少 over-refusal。摘要没有提供模型名称、参数规模、测试基准、攻击集和具体提升数字，因此无法判断它是在开源中小模型上的局部改进，还是在较强聊天模型上的稳定结果。最关键的实验应包括：恶意提示上的 attack success rate / refusal rate，正常指令上的 helpfulness 或 benchmark 准确率，以及不同层位、不同 steering 强度下的鲁棒性曲线。如果还做了表示投影或子空间重叠分析，那会比单纯 benchmark 分数更能支撑它的理论主张。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a central weakness of activation steering for safety: adding a refusal vector can improve jailbreak resistance, but it often degrades utility on benign prompts by causing over-refusal. Existing fixes such as vector calibration or conditional steering reduce the problem only heuristically. The core question is whether refusal behavior can be steered in a way that preserves normal capabilities by construction rather than by ad hoc tuning.","importance":"This is primarily a post-training and inference-control contribution, but it is still relevant to foundation model research because it probes how safety behavior is represented internally. The useful idea is not just another safety patch. It is the claim that refusal can be controlled inside a constrained subspace that minimally interferes with useful behavior. If that claim holds empirically, it suggests that safety and utility are at least partially separable in representation space, which has implications for controllability, alignment injection, and whether similar constraints could eventually be moved earlier into training.","technical_approach":"AlphaSteer learns a refusal steering direction under a principled null-space constraint. Instead of optimizing only for stronger refusal, it restricts the steering direction to lie as much as possible in a subspace that leaves benign-task representations unchanged or minimally affected. The difference from prior calibration or gating methods is that utility preservation becomes an explicit geometric constraint rather than an indirect data-balancing trick. The method should work when refusal behavior and normal task behavior are not fully entangled. Its limitation is equally clear: if those subspaces overlap heavily, the available null space shrinks and the safety-utility trade-off cannot disappear entirely.","experiments":"The abstract supports the claim that AlphaSteer improves the safety-utility trade-off compared with prior activation steering methods, especially by reducing over-refusal under jailbreak defense settings. However, the abstract does not specify model families, parameter scales, benchmarks, attack suites, or exact gains, so the empirical scope is unclear. The most important experiments should report attack success or refusal performance on harmful prompts, utility on benign instruction-following tasks, and robustness across steering layers and steering strengths. Subspace-overlap analysis or representation-level diagnostics would be especially valuable because the paper’s main contribution is its geometric constraint, not just its downstream scores.","authors_note":""},"tags":["alignment","safety","activation-steering","refusal","inference"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011790","title":"Secret-Protected Evolution for Differentially Private Synthetic Text Generation","authors":["Tianze Wang","Zhaoyu Chen","Jian Du","Yingtai Xiao","Linjun Zhang","Qiang Yan"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3ba","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011790"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011790","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：差分隐私合成文本生成现在常用统一的 DP 保护强度去保护整份文本，但真实语料里敏感信息只占一部分，结果就是大量非敏感内容也被过度保护，导致文本可用性明显下降、生成开销也变大。过去这类方法往往追求“一刀切”的最坏情况保证，理论干净但代价高。作者想解决的是，能否在仍保留严格隐私保证的前提下，把保护重点聚焦到真正的 secret 内容上，从而减少无谓的 utility 损失。","importance":"这项工作的贡献是方法级加隐私定义级。它对 pretrain 研究的重要性在于，高质量私有文本是现实里很有价值但很难用的数据源；如果 secret-aware 的私有合成能在效用上明显优于传统 DP 方案，那它会直接影响私有语料参与预训练或持续预训练的可行性。更具体地说，这篇论文不是单纯优化一个生成器，而是在重新定义‘该保护什么’。这种问题设定比继续在固定 DP 预算下微调一点 perplexity 更关键，因为它决定了隐私-效用前沿的形状。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 SecPE（Secret-Protected Evolution），它在 private evolution 框架上加入 secret-aware protection。简单说，它不再对所有内容施加同质隐私约束，而是围绕 secret 信息定义更细粒度的保护目标，并理论证明满足某种 $(\\epsilon, \\rho)$ secret-protection 保证。这里的关键设计取舍是：承认语料的敏感性分布不均匀，让机制把预算和扰动更多放在高敏感片段，而不是平均摊薄到全部 token 或样本。与传统 DP synthetic text generation 相比，区别不只是调噪声大小，而是保护对象从“整个数据分布”转向“带 secret 语义的局部风险”。边界也很明确：如果 secret 识别本身不可靠，或者敏感信息与普通内容高度耦合，secret-aware 机制就可能出现漏保或收益下降。","experiments":"摘要可确认的实验结论是：SecPE 在保持秘密保护保证的同时，目标是比统一 DP 保护取得更好的文本效用并降低不必要的开销。摘要没有给出使用的基座模型、数据集、privacy budget、生成质量指标或下游效用数字，因此无法判断优势有多大，也无法确认它是否覆盖 LLM 规模场景。关键实验应包括：与标准 DP 文本生成和 private evolution baseline 在相同保护水平下比较 utility，如 perplexity、下游任务表现或人工质量；不同 secret 比例或 secret 密度下的收益变化；以及错误 secret 标注对保护和效用的影响。如果这些 stress test 做得充分，这篇工作会比单一平均分更有参考价值。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a major inefficiency in differentially private synthetic text generation: existing methods typically apply uniform privacy protection to all content, even though only a fraction of the text is truly sensitive. That leads to unnecessary utility loss and extra computation because non-sensitive content is overprotected. The authors ask whether privacy mechanisms can focus protection on actual secrets while still retaining formal guarantees.","importance":"This is both a method contribution and a problem-definition contribution. For pretraining, it matters because valuable private text is often inaccessible under standard data-use constraints. If secret-aware private generation can preserve substantially more utility than uniform DP protection, it could expand the practical role of private corpora in pretraining or continual pretraining. The important shift here is not merely a better generator under the same old setup. It is a redefinition of what should be protected, which directly changes the privacy-utility frontier.","technical_approach":"SecPE extends private evolution with secret-aware protection. Instead of enforcing homogeneous privacy over all text, it defines protection around secret-bearing content and proves an $(\\epsilon, \\rho)$ style secret-protection guarantee. The central design choice is to acknowledge that sensitivity is unevenly distributed and to allocate perturbation or privacy budget accordingly. Relative to standard DP synthetic text generation, the difference is not just noise calibration. It is a shift from protecting the entire data distribution uniformly to protecting localized semantic risk associated with secrets. The main limitation is dependence on reliable secret identification. If secrets are mislabeled or tightly entangled with ordinary content, the gains may shrink and coverage may weaken.","experiments":"The abstract supports the claim that SecPE aims to achieve better text utility and lower unnecessary overhead than uniform DP approaches while preserving secret protection guarantees. However, it provides no model scale, datasets, privacy budgets, quality metrics, or downstream results, so the empirical magnitude remains unclear. The most useful experiments should compare SecPE against standard DP text generation and private evolution baselines at matched protection levels, test performance across different secret densities, and analyze robustness to imperfect secret labeling. Those stress tests would matter more than a single average score because the central claim is targeted protection, not generic quality improvement.","authors_note":""},"tags":["synthetic-data","privacy","text-generation","data-quality","pretraining"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011793","title":"pi-Flow: Policy-Based Few-Step Generation via Imitation Distillation","authors":["Hansheng Chen","Kai Zhang","Hao Tan","Leonidas Guibas","Gordon Wetzstein","Sai Bi"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3bb","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011793"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011793","category_id":"multimodal_gen","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：few-step diffusion / flow 模型通常用一个预测 velocity 的 teacher 去蒸馏一个直接做 shortcut 去噪的 student，但 teacher 和 student 的输出形式不一致，导致蒸馏流程复杂，而且常出现质量和多样性此消彼长。过去的做法常靠多阶段蒸馏、复杂时间采样或额外一致性约束来弥补这种 format mismatch，但工程复杂且不稳定。作者想解决的是，能不能让 student 保留 flow/ODE 生成的动态表达能力，同时又只用极少步推理。","importance":"这项工作的价值在于，它把 few-step 生成的 distillation 问题重新表述成 policy learning，而不是继续在“学生要不要直接预测终点”这个框架里打补丁。对生成模型预训练研究者来说，这是一种有潜力的方法级创新：让学生输出一个能在未来子步上产生速度场的紧凑 policy，相当于把多步积分的一部分计算前移到表示里。若这一思路成立，它对 flow matching、consistency distillation、乃至统一的 AR+iterative generation 设计都有启发，因为它强调‘少网络调用’不一定等于‘一次性预测全部终点信息’。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 pi-Flow：修改 student flow model 的输出层，让它在某一时刻预测一个无需额外网络前向的 policy，然后用这个 policy 在后续若干子步上动态产生 flow velocity，完成快速 ODE 积分。关键设计有两点。第一，学生不直接回归一个静态大跳终点，而是输出一个可执行的局部控制策略，所以保留了轨迹层面的灵活性。第二，作者提出 imitation distillation，不是只对最终样本或一步输出做匹配，而是沿着 teacher 轨迹去匹配 policy 生成的速度，这样 supervision 与学生实际执行的轨迹更一致。和现有 shortcut distillation 相比，这种设计减少了 output format mismatch；代价是 policy 设计必须足够低开销且足够稳定，否则会把误差累积到后续子步。","experiments":"从摘要能确认的结论是：pi-Flow 声称能在几乎不增加额外计算的前提下，通过少步 ODE 积分实现更快且更准的生成，并缓解 quality-diversity trade-off。摘要没有给出使用的数据集、teacher/student 架构、采样步数、FID 或 precision-recall 数字，因此无法判断其绝对竞争力。最有价值的实验应包括：相同步数下对比 consistency / shortcut / flow distillation baseline 的质量和多样性；随着步数减少时性能衰减是否更平滑；以及 policy 维度、子步数、模仿轨迹匹配方式的 ablation。如果作者还能展示在不同 teacher 家族上都有效，那说明收益来自学生表示形式，而不是特定训练配方。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a structural mismatch in few-step generative distillation. Standard setups distill a velocity-predicting diffusion or flow teacher into a student that predicts a large shortcut toward the clean sample. That mismatch between teacher format and student format makes training complicated and often induces a quality-diversity trade-off. Existing methods patch around this with more elaborate distillation objectives, but the underlying output mismatch remains.","importance":"The contribution is a meaningful method-level shift: it reframes few-step generation as policy learning rather than as direct shortcut regression. For pretraining-oriented generative modeling, that is interesting because it suggests a student can retain trajectory-level flexibility without paying for repeated network evaluations. More broadly, the paper hints that efficient generation does not have to mean collapsing everything into a single endpoint prediction. A compact policy can carry forward enough dynamics to preserve sample quality with very few steps.","technical_approach":"pi-Flow changes the student output head so that, at one timestep, the network predicts a lightweight policy rather than a single static shortcut. That policy then generates dynamic flow velocities across future substeps with negligible extra cost, enabling fast ODE integration without additional full network calls. The second key idea is imitation distillation: instead of matching only a final denoised target or one-step output, the training aligns the policy-generated velocities with the teacher’s velocities along the trajectory. This reduces the teacher-student format mismatch because the student is supervised in the same dynamic space that it will later execute. The trade-off is that policy errors can accumulate over substeps, so stability depends on the policy parameterization being both expressive and low-variance.","experiments":"The abstract supports the claim that pi-Flow achieves faster and more accurate few-step generation while easing the usual quality-diversity trade-off, and does so with almost no extra overhead. However, the abstract provides no datasets, architectures, step counts, FID values, or precision-recall numbers, so the empirical scale is unclear. The most informative experiments should compare against consistency, shortcut, and standard flow-distillation baselines at matched step budgets, examine degradation as the number of steps shrinks, and include ablations over policy size, number of substeps, and trajectory-matching strategy. Cross-teacher robustness would also matter, because it would show that the gain comes from the student formulation rather than a narrow recipe.","authors_note":""},"tags":["diffusion","distillation","few-step","imitation-learning","sampling"],"notable_authors":["Leonidas Guibas","Gordon Wetzstein"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011811","title":"Look Back to Reason Forward: Revisitable Memory for Long-Context LLM Agents","authors":["Yaorui SHI","Yuxin Chen","Siyuan Wang","Sihang Li","Hengxing Cai","Qi GU","Xiang Wang","An Zhang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3bc","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011811"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011811","category_id":"agent","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：长上下文问答里，关键信息可能散落在数百万 token 中，现有 memory-augmented LLM 常采用一边读一边写 memory 的单向扫描策略，扩展性不错，但一旦早期信息被覆盖或压缩错了，后面无法回头修正。过去这类 memorize-while-reading 方法的瓶颈不只是记忆容量，而是推理路径被限制成 forward-only，导致信息不可逆丢失、信用分配稀疏。作者要解决的是，能否让 agent 在长文档处理里“回看”旧记忆并非线性推理，而不是只依赖一次扫描的即时摘要。","importance":"这项工作属于 long-context agent / memory 系统，但它对 foundation model 训练也有启发。关键贡献不是单纯多加一个 memory buffer，而是把 memory 从覆盖式缓存改成可 revisitable 的推理介质，并配合 RL 去训练何时回调旧记忆。对 pretrain-posttrain 接口而言，这很重要：它说明长上下文能力未必主要取决于 base model 原生上下文窗口，外部记忆的可访问结构和训练信号设计同样关键。如果方法稳健，长上下文 agent 可能更像“检索+控制”问题，而不是单靠更长 RoPE 和更大 attention。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 ReMemR1，一个带 callback-enhanced memory 的记忆增强 agent。它保留单次扫描建立 memory 的可扩展性，但不把历史 memory 覆盖掉，而是允许模型从整个 memory history 中选择性回调，从而形成非线性、多跳的证据整合路径。为了解决这种 memory decision 难训练的问题，作者进一步提出 Reinforcement Learning with Learning Signals from Revisited Reads，大意是把模型回看记忆后得到的收益转成更密集的训练信号，减少传统稀疏 RL 奖励下很难学会“早存、后取、再推理”的问题。与现有方法相比，区别不在有没有 memory，而在 memory 是否可回访，以及训练是否显式奖励正确的回访行为。代价是系统复杂度增加，memory history 的检索和管理也可能带来额外延迟。","experiments":"摘要明确声称：ReMemR1 通过可回访记忆和新的 RL 信号，改善了百万级 token 场景下的长上下文问答。摘要没有给出模型规模、上下文长度、数据集、EM/F1 或 retrieval 指标，因此无法判断提升幅度，也无法确认是否优于强 retrieval baseline。最重要的实验应包括：与 memorize-while-reading、RAG、长上下文原生 Transformer baseline 的比较；上下文长度增加时性能衰减曲线；以及 callback memory、完整历史访问、RL learning signals 各自的 ablation。如果作者还能展示模型确实会回到早期关键证据而不是随机检索，这会比最终准确率更能证明方法成立。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a failure mode in long-context QA: important evidence may be scattered across millions of tokens, yet many memory-augmented LLM systems still process documents in a single forward scan, updating memory as they read. That scales well, but it is irreversible. Once early evidence is compressed badly or overwritten, the model cannot revisit and correct its reasoning. The core problem is therefore not just memory size, but the restriction to forward-only reasoning over long documents.","importance":"This is a systems-and-agent contribution, but it has clear implications for foundation model training. The key insight is that long-context performance may depend as much on memory access structure and training signals as on native context-window length. By turning memory into a revisitable reasoning substrate rather than a write-once cache, the work shifts the problem from sheer context scaling toward learned retrieval and control. That is relevant to the pretrain–posttrain interface because it suggests some long-context failures should be fixed outside the base model’s attention mechanism.","technical_approach":"ReMemR1 is a memory-augmented agent with callback-enhanced memory. It keeps the scalability of single-pass memory construction, but preserves memory history so the model can selectively revisit earlier stored states and reason nonlinearly over evidence. To train those revisit decisions, the paper adds a reinforcement-learning scheme with learning signals derived from revisited reads, aiming to make the reward for useful memory callbacks denser and easier to optimize. Relative to prior memory-augmented methods, the difference is not merely having memory, but making memory revisitable and explicitly training the callback policy. The trade-off is higher system complexity and likely extra latency from history management and retrieval.","experiments":"The abstract clearly claims improved long-context QA in million-token settings through revisitable memory and stronger RL signals. But it gives no model scales, context lengths, datasets, or exact EM/F1 or retrieval gains, so the empirical size of the improvement is unknown. The most important experiments should compare against memorize-while-reading baselines, retrieval-based baselines, and native long-context transformers, evaluate degradation as context grows, and isolate the effects of callback memory, full-history access, and the new RL signal. Evidence that the agent truly revisits early critical evidence, rather than just retrieving nearby cues, would be especially important.","authors_note":""},"tags":["long-context","memory","agent","retrieval","reasoning"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011824","title":"ParoQuant: Pairwise Rotation Quantization for Efficient Reasoning LLM Inference","authors":["Yesheng Liang","Haisheng Chen","Song Han","Zhijian Liu"],"institutions":["MIT"],"abstract":"$3bd","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011824"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011824","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"Reasoning LLM（如 o1 类模型）在长 CoT 推理时，量化误差会沿推理链累积放大，导致 PTQ 后精度严重下降。现有方法要么不能充分抑制 outlier，要么引入推理时的额外开销。","importance":"系统/效率级贡献。Reasoning LLM 的推理成本极高（长 CoT 意味着大量 token 生成），低比特量化对部署至关重要。ParoQuant 在硬件友好的前提下解决 outlier 问题，对 reasoning LLM 的实际 serving 有直接价值。","technical_approach":"提出 Pairwise Rotation Quantization：用独立的 Givens 旋转（每次只旋转两个通道）配合 channel-wise scaling 来平滑通道间的数值范围。关键设计选择是 Givens 旋转——它既是硬件高效的（只涉及 2×2 矩阵乘），又是可优化的（旋转角度可学习）。相比 Hadamard 变换等全局旋转，Givens 旋转更灵活且推理开销更小；相比 SmoothQuant 只做 scaling，旋转能更彻底地消除 outlier。","experiments":"摘要未给出具体数字，但声称在 reasoning LLM 上显著优于现有 PTQ 方法，且推理开销可控。需看全文确认具体模型规模、bit-width 和精度保持情况。","authors_note":"作者包括 Song Han（MIT，高效推理/量化领域代表人物，代表作 SmoothQuant、AWQ 等）。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Post-training quantization for reasoning LLMs suffers from error accumulation across long chains of thought, where outliers in weights/activations cause severe accuracy loss. Existing methods either fail to suppress outliers or add significant inference overhead.","importance":"System/efficiency-level contribution. Reasoning LLMs generate extremely long sequences, making low-bit quantization critical for deployment. ParoQuant provides a hardware-friendly solution to the outlier problem specifically for this regime.","technical_approach":"Uses independent Givens rotations (pairwise 2×2 channel rotations) combined with channel-wise scaling to flatten the dynamic range before quantization. Givens rotations are both hardware-efficient (only 2×2 matmuls) and optimizable (learnable angles). Compared to global Hadamard transforms, they are more flexible with lower overhead; compared to scaling-only approaches like SmoothQuant, rotations more thoroughly eliminate outliers.","experiments":"Abstract claims significant improvements over existing PTQ methods on reasoning LLMs with manageable inference overhead. LLM Compression via Learning-based Sparse Tensor Decomposition","authors":["Yi Li","Zhichun Guo","Miao Yin","Bingzhe Li"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3c4","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011895"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011895","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文解决的是张量分解压缩 LLM 时一个很实际的瓶颈：虽然分解能找到共享低秩基，但中间的 dense core tensor 会随着秩多项式增长，最后反而变成新的存储大头。过去很多 post-training、data-free 压缩方法在理论上能分解，在工程上却卡在 core tensor 太密、压不下去，因此高压缩比很难继续提升。","importance":"这项工作的意义偏方法级和系统级。它不是改变预训练范式，而是提升已有大模型在部署侧的可压缩性，尤其适合无法重训、也拿不到训练数据的场景。对 pretrain 研究的间接启示是：如果某些模块在后训练分解后仍可保持性能，说明这些权重存在强结构冗余；这会反过来影响我们对 attention/FFN 参数化方式、低秩结构先验以及训练后冗余形成机制的理解。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 LeSTD，一个两阶段的 learning-based sparse tensor decomposition 框架，目标是把原先的 dense core tensor 进一步变稀疏，从而突破传统 Tucker/类似分解的 core bottleneck。第一阶段应是学习共享低秩因子或基底，把高维权重映射到较低维张量表示；第二阶段则不再接受 dense core，而是显式学习 sparse core 的非零结构与数值，使存储量跟有效自由度走，而不是跟所有 core entry 走。它和已有纯解析型、一次性分解方法的关键区别在于：不是只靠线性代数求一个低秩近似，而是把“哪些 core 元素值得保留”作为学习问题处理。这种设计有效的原因很直接——在高秩需求下，真正有贡献的交互项往往是稀疏的，如果继续全保留，压缩收益会被 dense core 吃掉。","experiments":"摘要表明该方法面向 Multi-Head Attention 模块的高比例压缩，并强调相对现有 tensor decomposition 方法能突破 compression ceiling。可惜摘要被截断，没有给出具体模型、参数规模、压缩倍率、perplexity 或零样本精度损失，也没有给出和 SVD、Tucker、LoRA-style reconstruction 等 baseline 的定量比较。能确认的实验主张只有两点：1）在 data-free post-training setting 下可用；2）高压缩比时优于受 dense core 限制的传统张量方法。没有数字意味着目前更适合把它看成一个值得跟进的结构压缩方向，而不是已经被充分验证的通用方案。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a concrete bottleneck in tensor-decomposition-based LLM compression: even when a shared low-rank basis is found, the resulting core tensor remains dense and grows polynomially with the chosen ranks, eventually becoming the new storage bottleneck. In practice, this caps achievable compression, especially in post-training, data-free settings where retraining is not an option.","importance":"This is mainly a method and systems contribution. It does not change pretraining itself, but it matters for deployment and for understanding redundancy in trained transformers. If high-ratio compression becomes possible without data, that is evidence that substantial structure in attention weights is left unused after training. That has downstream value for how we think about parameterization, low-rank priors, and where redundancy accumulates during pretraining.","technical_approach":"The core idea is LeSTD, a two-stage learning-based sparse tensor decomposition method designed to break the dense-core bottleneck. The first stage appears to learn shared low-rank factors for the weight tensor, while the second stage explicitly learns a sparse core rather than accepting the standard dense one. That is the key departure from classical analytical decompositions: it treats the support of the core tensor as a learnable object, not a byproduct. The design is sensible because, at high target ranks, the useful interactions are often sparse; keeping all core entries preserves approximation flexibility but destroys compression.","experiments":"From the abstract, the method is aimed at high-ratio compression of Multi-Head Attention and is claimed to outperform prior tensor methods when compression becomes aggressive. However, the abstract is truncated and provides no model names, parameter counts, compression ratios, perplexity changes, or downstream accuracy numbers. So the safe summary is limited: it is a data-free post-training method, and its claimed advantage is strongest in the regime where dense-core methods hit a storage ceiling. Without quantitative details, it should be treated as a promising compression direction rather than a fully established result.","authors_note":""},"tags":["compression","tensor-decomposition","sparsity","post-training"],"notable_authors":["Yi Li","Zhichun Guo","Miao Yin","Bingzhe Li"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10011899","title":"Strategic Scaling of Test-Time Compute: A Bandit Learning Approach","authors":["Bowen Zuo","Yinglun Zhu"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$3c5","venue":"ICLR","year":2026,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"","doi":"","virtual":"https://iclr.cc/virtual/2026/poster/10011899"},"_virtual_path":"/virtual/2026/poster/10011899","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的问题很明确：test-time compute 不该对所有 query 平均分配，因为难度差异很大，统一给预算既浪费算力，也拖低整体收益。过去多数 self-consistency、best-of-n、tree search 类方法默认固定采样数或固定思考步数，这在 easy query 上过度计算，在 hopeless query 上也容易继续烧预算。","importance":"这项工作的重要性在于把 test-time scaling 从启发式调参推进到有理论对象的资源分配问题。对 pretrain/posttrain 接口的启示是：模型能力不只取决于参数和训练数据，还取决于推理预算如何针对输入分布自适应地分配；如果这个分配能学出来，那么同一个 base model 的有效能力边界会被重新定义。它更像方法级贡献，但对 serving 和 reasoning 系统设计影响很直接。","technical_approach":"核心方法是把测试时算力分配形式化成 bandit learning 问题，并设计在线估计 query difficulty 的自适应算法。关键不只是区分难易，还要进一步区分“难但可解”和“难且基本无解”，因为后者继续加 compute 的边际回报很低。与现有固定 compute 策略相比，这类 bandit 方法的优势是能在探索与利用之间权衡：早期用少量试探信号判断是否值得继续扩展，后期把预算集中到最可能从额外推理中受益的样本上。摘要提到有理论保证，说明作者不仅给了 heuristic policy，还分析了 regret 或资源效率界。","experiments":"实验主张是：相同总预算下，自适应分配优于 uniform allocation；对难题会给更多 compute，对简单题会保精度同时省算力，对不可解题则减少无效开销。摘要没有给出具体模型、任务、预算设置、准确率提升、平均 token/rollout 节省比例，也没有说明 baseline 是 self-consistency 还是 search-based reasoners，因此无法量化收益。即便如此，这里最值得关注的实验观察应该是两层筛选是否都成立：先按难度分层，再在难题中按可解性分层。如果后者成立，它比单纯 difficulty prediction 更有实际价值。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a simple but important inefficiency: test-time compute should not be spent uniformly across queries because query difficulty varies widely. Most current approaches—fixed sampling counts, fixed search depth, fixed deliberation budgets—implicitly assume equal value of extra compute, which wastes budget on easy cases and often keeps spending on queries that are effectively unsalvageable.","importance":"This matters because it turns test-time scaling from a heuristic into a resource allocation problem with structure. For the pretrain/posttrain interface, the implication is that model capability is partly a function of how inference budget is allocated across the input distribution, not just of model size or training data. That makes this a method-level contribution with clear systems impact, especially for reasoning workloads and cost-constrained serving.","technical_approach":"The key idea is to formulate test-time compute allocation as a bandit learning problem and learn query difficulty online. The important nuance is that the algorithm aims to separate not only easy versus hard queries, but also solvable hard queries from likely unsolvable ones, since extra compute only pays off for the former. Compared with fixed-budget methods, a bandit approach can use small early signals to decide whether additional reasoning is worth it, then concentrate budget where marginal returns are highest. The abstract also claims theoretical guarantees, suggesting this is more than a heuristic scheduling policy.","experiments":"The empirical claim is that adaptive allocation beats uniform allocation under the same overall compute budget. Hard queries receive more compute, easy queries retain accuracy with less compute, and likely unsolvable queries are deprioritized to avoid waste. The abstract gives no model sizes, datasets, budget ranges, or numerical gains, so the result cannot be quantified here. The most important experimental question is whether both layers of discrimination hold in practice: difficulty estimation first, then solvability estimation within the difficult subset. If the latter is real, it is much more useful than plain difficulty prediction.","authors_note":""},"tags":["test-time-compute","bandit","adaptive-inference","reasoning"],"notable_authors":["Bowen Zuo","Yinglun Zhu"]},{"id":"iclr-2026-10012089","title":"Visualizing LLM Latent Space Geometry Through Dimensionality Reduction","authors":["Alex Ning","Vainateya Rangaraju","Yen-Ling Kuo"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"In this blog post, we extract, process, and visualize latent state geometries in Transformer-based language models through dimensionality reduction to build a better intuition of their internal dynamics. I argue that diffusion's destroying approach to withholding is more flexible than typical hand-crafted information withholding techniques, providing a rich training playground that could be advantageous in some settings, notably data-scarce ones. I then address subtle issues that may arise when porting reinforcement learning techniques to the diffusion context, and wonder how such exploration problems could be addressed in more diffusion-native ways. I do not have definitive answers, but I do point my fingers in directions I deem interesting. A tutorial follows this thesis, expanding on the destroy-then-generate perspective. A novel kind of probabilistic graphical models is introduced to facilitate the tutorial's exposition. This operator view unifies diffusion, flow matching, and consistency models, and suggests a practical diagnostic — semigroup-consistent jumps yield both step-robust generation and low compositional drift. We derive Eulerian/Lagrangian distillation objectives and use inpainting experiments to show why semigroup-consistent jumps can be both step-robust and composition-stable. Unpacking Spatial Awareness in Vision-Language Models","authors":["Jiyoon Pyo","Yao-Yi Chiang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Modern vision-language models (VLMs) have achieved impressive success in recognizing and describing visual content, yet they continue to struggle with understanding spatial relationships. The limitation persists despite massive data and model scaling, suggesting that the root of the problem lies in the architecture and training objective rather than data alone. This post examines the underlying causes and discusses why recent proposed fixes, while promising, remain insufficient to achieve robust spatial reasoning.
